# 2026-06-06 MiMo prompt-shape/speed lane

Boundary: active Python worktree only, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

I continued the MiMo V2.5 JANG_2L release-blocker lane and ran one focused
conservative prompt-shape sweep after the existing simple/MLLM conservative
cache-prompt probes both returned empty visible content.

Fresh artifact:

- `build/current-mimo-v2-jang2l-prompt-shape-sweep-20260606.json`

Findings:

- Simple conservative runtime without continuous batching, prefix cache, KV
  quantization, or native MTP can answer short exact prompts.
- The long cache-style exact prompt fails only when the instruction is in a
  separate `system` role; the no-system equivalent returns `ACK`.
- Normal short chat is coherent but slow, about `1.65 tok/s`.
- A 120-word speed probe timed out at `90s`.
- Max2 MiMo docs confirm the promoted JANG_2L Python bundle's canonical
  cached generation was coherent but around `1.97-2.64 tok/s`, so the current
  bundle is not a `40+ tok/s` candidate.

Tracker updates:

- `docs/internal/VL_AUDIO_VIDEO_RUNTIME_WORKLIST_2026_06_06.md`
- `docs/internal/CROSS_MODEL_RUNTIME_ISSUE_REGISTER_2026_06_05.md`

Current classification remains open:

- `decode_loop` or `model_artifact` for system-role long-prompt immediate EOS.
- `runtime_dispatch` for MiMo VL/audio/video because JANG tools still has only
  a text-oriented MiMo model and preserved media weights are not a wired
  multimodal forward path.
- No release/tag/notarization/public download claim from this lane.

Follow-up proof:

- `build/current-mimo-v2-jang2l-first-token-probe-registered-20260606.json`
  registers `jang_tools.mimo_v2.mlx_register` and directly probes first-token
  logits. The failing separate-system prompt ranks `<|im_end|>` first; working
  folded-user and short-system prompts rank `ACK` first.
- Deleted stale HF MiMo module cache:
  `/Users/eric/.cache/huggingface/modules/transformers_modules/MiMo_hyphen_V2_dot_5_hyphen_JANG_2L`.
- Active audit is now
  `build/current-mimo-v2-jang2l-current-audit-after-first-token-stop-20260606.json`.
  It reports manifest integrity true, stale local state absent, tool protocol
  true, and release still open for long-prompt coherence, exact-cache prompt
  following, decode speed, and system-prompt first-token stop.
- Objective digest refreshed:
  `build/current-objective-proof-after-mimo-first-token-stop-20260606.json`;
  full release remains open.

# 2026-06-06 DSV4 route-mode exactness continuation

Boundary: active Python worktree only. Used the explicit local user-RAM override
for a narrow DSV4 route/mode exact-code diagnostic; no release/package claim.

Fresh artifacts:

- `build/current-dsv4-route-mode-code-exactness-chat-off-user-ram-override-20260606.json`
- `build/current-dsv4-route-mode-code-exactness-chat-on-user-ram-override-20260606.json`
- `build/current-dsv4-route-mode-code-exactness-ab-route-user-ram-override-20260606.json`

Findings:

- Chat/off (`thinking_closed`, prompt suffix ends `</think>`) returns HTTP 200
  but corrupts exact identifiers: `THREE.WebWebGLRenderer()`.
- Chat/on (`thinking_open`) returns exact required code.
- Responses/off reproduces `THREE.WebWebGLRenderer()`.
- `chat_off_no_punct_rep1` still corrupts exact code as `THREE.ScScene()`.
- Responses/on returns exact required code.

Classification:

- Current blocker is DSV4 exact-code/file-generation quality on the
  thinking-closed rail, not a crash and not yet proven corrupt upload.
- Do not force thinking on as a fake fix. Next proof must determine whether the
  DSV4 off suffix is model-card correct or a runtime encoder contract bug.

## [2026-06-02 12:08 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Local release-gate/audit normalization after the 1.5.52 Tahoe install refresh.

Eric directed local MacBook only, no max2, no Swift. I stayed in
`/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.

Fixed a real release-gate failure: `release-gate-python-app.py` was allowed to
fall through to a global `twine` console script whose shebang pointed at
`/Applications/vMLX.app/Contents/Resources/bundled-python/...`. That made the
developer release gate import user-site Twine/nh3 through the installed app
runtime and hit the Team ID `write epipe`/load-style failure class. The gate now
prefers repo venv `python -m twine`, ignores app-bundled console-script
shebangs, and sets `PYTHONNOUSERSITE=1` for Twine probes. `twine>=6.2.0` is in
the dev extra and the lock is refreshed.

Fixed the public issue #169 audit assumption: `/Applications/vMLX.app` may be
the Tahoe-native app on macOS 26 while staged Sequoia and Tahoe DMGs are both
present. The audit now requires `installed_app_supported_runtime_flavor` plus
both staged flavors, instead of requiring the installed app to be Sequoia
compat. This keeps the Sequoia metallib regression guarded without falsely
failing a Tahoe-native local install.

Refreshed artifacts:

- `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260602-developer-id-staged-signing.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`.
- `build/current-public-app-issue-audit-20260602-after-performance-health-epipe-app-sync.json`
  -> `status=open`, `focused_failures=[]`, `focused_open=["117"]`.
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260602-vm-stat-gate-validation.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed_steps=[]`, open requirements only:
  MiniMax #179 reporter parity/root cause, real UI cross-family matrix, and
  DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality.

Focused verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m twine check dist/*
# passed

panel/scripts/release-gate-python-app.py --skip-app --skip-gui
# Twine/panel/typecheck/bundled import/objective refresh passed; expected
# objective/release-ready failures only.

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q ...focused release gate/public audit/manifest/package set...
# 58 passed

.venv/bin/python -m py_compile ... && git diff --check
# clean
```

Current proof sweep now returns `status=pass`, `failed_components=[]`,
`non_mimo_component_status=pass`. Remaining objective rows are explicit release
gaps, not package/audit regressions.

## [2026-06-02 12:35 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

LFM 1.5.52 installed-app real UI proof refreshed; Step live rerun blocked by
current local memory floor.

Boundary followed: local MacBook only. No Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`,
no max2.

Installed app proof:

`docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-lfm25-jang2l-installed-v1552-responses-tools-cachecontrols-local-refresh-20260602-proof.json`

Result: `status=pass`.

This ran `/Applications/vMLX.app` 1.5.52 installed-app UI against a real
LFM2.5 JANG_2L model/server. Proven surfaces:

- installed app UI
- real loaded model
- Responses API
- Responses delta streaming
- Responses cache-detail usage
- real built-in tool loop
- cache endpoint stats
- cache-hit telemetry
- L2 disk storage
- server cache controls
- tool+L2 integration
- model-owned generation defaults
- parser/language leak checks

Key runtime/cache evidence from the proof:

- native cache family `lfm2`, `cache_type=hybrid_ssm_typed`
- components: `attention_kv`, `ssm_companion_state`, `async_rederive`
- generic TurboQuant KV disabled because hybrid SSM state is present
- attention KV storage quantization enabled at 4-bit/group 64
- block disk L2 wrote 29 blocks / 1745 tokens
- SSM L2 stored 4241 tokens
- cache hits used `paged+ssm`, including 1107 cached tokens on the second tool
  turn
- generation defaults resolved from model/runtime metadata:
  `temperature=0.0`, `top_p=1.0`, `max_tokens=512`, `enable_thinking=False`
- live speed samples: 228.3 and 226.8 live tok/s, TTFT 0.10s and 0.12s

Step3.7 installed-app live rerun was not launched. Current local memory is below
the safe floor for that 81.61 GB model:

`build/current-real-ui-step37-memory-preflight-20260602-v1552-local-install.json`

- model size: 81.61 GB
- required floor with safety margin: 122 GB
- current vm_stat free+speculative+purgeable: 80.24 GB
- launch_allowed=false

Do not start Step/DSV4 heavy live UI rows on this MacBook until the preflight
floor is met, unless Eric explicitly accepts the crash/swap risk.

## [2026-06-02 06:15 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

ZAYA VL Responses/tools/media/cache live UI proof is now green in current
source. Local MacBook only; no max2, no Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Root cause:

- The panel injected generic `AGENTIC_SYSTEM_PROMPT` for a native ZAYA
  Responses/tool request.
- Direct server probes showed flat Responses tools were correct without that
  prompt, including the UI's 23 built-in flat tool schemas.
- Adding the app generic prompt made ZAYA emit malformed text-style tool markup
  instead of structured `function_call` arguments. That matched the red UI
  artifacts that persisted `{}` / missing command details.

Fix:

- `panel/src/main/ipc/chat.ts` now suppresses the generic agentic prompt for
  ZAYA native built-in tool requests.
- The guard covers detected families `zaya`, `zaya1-vl`, `zaya1_vl`, and
  loopback/remote sessions where only `chat.modelPath || chat.modelId` contains
  `zaya`.
- Regression added in `panel/tests/tool-auto-continue.test.ts`.

Passing live proof:

`docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-zaya-vl-responses-stricttools-cachecontrols-dev-post-release-20260602-after-zaya-modelname-agentic-suppress-proof.json`

- `assertionError=null`
- `firstAssistantContent=REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_ONE`
- second tool sentinel also written:
  `real_ui_tool_probe_2.txt=REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_TWO`
- `secondAssistantContent=The image is primarily red.`
- `media.imageSemanticVerified=true`
- app log shows both tool turns used `/v1/responses`, executed one
  `run_command` each, and the second tool turn had cached tokens.

Verification:

```sh
cd panel && npm test -- tests/tool-auto-continue.test.ts tests/tool-status-responsiveness.test.ts tests/responses-warnings.test.ts
# 34 passed

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_tool_format.py -k 'zaya'
# 30 passed

cd panel && npm run typecheck
# pass

.venv/bin/python -m py_compile vmlx_engine/api/tool_calling.py tests/test_tool_format.py
git diff --check
# clean
```

Boundary: this is current-source proof after the already published v1.5.50
emergency DMG. It needs a follow-up build/release if we want the public binary
to include the ZAYA app-path fix.

---

Fresh memory preflights:

- #179 MiniMax-K live probe:
  `build/current-issue179-minimax-k-responses-cancel-probe-memory-preflight-20260602-cache-detail-zero-cached-recheck.json`
  -> `status=skipped`, `launch_allowed=false`, available `81.36 GB`,
  required `81.96 GB`, gap `0.60 GB`, active heavy process count `0`.
- DSV4 source exactness:
  `build/current-dsv4-route-mode-code-exactness-memory-preflight-20260602-developer-id-local-recheck.json`
  -> `status=skipped`, `launch_allowed=false`, available `81.36 GB`,
  required `120.0 GB`, gap `38.64 GB`, active heavy process count `0`.
- DSV4 real UI:
  `build/current-real-ui-dsv4-memory-preflight-20260602-developer-id-local-recheck.json`
  -> `status=skipped_insufficient_memory`, `launch_allowed=false`,
  available `81.37 GB`, required `120.0 GB`, gap `38.63 GB`.

Change:

- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py` now rejects DSV4
  source exactness open-row preflight details unless they include:
  - `did_not_launch=true`
  - `launch_decision=do_not_launch`
  - `launch_allowed=false`
  - model path ending `DeepSeek-V4-Flash-JANGTQ-K`
  - `active_heavy_process_count=0`
  - empty `active_heavy_processes`
- Added regression
  `test_release_regression_manifest_rejects_dsv4_source_preflight_without_no_launch_context`.

Why:

- The previous validator checked artifact path, timestamp, skipped status,
  reason, memory floor, and case coverage, but could accept a source preflight
  detail that claimed launch was allowed or that a heavy process was active.
  That would be stale/unsafe release evidence.

## [2026-06-01 19:38 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Local app/source EPIPE stderr split guard is implemented, installed, and
verified. This is Python/panel only; no Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`,
no max2, and no live model launch.

Root cause for this slice:

- `panel/src/main/sessions.ts` already suppressed expected backend disconnect
  stderr when a complete chunk contained `write EPIPE`/broken-pipe text.
- It did not preserve partial stderr across chunks, so a split `write` then
  ` EPIPE` could leak raw backend pipe noise into session logs/UI before the
  second chunk identified the expected disconnect.

Change:

- Added `panel/src/main/backend-stderr.ts` with split-safe line buffering and
  normalized backend disconnect events.
- Wired session backend stderr through `normalizeBackendStderrChunk()` with a
  per-process `backendStderrPending` buffer.
- Added panel tests for split `write EPIPE` normalization.
- Added API-surface and installed-app parity gates so source/staged/installed
  app builds must carry the split-safe backend stderr guard.

Fresh proof:

- `/Applications/vMLX.app` rebuilt/installed by `panel/scripts/build-and-install.sh`;
  final app signature verifies on disk.
- `build/current-installed-app-runtime-parity-audit-20260601-cache-ipc-epipe-installed.json`
  status `pass`.
- `build/current-staged-app-runtime-parity-audit-20260601-epipe-python-launch-refresh.json`
  status `pass`.
- `build/current-public-app-issue-audit-20260601-installed-download-proof.json`
  status `open` rather than failed after app refresh.
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json` status
  `open`, `failed_steps=[]`, open requirements remain #179 MiniMax-K reporter
  parity/root cause, real Electron UI cross-family matrix, and DSV4 exact-code
  quality.
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`
  still `release_ready=false`, `prepackage_ready=false`.

Verification:

```sh
cd panel && npm test -- --run tests/backend-stderr.test.ts tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts
# 36 passed

cd panel && npm run typecheck
# pass

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_public_app_issue_audit.py \
  tests/test_installed_app_runtime_parity_audit.py \
```

## [2026-06-01 19:24 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Starting local app rebuild/install for split-stderr EPIPE guard.

Boundary: local MacBook only, Python/panel worktree only. No
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no Swift, no max2, and no model launch.

Root cause found in this slice:

- Backend stderr normalization only handled complete stderr chunks. If Python
  emitted `write EPIPE` split across data events, the first partial raw line
  could reach the UI/session log before the second chunk made the disconnect
  shape visible.
- Source now has a line-buffered `panel/src/main/backend-stderr.ts`
  normalizer, and `panel/src/main/sessions.ts` carries
  `backendStderrPending` per managed process before raw stderr logging.

Current app parity status before install:

- API surface source proof passes with
  `panel_backend_stderr_split_epipe_guard=true`.
- Current suite failed because `/Applications/vMLX.app` and staged
  `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app` are stale for the stricter installed-app
  parity scanner (`installed_panel_backend_stderr_line_disconnect_guard`).

I am running `panel/scripts/build-and-install.sh` now to rebuild, ad-hoc sign,
install `/Applications/vMLX.app`, and then rerun installed/staged parity.

## [2026-06-01 17:44 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

vMLX public issue #166 is now covered by source, bundled, and installed-app
guards.

Boundary followed: local MacBook only, Python/panel worktree only. No
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no Swift, no max2.

Finding:

- The reported failure shape was `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named
  'mlx_vlm.models.gemma4_assistant'`.
- Local `mlx_vlm` exposes the implementation at
  `mlx_vlm.speculative.drafters.gemma4_assistant`, and
  `mlx_vlm.utils.get_model_and_args({"model_type": "gemma4_assistant"})`
  already resolves through that speculative drafter path.
- The missing boundary was compatibility import support for callers/artifacts
  expecting `mlx_vlm.models.gemma4_assistant`.

Changes:

- `vmlx_engine.__init__._install_mlx_vlm_registry_patches()` now aliases
  `mlx_vlm.models.gemma4_assistant` to
  `mlx_vlm.speculative.drafters.gemma4_assistant` and adds
  `gemma4_assistant` to prompt-utils `MODEL_CONFIG` using the existing Gemma 4
  message format. This does not remap assistant artifacts to base Gemma 4.
- `vmlx_engine/__init__.py` is now in the bundled Python source hash gates for
  both `panel/scripts/verify-bundled-python.sh` and
  `panel/scripts/release-gate-python-app.py`.
- `panel/scripts/verify-bundled-python.sh` now imports
  `mlx_vlm.models.gemma4_assistant` through bundled Python and requires
  `Model` and `ModelConfig`.
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_public_app_issue_audit.py` now tracks public vMLX
  #166 and proves:
  - `source_gemma4_assistant_alias`
  - `bundled_verify_gemma4_assistant_alias`
  - `installed_app_gemma4_assistant_alias`
- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py` now rejects #166 if any
  of those checks are absent or false.

Fresh artifacts:

- `build/current-public-app-issue-audit-20260601-installed-download-proof.json`
  - #166 `focused_source_slice=pass`
  - release clearance:
    `mapped_to_gemma4_assistant_mlx_vlm_alias_guard`
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`
  - still `release_ready=false`, `prepackage_ready=false`

  dev-UI proof harness for built-in tools, reasoning segments, image/video tool
  calls, model-owned generation defaults, and cache settings UI.

Changed:

- Added `isSocketDisconnectError()` handling for `EPIPE`, `ECONNRESET`,
  `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`, `ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END`, nested causes, and
  AggregateError-style nested errors.
- Routed mock SSE writes/end through `safeHttpWrite` / `safeHttpEnd`.
- Hardened the CDP client so closed/destroyed sockets reject pending calls
  instead of throwing raw `write EPIPE` from frame writes.

Local MacBook only. No max2, no MiMo, no Swift, no `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Step3.7 source-owned contract tightened one more notch. The actual local
`Step-3.7-Flash-JANG_2L/config.json` has `projector_config=null`, while the HF
reference builds `vit_large_projector` as:

`vision_config.width * 4 -> text_config.hidden_size`

For this bundle that is `1536 * 4 -> 4096`, so the source-owned config layer
must not carry `None` projector dimensions.

- `vmlx_engine/models/step3p7_mlx_vlm.py`
  - preserves `vision_config.width`, `layers`, and `heads`;

  `<|tool_call_end|>` markers;

  content when no valid call parsed;
- server streaming marker/residue cleanup also missed those LFM markers.

- stripped malformed LFM markers from parser-visible content;

# 2026-05-27 21:02 PDT - Codex local bundle/proof sweep repair

Local-only bundle/proof sweep repair after EPIPE/ZAYA/think_xml drift.

Boundary: stayed in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`; no Swift,
no `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2 testing, no MiMo live work.

Findings/fixes:

- The bundled app runtime was stale after source fixes: `npm run verify-bundled`
  initially failed because bundled `vmlx_engine/server.py` did not match source.
  Rebuilt `panel/bundled-python` with `panel/scripts/bundle-python.sh`; bundled
  verifier and packaged integrity now pass.
- The MiMo metadata work introduced a no-heavy parser-registry drift:
  `mimo_v2` could emit `reasoning_parser="think_xml"`, but the engine/panel
  allowlists and dropdown did not include it. Fixed the allowlists/dropdown so
  panel-emitted `think_xml` is launchable. This does not clear MiMo generation.
- Refreshed every stale source-hash proof touched by that parser/UI change:
  max-output/context, panel settings, parser registry, model artifact,
  model family, generation defaults, native MTP, VL/media, packaged integrity.
- Explicit policy now encoded: expected MiMo 2L failed/missing live smoke remains
  an open release-ledger blocker, but it does not poison the current proof sweep
  for non-MiMo rows Eric asked us to continue.

Fresh artifacts:

- `build/current-regression-suite-20260527-after-think-xml-registry-fix.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `failed_steps=[]`
  - expected open requirements only:
    - `Cross-family live multi-turn smoke matrix is release-cleared`
    - `Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared`
    - `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260527-after-think-xml-registry-fix.json`
  - `current_proof_sweep=pass`
- `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260527-after-think-xml-registry-fix-rerun.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - bundled verifier rc=0
  - dry release gate fails only on known objective rows

Still not release-cleared:

- Release blocker ledger remains open for MiMo 2L live/tool smoke failure and
  sink incoherence.
- #179 MiniMax-K reporter parity/root-cause proof remains open.
- Real UI matrix ledger remains open; DSV4 full live proof is locally
  memory-preflight blocked.

## [2026-05-28 03:22 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

API gateway EPIPE socket guard refreshed, local-only.

Boundary:

- stayed in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`;
- no Swift;
- no `/Users/eric/vmlx`;
- no max2 testing;
- no MiMo work.

Root cause for the remaining EPIPE class: the guarded writer existed, but
`responseWritable()` / `requestWritable()` did not check `writableDestroyed` or
the underlying socket's destroyed state. A late write could still reach a dead
client/backend socket before `destroyed` or `writableEnded` alone stopped it.

Change:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts` now refuses response writes when the response
  stream is `writableDestroyed` or its socket is destroyed.
- `writeProxyBody()` now refuses backend request-body writes when the client
  request is `writableDestroyed` or its socket is destroyed.
- Added panel regressions:
  - `does not write gateway response chunks after the client socket is destroyed`;
  - `does not write proxied request bodies after the backend socket is destroyed`.
- Wired those markers into `tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py`.
- Updated current proof pointers to:
  `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260528-epipe-socket-guard.json` and
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260528-epipe-socket-guard.json`.

Evidence:

```sh
npx vitest run tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts -t \
  "does not write gateway response chunks after the client socket is destroyed" \
  --reporter=verbose
# red before fix: write() was called on a destroyed socket

npx vitest run \
  tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts \
  tests/api-gateway-ollama-behavior.test.ts \
  --reporter=verbose
# 18 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py \
  --out build/current-api-surface-contract-20260528-epipe-socket-guard.json
# status=pass, panel_api_request_builders 121 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
  --skip-release-gate \
  --out build/current-regression-suite-20260528-epipe-socket-guard.json
# status=pass, failed_steps=[]

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --require-current-proof-sweep \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-epipe-socket-guard.json
# current_proof_sweep=pass

npm run typecheck
# pass

.venv/bin/python -m py_compile \
  tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py \
  tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
  tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py \
  tests/test_api_surface_contract.py \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py \
  tests/test_current_regression_suite.py
git diff --check
# clean
```

Do not claim release cleared. Current suite still has the expected open rows:

# 2026-05-25 06:37 PDT - To Other Agent: API gateway single-model fail-closed proof

I audited the no-heavy app/API gateway single-model mode lane Eric called out:
ALG/model-id auto switching, only one local model loaded, standby wake, and
streaming delta preservation. No Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`, and
no live model load.

New route-level regression added:

`panel/tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts`

Case:

- `gateway_single_model_mode=true`;
- target is selected by served model id and is currently stopped;
- another local model is running;
- `stopSession(other)` fails;
- gateway returns `503` with `single_model_unload_failed`;
- gateway does not start/wake/touch the target session and does not proxy to
  the target backend.

This is now required by the API surface release contract via marker:

`refuses auto-switch when previous local model cannot unload before starting target`

## 2026-05-27 17:51 PDT - Codex MiMo 4M candidate is structurally valid but max2 Metal-OOMs

Python/panel/vMLX release gate only. No Swift and no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx` work.

The max2 MiMo 4M conversion finished and produced an index. Structural verify
passed:

- bundle:
  `/Users/eric/.mlxstudio/models/JANGQ-AI/MiMo-V2.5-JANG_4M-candidate-20260527`
- profile: `JANG_4M`
- tensors: `109184` across `180` shards
- safetensor payload: `180.33 GB`
- audio tokenizer present; chat template matches embedded tokenizer config

The other lane had already launched:

```sh
.venv/bin/python /Users/eric/jang/jang-tools/scripts/mimo_v2/sink_ab_probe.py \
  --model /Users/eric/.mlxstudio/models/JANGQ-AI/MiMo-V2.5-JANG_4M-candidate-20260527 \
  --max-tokens 64 --temp 0 \
  --out build/current-mimo-v2-jang4m-candidate-sink-ab-max2-20260527.json
```

I did not start a competing load. After it exited, I copied the artifact to:

- `build/remote-max2/current-mimo-v2-jang4m-candidate-sink-ab-max2-20260527.json`

Result:

- normal child: returncode `-6`;
- `JANG_MIMO_DISABLE_SINK=1` child: returncode `-6`;
- both stderr tails show
  `[METAL] Command buffer execution failed: Insufficient Memory`.

Capacity boundary:

- max2 `hw.memsize` is `137438953472` bytes (128 GiB);
- the candidate safetensor payload alone is `180.33 GB`.

So the 4M candidate is not a viable live proof target on max2 under the current
load/runtime policy. This is a real release blocker, but it is a Metal memory
fit/load-policy blocker, not an output-quality result.

I wired the artifact into the release ledger:

- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
- `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`
- refreshed
  `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260527-release-blocker-ledger.json`

- `mimo_4m_candidate_metal_oom`

- `think_in_template=False`

- Current `MiMo-V2.5-JANG_2L` is globally incoherent, not just bad on the

- Direct JANG runtime, `JANG_MIMO_DISABLE_SINK=1`, `enable_thinking=true`:
  - `What is 2+2? Answer in one sentence.` produced numeric/punctuation garbage.
  - The same algebra MMLU-style prompt that is coherent in the old
    `2l_mmlu_answers.txt` note also produced numeric/punctuation garbage.
- The old `docs/internal/remote-jang/2026-05-27-mimo-jang2l/2l_mmlu_answers.txt`
  has coherent outputs but no command/provenance header. Treat it as historical
  contradiction, not current proof.

Lightweight math boundaries checked without changing code:

- MLX fast SDPA with MiMo full-attention shapes
  `(64 q heads, 4 kv heads, qk_dim=192, v_dim=128)` matches a manual repeated-KV
  reference for T=1/5/64.
- Synthetic quantized `SwitchGLU` with `QuantizedSwitchLinear` matches manual
  dequantized expert math to numerical precision.

So the active suspicion is no longer generic MLX SDPA or the stock switch
kernel. Remaining likely boundaries are:

- the `JANG_2L` profile is too aggressive for MiMo routed experts;
- the current/repacked `JANG_2L` bundle drifted from the older coherent artifact;
- a MiMo-specific model math/detail still differs from the Torch reference.

Another lane is actively converting a discriminator bundle on max2:

```sh
/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx/.venv/bin/python -m jang_tools.mimo_v2.convert_jang \
  --src /Volumes/EricsLLMDrive/jangq-ai/sources/MiMo-V2.5 \
  --dst /Users/eric/.mlxstudio/models/JANGQ-AI/MiMo-V2.5-JANG_4M-candidate-20260527 \
  --profile 4 \
  --drop-mtp
```

At 17:30 PDT it was still running and the candidate directory was about 126 GB.
I killed only my local polling SSH process, not the conversion.

Next proof when the converter exits:

1. Run `jang_tools.mimo_v2.verify_bundle` on
   `/Users/eric/.mlxstudio/models/JANGQ-AI/MiMo-V2.5-JANG_4M-candidate-20260527`.
2. Run the same direct prompts as 2L, preferably one load with:
   - `2+2`;
   - the algebra MMLU prompt;
   - the prefix-cache bullet prompt.
3. If 4M is coherent, preserve `mimo_sink_ab_incoherent` as a 2L profile/bundle
   blocker and add a release-gated 4M candidate proof row.
4. If 4M is incoherent, continue model-math tracing against the Torch reference;
   do not blame cache/parser/UI/sampler.

  affirmative media-carryover claims.

## [2026-05-25 05:49 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Found a live-smoke proof-quality issue before launching another ZAYA probe.

- Active process `1764` is already serving
  `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/ZAYA1-VL-8B-JANGTQ4` on port `8875` from parent
  Codex `3141`.
- I am not launching a second ZAYA copy.
- `lsof -a -p 1764 -d cwd` shows the server cwd is
  `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
- Because the command is `panel/bundled-python/python/bin/python3.12 -m
  vmlx_engine.cli ...`, cwd at the repo root means the supposedly bundled server
  can import checkout source via `sys.path[0]`. That makes any such "bundled"
  live-smoke artifact source-tainted unless the subprocess cwd is outside the
  repo.

I patched `bench/all_local_model_smoke.py` so isolated smoke runs use an
absolute block-cache path and run the server subprocess from the row artifact
directory instead of repo root. Focused harness tests pass:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_all_local_model_smoke.py::test_build_serve_command_uses_production_auto_cache \
  tests/test_all_local_model_smoke.py::test_build_serve_command_uses_dsv4_native_composite_cache_flags \
  tests/test_all_local_model_smoke.py::test_server_process_cwd_avoids_repo_root_for_isolated_bundled_smoke \
  tests/test_all_local_model_smoke.py::test_server_process_cwd_uses_repo_root_for_source_smoke
# 4 passed
```

I also verified directly that from `/tmp`, bundled Python imports
`panel/bundled-python/python/lib/python3.12/site-packages/vmlx_engine/__init__.py`.

Do not promote new "bundled" smoke artifacts unless their recorded
`server_cwd` is outside the repo, or otherwise independently proves bundled
site-packages were imported.

# 2026-05-22 05:03 PDT - To Other Agent: DSV4 Quality Blocker Is Now Tied To Full Rebuild Lane, Not Head-Only Overlay

I re-read the DSV4 quality artifacts after closing the parser guard:

- `build/current-dsv4-long-output-quality-clearance-20260521.json`
- `build/current-dsv4-identifier-count-ablation-20260521/result.json`
- `build/current-production-family-audit-static-dsv4-20260522.json`

Current evidence still says:

- The rendered prompt contains the required Three.js identifiers.
- The failing ablation used:
  - `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0`
  - `VMLINUX_DSV4_ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHE=0`
- Cache/tool/UI/Responses/parser paths are not the proven cause of this row.
- Local DSV4 still corrupts exact identifiers at `temperature=0`, e.g.
  `THREE.WebWebGLRenderer`, `THREE.PPerspectiveCamera`,
  `THREE.MMeshBasicMaterial`, `THREE.BBoxGeometry`, `THREE.ScScene`.
- Static audit still flags quantized output head plus lower-precision final
  norm on the current local affine artifact.

Important nuance:

- `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/DeepSeek-V4-Flash-JANGTQ-K-HeadBF16-Probe-20260520`
  is only a symlink overlay replacing head/norm.
- That is not equivalent to a full `DSV4_HIGH_PRECISION=1` rebuild, because
  the converter high-precision lane preserves all every-token non-routed
  modules: attention, shared experts, compressor/indexer, embed, and head.

I pinned that in `/Users/eric/jang`:

- pushed `496e223 test: pin dsv4 high precision rebuild lane`
- changed:
  - `jang-tools/tests/test_dsv4_converter_contract.py`
  - `docs/runtime/2026-05-22-dsv4-vmlx-live-quality-blocker.md`
- verification:
  - `PYTHONPATH=/Users/eric/jang/jang-tools uv run --extra dev python -m pytest -q /Users/eric/jang/jang-tools/tests/test_dsv4_converter_contract.py`
  - `27 passed`, 2 warnings

This is not a fake sampler/penalty/output-cap fix. It preserves a real
model-artifact rebuild lane and documents that the new artifact still must pass
the vMLX identifier and long-output live gates before release claims.

# 2026-05-22 04:43 PDT - To Other Agent: Reasoning Parser Dropdown Coverage Is Now Release-Pinned

I closed another parser-registry regression edge, specifically the UI side of
reasoning parser autodetect.

Problem:

- We already guarded that panel-emitted parser ids are accepted by engine/CLI.
- We already guarded that the tool parser dropdown covers panel-emitted tool
  parsers.
- We did not have the equivalent exhaustive UI guard for reasoning parsers.
  That leaves room for another MiniMax-style mismatch where autodetect emits a
  parser, backend accepts it, but the settings UI cannot display/preserve it.

Red/status-red:

- `build/current-parser-registry-contract-20260522-reasoning-dropdown-red.json`
  showed missing marker
  `reasoning parser dropdown covers every parser the panel registry can emit`.

# 2026-05-22 02:27 PDT - To Other Agent: Server/Chat Max Output Boundary Guard Added

I added a combined boundary guard for Eric's specific worry that separate chat max-output handling could interfere with Server Settings max output.

New marker:

- `server startup maxTokens and chat maxTokens remain independent when both are set`

What it proves:

- `sessions.ts` launch path uses session/server `config.maxTokens` for `--max-tokens`.
- Launch path does not read chat overrides.
- `ipc/chat.ts` request path uses per-chat `overrides?.maxTokens` via `dsv4OutputBudget`.
- Request path emits `max_tokens` / `max_output_tokens`.
- Request path does not read `config.maxTokens` or `session.config.maxTokens`.

# 2026-05-22 04:59 PDT - To Other Agent: Plain KV Cache Health Bleed-Through Guard Is Now Pinned

I added the next isolated release-hardening row for the cache family wiring
Eric keeps pressing on.

New required row:

- `decode_speed_plain_kv_cache_health_not_native`

What it protects:

- normal plain KV rows, including JANG/JANGTQ/MXFP decode-speed rows, must not
  accept a live health payload that reports DSV4 `native_composite` cache;
- they also must not accept ZAYA `typed_cca` health;
- accepted plain KV health is only `kv` or `paged_kv`.

Important nuance:

- this does not force TurboQuant KV off for plain KV rows;
- it only rejects the wrong cache architecture, so it is not a fake policy
  override or hidden runtime behavior change.

Red proof:

- focused test first failed because plain KV plus health `native_composite`
  returned `[]` from `cache_health_mismatches()`.

`options.num_predict` must be the generated-output cap, and `options.num_ctx`
must stay the prompt/context cap.

- failed because the marker was required before the endpoint test/command was
  present.

Green proof:

    `34 passed / 1 skipped`

  - `status=pass`, `failed_steps=[]`

What it proves:

- streaming `/api/chat` explicit `options.num_predict=44` overrides server
  default `_default_max_tokens=512`;
- raw streaming `/api/generate` explicit `options.num_predict=55` overrides the
  same server default;
- omitted streaming Ollama `num_predict` uses the server default;
- `options.num_ctx` remains independent prompt/context cap at 4096/8192/2048;
- Ollama NDJSON reaches `done: true`.

# 2026-05-22 04:07 PDT - To Other Agent: Ollama num_predict Poisoning Edge Closed

I closed another max-output/API edge. This one is panel API-gateway side, not
server-side:

- Ollama `options.num_predict` maps to OpenAI `max_tokens`.
- Existing coverage proved unset/disabled sentinels and valid positive values.
- Missing edge: malformed or non-finite values must not be forwarded as
  poisoned `max_tokens`.

- `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260522-ollama-malformed-red.json`
- failed only with:
  `missing_markers=["omits malformed Ollama num_predict values instead of poisoning max_tokens"]`

Fix:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts`
- `applyOllamaNumPredict(...)` now forwards only finite positive values and
  normalizes with `Math.floor(parsed)`.
- `"not-a-number"` / `"Infinity"` are omitted instead of becoming
  `max_tokens`.

# 2026-05-22 07:45 PDT - To Other Agent: direct release gate now blocks on objective digest

While running final prebuild checks I found a release-safety gap: the umbrella
suite caught the open DSV4 quality row, but a direct
`panel/scripts/release-gate-python-app.py --skip-app --skip-gui` could return
success because the release gate did not enforce the objective digest itself.

I added `check_objective_proof_digest()` and wired it into `check_static()`.
Now direct release-gate runs refresh
`build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260521.json` and fail if any
requirement is not `pass`.

Fresh proof:

## 2026-05-22 08:59 PDT - Max-output gate now requires Responses-specific server/chat boundary

I tightened the max-output/context proof around the exact server max output vs per-chat output concern.

- `does not invent Responses sampler or output-budget values when chat overrides are absent`

- Responses uses `max_output_tokens`.
- Server Settings Max Output uses startup `--max-tokens` as the omitted-request default.

## [2026-05-22 11:55 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 slowdown cross-family risk audit

I checked whether the DSV4 speed/spacing issue should be assumed to apply to
other model families.

Finding:

- The exact observed slowdown is still DSV4-specific until proven otherwise.
- DSV4 is using `DSV4BatchGenerator`; normal text/JANG/JANGTQ/MXFP rows use the
  patched scheduler/`GenerationBatch` or `SingleBatchGenerator`; MLLM/VL/native
  MTP rows use `mllm_batch_generator`.
- The installed DSV4 probe had only 56 prompt tokens but 384 completion tokens
  in 166.9s, so prompt snapshot copying is not enough to explain the decode
  slope. The DSV4-specific full-vocab logprob/sync work is the stronger culprit.

Dirty source slice under test:

- `vmlx_engine/utils/dsv4_batch_generator.py`
  - skips full-vocab logprob capture unless requested;
  - removes redundant sampled-token syncs;
  - makes prompt snapshot capture optional.
- `vmlx_engine/scheduler.py`
  - only requests DSV4 prompt snapshots when a block-aware cache store exists.

Cross-family proof run:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_dsv4_batch_generator_speed.py \
  tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_generator_skips_prompt_snapshot_when_cache_store_disabled \
  tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_generator_captures_prompt_snapshot_when_cache_store_enabled \
  tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_cache_hit_store_skips_sync_full_reprefill_when_snapshot_missing
# 7 passed

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_batching.py::TestSchedulerLogprobs \
  tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestGenerationBatchFastNoLogprobs \
  tests/test_mllm_continuous_batching.py::TestMLLMBatchFastNoLogprobs
# 11 passed

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_single_active_batch_generator.py tests/test_perf_sampler_async.py
# 19 passed

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_ssm_companion_cache.py -k 'cache or ssm or rederive'
# 51 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_max_output_context_contract.py \
  --out build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260522-cross-family-audit.json
# status=pass failed=[] missing_markers=[]
```

Cross-family read:

- Normal text/JANG/JANGTQ/MXFP: not on the DSV4 generator; existing sampler
  fast-path guards passed.
- SingleBatch: request-scoped sampler and async stream tests passed.
- MLLM/VL/native-MTP: no-logprobs sampler fast path passed; prefill/materialize
  costs remain a separate TTFT class, not the DSV4 per-token decode slope.
- Hybrid SSM: companion cache/rederive tests passed; this is a separate cache
  correctness class.
- Spacing: still needs raw DSV4 SSE/API capture. UI already uses preserved
  whitespace rendering, so do not patch rendering until raw deltas prove the
  boundary.

Not cleared:

- DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality;
- DSV4 live speed A/B no-cache/pool-quant-off vs native-cache/pool-quant-on;
- raw stream capture for the spacing corruption.

## [2026-05-22 12:04 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 cache/pool A/B and production guard

I ran the live A/B that was still missing. Result: DSV4 native prefix/cache is
functional, but the experimental pool-quant codec is a real decode-speed
poison and should not be emitted by the app.

Artifacts:

- `build/current-dsv4-decode-speed-source-after-hotpath-green-20260522.json`
  - source, no DSV4 native prefix opt-in, `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0`
  - decode rows around `21.6-22.7 tok/s`
- `build/current-dsv4-cache-on-chat-speed-pooloff-after-hotpath-20260522.json`
  - source, `--dsv4-enable-prefix-cache`, paged, block L2, `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0`
  - 36 prompt / 384 completion tokens in `37.18s` -> `10.33 tok/s`
  - health reports `native_cache.cache_type=native_composite`, `prefix=true`,
    `paged=true`, `block_disk_l2=true`, `pool_quant.enabled=false`
- `build/current-dsv4-cache-on-chat-speed-after-hotpath-20260522.json`
  - same as above but `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1`
  - 36 prompt / 384 completion tokens in `129.11s` -> `2.97 tok/s`
  - health reports `pool_quant.enabled=true`
- `build/current-dsv4-default-cache-tool-loop-after-hotpath-20260522/result.json`
  - source, default-cache tool loop with pool quant ON
  - status pass functionally: `list_directory -> write_file -> DONE`, file
    written, native prefix/paged/L2/cache detail seen
  - still slow: round 0 39 output tokens in 33.2s, round 1 81 in 23.1s

Root cause evidence:

- In `jang_tools/dsv4/pool_quant_cache.py`, `_StateProxy.__getitem__("pooled")`
  dequantizes the pooled tensor on each read.
- `DeepseekV4Cache.update_pool()` then concatenates the dequantized old pool
  with the new row and assigns `state["pooled"] = pool`, which requantizes the
  entire accumulated pool every decode step.
- That is per-token, per-compressed-layer O(pool length) quant/dequant work and
  repeatedly requantizes old pool rows. It explains the 10.33 -> 2.97 tok/s
  drop and is plausibly related to syntax/spacing drift when that codec is on.

Production guard added in this worktree:

- `panel/src/shared/dsv4Env.ts`
  - always emits `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0` for DSV4; `dsv4PoolQuant` is now
    migration-only.
- `panel/src/main/sessions.ts`
  - `applyFamilyStartupDefaults()` clears stale `dsv4PoolQuant` to false for
    DeepSeek-V4 sessions before launch/persist.
- `panel/src/renderer/src/components/sessions/SessionConfigForm.tsx`
  - DSV4 Pool Quantization checkbox is disabled with the reason.

Verification:

```sh
cd panel && npx vitest run tests/dsv4-env.test.ts tests/settings-flow.test.ts \
  --testNamePattern "pool quant|DSV4 runtime controls|deepseek-v4 family defaults clear stale pool quant|DSV4 pool quant" \
  --reporter=verbose
# 8 passed / 249 skipped

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py \
  -k 'pool_quant or generic_kv_quantization_off or native_pool_codec'
# 6 passed / 42 deselected

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_noheavy_panel_settings_contract.py \
  --out build/current-panel-settings-contract-20260522-dsv4-pool-quant-disabled.json
# status=pass; panel settings 285 passed; panel registry 54 passed; engine registry 130 passed
```

Important:

- This does not fake sampling, repetition penalty, max tokens, or model output.
- It preserves DSV4 native unquantized SWA+CSA/HCA composite prefix/cache.
- It disables only the proven-bad experimental pool-quant codec until JANG fixes
  it with an append-only/quality-preserving design and live speed proof.
- Spacing corruption is still not cleared. Need raw SSE/API vs UI capture.

---
## [2026-05-22 12:16 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 pool-quant source fix tested, still not production-default

I traced and patched the real JANG source, not the generated venv copy:

- source: `/Users/eric/jang/jang-tools/jang_tools/dsv4/pool_quant_cache.py`
- test: `/Users/eric/jang/jang-tools/tests/test_dsv4_pool_quant_cache.py`
- note: `/Users/eric/jang/docs/runtime/2026-05-22-dsv4-pool-quant-cache-codec.md`

Root cause confirmed by red test:

- `PoolQuantizedV4Cache` inherited `DeepseekV4Cache.update_pool()`;
- each append read `state["pooled"]`, dequantizing the whole old pool;
- then concatenated the new row;
- then assigned `state["pooled"]`, quantizing the whole enlarged pool again;
- red proof showed second append quantized shape `(1, 4, 16)` instead of only
  new shape `(1, 1, 16)`.

JANG fix:

- `_StateProxy` now stores pooled rows as quantized segments;
- `PoolQuantizedV4Cache.update_pool()` appends only new pooled rows through
  `append_pooled()`;
- committed and pushed to JANG main:
  `49e558e fix: append dsv4 pool quant rows`;
- focused JANG tests passed:
  `PYTHONPATH=/Users/eric/jang/jang-tools .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q ...`
  -> `5 passed`.

Live vMLX after installing local JANG source into this worktree venv:

- pool quant on:
  `build/current-dsv4-pool-quant-append-stream-spacing-20260522.json`
  - 192 streamed content deltas in 80.21s -> ~2.39 deltas/s
  - raw server HTML spacing looked normal in this probe
- pool quant off:
  `build/current-dsv4-pooloff-stream-spacing-20260522.json`
  - 192 streamed content deltas in 29.87s -> ~6.43 deltas/s
  - raw server HTML spacing looked normal in this probe

Conclusion:

- append-only pool quant fixes repeated requantization / quality-drift risk;
- it does NOT make pool quant fast enough for vMLX production;
- remaining cost is likely full-pool dequantize/concat on every attention read;
- keep app default `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0`;
- do not re-enable the DSV4 Pool Quantization UI/default until a second JANG
  design avoids full-pool read cost and live tool/code/spacing/speed gates pass.

No hidden sampler/cap/parameter behavior was added.

## [2026-05-22 12:51 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 traced cache replay is fast pool-off; cold snapshot/first sample and DSML syntax remain

I added env-gated DSV4 timing probes so we can stop guessing which boundary is
slow:

- `VMLINUX_DSV4_TRACE_TIMINGS=1`
- no sampler, max-token, generation-default, or cache behavior changes;
- generator events: `prefill_head`, `prompt_snapshot`, `prefill_last`,
  `cache_hit_tail_prefill`, `decode_model`, `sample_materialize`;
- scheduler events: `reconstruct_cache`, `extract_cache_states`, `store_cache`.

No-heavy gate:

```sh

  tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_timing_probe_is_env_gated_and_covers_cache_boundaries
# 1 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_cache_architecture_contract.py \
  --out build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260522-dsv4-timing.json
# status=pass; missing_markers=[]; cache_family_pytest 108 passed; panel_cache_launch_policy 76 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260522-dsv4-timing.json
# rows=18
```

Live traced pool-off default-cache tool loop:

- result:
  `build/current-dsv4-default-cache-tool-loop-trace-pooloff-20260522/result.json`
- log:
  `build/current-dsv4-default-cache-tool-loop-trace-pooloff-20260522/logs/dsv4-default-cache-tool-loop-1779479242.log`
- command used the existing live harness with
  `VMLINUX_DSV4_TRACE_TIMINGS=1`; default DSV4 pool quant stayed off.

Result is `review`, not pass:

- `tool_sequence_ordered=True`
- `final_done=True`
- `file_written=False`
- native prefix/paged/L2 active
- generic TQ KV off
- cache hit seen: `cached_tokens=231`, `cache_detail=paged+dsv4`
- round 1 true cache hit: 83 output tokens in `3.906s` (~21.3 tok/s)

Timing evidence:

- cache hit reconstruct: `2.075ms`
- cache hit tail prefill: `14.416ms`
- steady decode model median: about `4.27ms`
- steady sample/materialize median: about `36.49ms`
- cold round 0 prompt snapshot: `13636.926ms`
- cold round 0 first sample/materialize: `6783.427ms`
- store cache: `62.924-71.881ms`

Current read:

- true prefix-cache replay is not the 2-3 tok/s path in this pool-off traced
  run;
- the first cold tool round is dominated by prompt snapshot copy plus first
  sample/materialization;
- pool quant is still release-off: JANG append-only fixed repeated
  requantization, but previous live pool-on raw stream was still ~2.39 deltas/s
  because attention reads still dequantize/concat full pool state;
- malformed DSML syntax is still open and happened with pool quant off:
  raw output had `<｜DSML｜tool_ciles>`, `<｜DSML｜tool_ctools>`,
  and `<｜DSML｜inv>` forms. Do not attribute that one solely to pool quant or UI
  spacing.

Next isolated work should be either:

1. cold snapshot/first-sample speed path, without changing generation
   semantics; or
2. DSML tool syntax/file-write quality path, using raw Responses output before
   touching UI rendering.

No DSV4/model/test/release process was left running after the trace.

Follow-up verification after bundle refresh:

- refreshed bundled Python from this checkout using clean JANG source
  `/Users/eric/jang/.worktrees/vmlx-release-clean-49e558e/jang-tools`;
- `npm run verify-bundled` with clean JANG env -> pass;

## [2026-05-22 13:22 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 pool/env gate added; pool-on slowdown isolated to full-pool read path

I took Eric's prefix/cache/pool concern one more step without changing runtime
generation behavior.

Current evidence split:

- pool-off DSV4 native prefix/paged/L2 cache-hit replay is fast in the traced
  tool-loop artifact:
  `build/current-dsv4-default-cache-tool-loop-trace-pooloff-20260522/result.json`
  showed `cache_detail=paged+dsv4`, true cached tokens, and round 1 at 83
  output tokens / 3.906s;
- pool-on remains slow after the append-only JANG fix because
  `PoolQuantizedV4Cache.update_pool()` still returns `state["pooled"]`, whose
  getter dequantizes and concatenates every historical CSA/HCA pool segment on
  attention reads during decode;
- raw stream probes did not prove whitespace corruption:
  `build/current-dsv4-pooloff-stream-spacing-20260522.json` and
  `build/current-dsv4-pool-quant-append-stream-spacing-20260522.json` both had
  `has_spacing_suspect=false`;
- DSML malformed tool syntax/file-write remains open and is not pool-only: the
  pool-off default-cache tool loop still produced `tool_ciles`, `tool_ctools`,
  and `inv` variants.

Changes:

- cache architecture gate now hashes:
  - `panel/src/shared/dsv4Env.ts`;
  - `panel/tests/dsv4-env.test.ts`.
- gate now requires the panel marker:
  `does not enable the rejected DSV4 pool quant codec even if old config asks for it`.
- updated the DSV4 env helper comment: append-only writes are fixed; the current
  production reason for keeping `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0` is full historical pool
  dequant/concat on attention read.
- release manifest cache row now points at:
  `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260522-dsv4-pool-env-gate.json`.

Red/green:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_cache_architecture_contract.py
# red first, then 2 passed after gate patch

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_cache_architecture_contract.py \
  --out build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260522-dsv4-pool-env-gate.json
# status=pass; missing_markers=[]; cache_family_pytest 108 passed; panel_cache_launch_policy 88 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260522-dsv4-pool-env-gate.json
# rows=18

VMLX_JANG_TOOLS_SOURCE=/Users/eric/jang/.worktrees/vmlx-release-clean-49e558e/jang-tools \
  .venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \

```

Do not re-enable `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1` in app defaults. If you work the actual
pool-on speed fix, the next root-cause boundary is a selected-row/deferred
dequant path for compressor/indexer pools, not generic TurboQuant KV and not
max-output caps.

Follow-up: I also fixed the disabled UI tooltip for `DSV4 Pool Quantization`.
It now names the current bottleneck (full CSA/HCA pool dequantize/concatenate
during decode) instead of the stale repeated-requantization wording. Focused
panel marker passed, and cache gate
`build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260522-dsv4-pool-ui-copy.json`
passed.

## [2026-05-22 13:53 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 pool-on speed and live write_file tool loop fixed/proven

I fixed/proved two distinct DSV4 issues. Please do not collapse these back into
max tokens, reasoning, or generic TurboQuant.

Root cause split:

- Pool-on speed: `jang_tools.dsv4.pool_quant_cache._StateProxy.__getitem__("pooled")`
  dequantized and concatenated every historical CSA/HCA pool segment on every
  attention read. That explains the pool-on 2-3 tok/s class and why pool-off
  did not show it.
- Tool write failure: raw DSV4 output had correct path/content in this degraded
  form:
  `<｜DSML｜inv><｜DSML｜name>write_file</｜DSML｜>...<｜DSML｜parameter ...>...`
  but the canonical encoder parser produced bogus values like `" string="`,
  so the server accepted bad required arguments before regex repair could use
  the raw DSML parameter bodies.

JANG commit:

```text
/Users/eric/jang/jang-tools
b5f66a7 fix: cache materialized dsv4 pool reads
```

It was pushed to `origin/main`. It appends only new quantized pool rows, reuses
a materialized pooled view between appends, and reports that materialized live
pool in `nbytes`.

vMLX changes:

- `DSMLToolParser` now repairs the degraded named-invoke form and short DSML
  parameter close tags schema-safely.
- Cache architecture gate now prepends `VMLX_JANG_TOOLS_SOURCE` /
  `VMLINUX_JANG_TOOLS_SOURCE` to child `PYTHONPATH`; otherwise it can silently
  test the installed stale `jang_tools`.
- Release manifest now tracks:
  - `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260522-dsv4-pool-materialized-cache.json`;
  - `build/current-tool-call-contract-20260522-dsv4-live-write-file-repair.json`;
  - `build/current-dsv4-default-cache-tool-loop-poolon-materialized-parserfix-20260522/result.json`.

Verification:

```sh
PYTHONPATH=/Users/eric/jang/jang-tools \
  .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q /Users/eric/jang/jang-tools/tests/test_dsv4_pool_quant_cache.py
# 3 passed

VMLINUX_JANG_TOOLS_SOURCE=/Users/eric/jang/.worktrees/vmlx-release-clean-b5f66a7/jang-tools \
VMLX_JANG_TOOLS_SOURCE=/Users/eric/jang/.worktrees/vmlx-release-clean-b5f66a7/jang-tools \
  .venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_cache_architecture_contract.py \
  --out build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260522-dsv4-pool-materialized-cache.json
# status=pass; cache_family_pytest 109 passed; panel 88 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_tool_call_contract.py \
  --out build/current-tool-call-contract-20260522-dsv4-live-write-file-repair.json
# status=pass; engine DSML/tool 22 passed; panel tool loop/security 11 passed
```

## [2026-05-22 14:02 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 pool quant app/UI toggle wired after materialized-pool fix

Follow-up to the pool-on fix: the app wiring was still stale after the JANG
materialized-pool change. The panel still forced `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0` and the UI
showed DSV4 Pool Quantization as permanently disabled. I fixed and proved that
boundary.

Policy now:

- default remains off: missing/stale config initializes `dsv4PoolQuant=false`;
- explicit user opt-in reaches the runtime as `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1`;
- generic TurboQuant KV remains suppressed for DSV4 native composite cache;
- no hidden sampler, repetition, max-token, cache, or behavior forcing was

## [2026-05-22 14:12 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - max output/context edge: Casual preset server cap clarified and gated

Eric specifically worried that a separate chat output cap might conflict with
the server Max Output Tokens setting. I re-read the actual server/API/panel
coverage and tightened one weak UI/preset edge.

Current boundary remains:

- `Server Settings -> Max Output Tokens` maps to startup `--max-tokens`;
- Chat Settings `maxTokens` maps only to per-request `max_tokens` or
  `max_output_tokens`;
- Max Context Tokens maps only to `--max-prompt-tokens`;
- Auto chat/API requests omit output caps so the server/model default applies;
- explicit API/chat output caps can be below or above server startup default
  and do not mutate that default.

New edge fixed/gated:

- `CASUAL_CONFIG.maxTokens=8192` is an explicit server startup output cap, not
  a chat override and not a context/KV allocation control.
- I changed the stale source comment from “prevents huge KV allocation” to
  “Explicit server output cap; limits runaway long replies without changing
  context.”
- Added panel test:
  `casual preset maxTokens uses an explicit server output cap without changing model-owned defaults or context`.
- Added that marker to `run_max_output_context_contract.py`.
- Updated release manifest to point at:
  `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260522-casual-server-output-cap.json`.

Verification:

```sh
cd panel && npx vitest run tests/settings-flow.test.ts --reporter=verbose \
  -t 'casual preset maxTokens uses an explicit server output cap'
# 1 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_max_output_context_contract.py \
  --out build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260522-casual-server-output-cap.json
# status=pass; engine 22 passed; panel 45 passed; missing_markers=[]

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260522-casual-server-output-cap.json
# rows=18

VMLINUX_JANG_TOOLS_SOURCE=/Users/eric/jang/.worktrees/vmlx-release-clean-b5f66a7/jang-tools \
VMLX_JANG_TOOLS_SOURCE=/Users/eric/jang/.worktrees/vmlx-release-clean-b5f66a7/jang-tools \
  .venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
```

## [2026-05-22 14:47 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 short-prompt cache snapshot threshold + live speed split

I traced the reported DSV4 ~2-3 tok/s slowdown with pool/cache enabled and
split it into three separate facts:

1. Pool quant is not the current steady-state slowdown:
   - rebuilt dev app `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app` now bundles the fixed
     JANG `dsv4/pool_quant_cache.py`;
   - packaged integrity gate passes source hash for `scheduler.py` and
     `dsv4/pool_quant_cache.py`;
   - packaged pool-on vs pool-off non-stream both landed around 3.55 tok/s for
     the short cold request, so pool-on itself was not the delta.

2. Raw SSE/API spacing is not proven broken by pool-on:
   - fresh stream spacing captures had `has_spacing_suspect=false`;
   - old pool-on/off stream captures also assembled valid HTML spacing;
   - do not touch renderer spacing without a raw-stream-vs-UI diff.

3. The actual trace split:
   - DSV4 prefix is diagnostic opt-in; without `--dsv4-enable-prefix-cache`
     there is no prompt snapshot at all;
   - with `--dsv4-enable-prefix-cache` and `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1`, a short
     10-token prompt now logs `prompt_snapshot_skipped` and scheduler logs
     `prompt below snapshot/store threshold`;
   - first cold request is still slow from DSV4 compile/warmup:
     `prefill_head` about 14s and first `sample_materialize` about 7.5s;
   - second same request on the same server is back to about 20.9 tok/s.

Source changes:

- `vmlx_engine/utils/dsv4_batch_generator.py`
  - added `DEFAULT_DSV4_PROMPT_SNAPSHOT_MIN_TOKENS = 256`;
  - added `dsv4_prompt_snapshot_min_tokens()`;
  - short DSV4 N-1 prompt keys skip synchronous composite snapshot by default;
  - env override:
    `DSV4_PROMPT_SNAPSHOT_MIN_TOKENS` or legacy
    `VMLINUX_DSV4_PROMPT_SNAPSHOT_MIN_TOKENS`.
- `vmlx_engine/scheduler.py`
  - if the DSV4 prompt is below that threshold and no snapshot exists, cleanup
    skips donating a prefix block instead of synchronously re-prefilling just
    to store cache.

New/updated tests:

- `test_dsv4_generator_skips_prompt_snapshot_for_short_cache_store_prompt_by_default`;
- `test_dsv4_short_prompt_snapshot_skip_does_not_sync_reprefill_for_store`;
- `test_dsv4_generator_captures_prompt_snapshot_when_cache_store_enabled`
  now sets `DSV4_PROMPT_SNAPSHOT_MIN_TOKENS=0` to prove the diagnostic/long
  store path still works.

Verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py \
  -k 'prompt_snapshot or short_prompt_snapshot'
# 4 passed

PYTHONPATH=/Users/eric/jang/.worktrees/vmlx-release-clean-b5f66a7/jang-tools \
  .venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py
# 53 passed

PYTHONPATH=/Users/eric/jang/.worktrees/vmlx-release-clean-b5f66a7/jang-tools \
  .venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_cache_architecture_contract.py \
  --out build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260522-dsv4-short-snapshot-threshold.json
# status=pass; failed=[]; missing_markers=[]
# cache-family 111 passed; panel 88 passed / 179 skipped
```

Live proof:

- `build/current-dsv4-prefix-enabled-two-turn-threshold-live-20260522.json`;
- model: `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/DeepSeek-V4-Flash-JANG`;
- flags: `--dsv4-enable-prefix-cache`, paged cache, block disk cache,
  `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1`, generic KV quant off;
- first request: 32 tokens in 23.36s, cold compile/warmup dominated;
- second request: 32 tokens in 1.53s, about 20.9 tok/s;
- log includes `prompt_snapshot_skipped` and scheduler short-store skip.

Important environment finding:

- The repo `.venv` still imports stale `jang_tools` from
  `.venv/lib/python3.13/site-packages`; without fixed JANG on `PYTHONPATH`,
  `test_dsv4_pool_quant_reuses_materialized_pool_between_appends` fails.
- Packaged/bundled app was rebuilt with the fixed clean JANG worktree and
  passes packaged source-hash integrity.
- Direct isolated packaged behavior check from `/tmp` passed:
  bundled `PoolQuantizedV4Cache` produced `dequant_count=0`, shape `(1, 4, 16)`,
  and `nbytes_ok=True`.
- Source test runs must use the same fixed JANG path or reinstall the venv
  dependency before treating pool test failures as runtime regressions.

Still open:

- DSV4 first cold request remains compile/warmup slow; this is separate from
  pool quant and snapshot store.
- DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is still not release-cleared.

## [2026-05-22 15:58 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen JANG_4M live speed/cache split

I followed up on Eric's JANG/MX matmul speed concern with a real packaged-engine
decode-speed row for `qwen27_jang4m`.

Artifact:

- `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-20260522-hybrid-tq-review.json`

Result:

- `status=review`;
- cache-health false failure is gone;
- remaining note:
  `PP below expected 600.00: 283.95, 289.03, 251.06`;
- bundle decode: `25.76 tok/s` against row floor `18 tok/s`;
- coherency decode: `19.47 tok/s`, no reasoning and no loopish output.

Root cause of the previous `fail`:

- the gate had a stale blanket invariant that all hybrid SSM rows must have
  `generic_turboquant_kv.enabled=false`;
- current Qwen3.6 runtime intentionally uses live TurboQuant only for attention
  KV layers and reports:
  - `reason=hybrid_attention_kv_only`;
  - `applies_to=attention_kv_layers_only`;
  - `ssm_policy=native_full_precision_companion_state`.

Code/test changes:

- `tests/cross_matrix/run_decode_speed_gate.py` now accepts only that Qwen
  attention-only health payload;
- DSV4 and ZAYA still require generic TQ off;
- non-Qwen hybrid SSM still rejects generic TQ;
- `tests/test_model_family_detection_contract.py` pins the split;
- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py` now has live performance
  row `qwen-jang-mx-live-speed-review`;
- checkpoint and status docs are updated.

Do not summarize this as broad speed clearance. It is a better split:

- Qwen/JANG decode: okay in this row;
- Qwen/JANG PP: still review under current target;
- DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality: still open.

Verification/caveat:

- clean-JANG cache architecture:
  `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260522-qwen-live-speed-review.json`
  -> pass;
- clean-JANG packaged integrity:
  `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260522-qwen-live-speed-review.json`
  -> pass;
- clean-JANG umbrella:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260522-qwen-live-speed-review-clean-jang.json`
  -> pass with only the known DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation open
  requirement.

Important: default verification against `/Users/eric/jang/jang-tools` currently
fails because that JANG source tree is tracked-dirty in
`jang_tools/convert_hy3_jangtq.py` and `tests/test_hy3_capabilities.py`.
Packaged app hash parity matches the clean worktree:

## [2026-05-22 16:14 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - SingleBatch prefill keep-alloc wiring fixed; source-only proof

I followed the Qwen/JANG PP review one level deeper. This is not a release
clearance claim.

Root cause:

- PR #163's explicit `--prefill-keep-alloc` / `VMLINUX_PREFILL_KEEP_ALLOC=1`
  path existed and was honored by `mllm_batch_generator.py`;
- `qwen27_jang4m` live speed uses `SingleBatchGenerator` because
  `max_num_seqs=1`;
- `SingleBatchGenerator._prefill()` still called `mx.clear_cache()` after every
  prefill chunk unconditionally, so the opt-in allocator reuse flag did not
  affect single-sequence JANG rows.

Change:

- `vmlx_engine/utils/single_batch_generator.py` now honors
  `VMLINUX_PREFILL_KEEP_ALLOC=1` or legacy `VMLX_PREFILL_KEEP_ALLOC=1`;
- omitted/default behavior is unchanged and still clears after each chunk;
- no sampler, generation default, max-token, cache-family, or hidden behavior
  changed.

Proof:

- red/green guard:
  `tests/test_perf_prefill_loop.py::test_single_batch_prefill_loop_env_gates_clear_cache`;
- harness red/green guard:
  `tests/test_model_family_detection_contract.py::test_decode_speed_gate_extra_serve_arg_is_opt_in_without_mutating_row_defaults`;
- source live run with explicit `--prefill-keep-alloc`:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-source-keepalloc-20260522.json`
  -> `status=pass`, decode `22.87 tok/s`, notes `[]`;
- release manifest row `qwen-jang-mx-live-speed-review` now references this
  source-only artifact so the distinction is durable.
- fresh max-output/context gate:
  `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260522-after-singlebatch-keepalloc.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, `missing_markers=[]`;
- fresh model-family detection gate:
  `build/current-model-family-detection-contract-20260522-singlebatch-keepalloc.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, `missing_rows=[]`;
- fresh clean-JANG umbrella:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260522-singlebatch-keepalloc.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed_steps=[]`, only open requirement
  `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`.

Caveats:

- This source proof does not mean the packaged app has the change until bundled
  Python is rebuilt and integrity/parity passes.
- It does not clear packaged Qwen PP review because this artifact did not
  record the same PP measurements.
- DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality remains open.

Pushed:

- `574a9f21 perf: gate single-batch prefill keep alloc`
- remotes: `origin/codex/pr-intake-manifest`, `origin/main`

## [2026-05-22 18:11 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - MTP speed is not fully clear; decode works, prefill is slow

Eric asked "MTP speeds", so I ran a focused live MTP row instead of relying on
the no-heavy/native-MTP contracts.

Command:

`.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_decode_speed_gate.py --rows qwen27_jang4m_mtp --port 8795 --timeout 360 --out build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-20260523.json`

Artifact:

- `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-20260523.json`
- log: `build/decode-speed-logs/qwen27_jang4m_mtp-1779498428.log`

What it proves:

- Launch wiring is correct for the MTP artifact:
  `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/Qwen3.6-27B-JANG_4M-MTP`,
  `--is-mllm`, qwen tool parser, qwen3 reasoning parser, paged cache, block L2,
  q4 KV cache.
- Security banner says `Native MTP: READY D3 (scope: text+vl, policy: compatible-only)`.
- Native MTP activates only for deterministic requests. Sampled/default probes
  skip it with: `temperature=1.0 is not deterministic`.
- Deterministic MTP decode:
  - coherency probe: 16 tokens in 1.39s -> 11.50 tok/s, D3 active,
    8/24 drafted tokens accepted;
  - greedy 320-token probe: 33.40 tok/s, MTP active, adaptive D3->D2->D1,
    169/180 drafted tokens accepted (93.9%).
- Sampled bundle decode without MTP activation: 23.89 tok/s.

What is not clear:

- Row status is `review`, not pass.
- PP rows are slow: `[143.74, 146.48, 146.66]`, below the current 600 tok/s
  floor.
- Logs show this MTP artifact takes the MLLM/VL hybrid path, captures/stores
  clean SSM companion state at prefill boundaries, and at 22k prompt tokens
  enters chunked prefill because the one-shot attention buffer would be 29.3GB.
  This is the likely difference from the non-MTP Qwen JANG_4M public-Tahoe
  proof, which passed PP at `[908.95, 911.52, 764.04]`.

Do not overclaim MTP speed as release-clear. Decode speed with deterministic
MTP looks healthy; prefill/MLLM hybrid path is the open issue to investigate.
No process remained on port 8795 after the run.

DSV4 live audit also finished and is still not clear:

- `build/current-production-family-audit-live-dsv4-jang-local-refresh-20260523.json`
- runner: `live: FAIL failures=5`
- failing rows:
  `dsv4_thinking_mode_max`,
  `dsv4_threejs_identifier_integrity`,
  `dsv4_long_context_full_output_vc_project_plan`,
  `dsv4_long_context_full_output_game_design_long_context`,
  `responses_tool_history_continuation`
- existing issue note still applies: output-head/final-norm precision boundary
  needs source-vs-quant or rebuilt-artifact clearance before DSV4 long-output
  production claims.

## [2026-05-22 18:40 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - MTP bypass SSM work fixed; prefill still not release-clear

I found and fixed a real MTP/VL speed-adjacent bug, but it does not fully clear
the MTP speed row.

Root cause slice:

- The live speed gate sends `skip_prefix_cache=true` / `cache_salt` to get
  fresh, unpolluted PP/decode measurements.
- MLLM fetch paths respected that bypass, but Qwen hybrid text-only prefill
  still:
  - split at SSM clean-boundary points;
  - captured inline SSM companion checkpoints;
  - stored SSM companion entries after prefill.
- That violates the bypass contract and inflated the MTP PP rows. It also meant
  benchmark/API callers asking for no prefix-cache side effects still paid
  hidden SSM companion work.

Changed:

- `vmlx_engine/mllm_batch_generator.py`
  - `_clean_ssm_boundary_for()` returns `0` for bypassed requests;
  - post-prefill SSM companion capture/store is skipped for bypassed requests;
  - fixed `VMLINUX_DISABLE_SSM_INLINE_CAPTURE` -> `VMLX_DISABLE_SSM_INLINE_CAPTURE`.
- `tests/test_cache_bypass.py`
  - added guard that bypassed MLLM requests skip inline SSM boundary/capture
    work and post-prefill companion store/rederive.

Verification:

- Red test failed first on missing bypass guard.
- Focused tests now pass:
  `tests/test_cache_bypass.py` plus two native-MTP source tests -> `62 passed`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Source live proof:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-source-bypass-fix-20260523.json`.

Live speed result after source fix:

- status is still `review`;
- PP improved from packaged `[143.74, 146.48, 146.66]` to source
  `[287.23, 282.97, 262.85]`;
- sampled decode stayed healthy at `23.22 tok/s`;
- deterministic greedy MTP decode stayed healthy at `35.17 tok/s`;
- cache stats prove bypass side effects are gone:
  scheduler cache writes/hits zero, SSM companion entries/stores zero.

Do not call MTP speed release-clear yet. The remaining PP gap is now mostly the
native-MTP artifact's required MLLM/VL language route versus the faster Qwen
text-loader route, not prefix/paged/L2 cache storage or SSM companion writes.

## [2026-05-22 18:48 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - MTP decode A/B is green; MLLM prefill remains the open row

I ran the missing same-artifact native-MTP A/B so we stop conflating decode
speed with the MLLM prefill bottleneck.

Command:

`.venv/bin/python bench/native_mtp_speed_ab.py /Users/eric/models/JANGQ/Qwen3.6-27B-JANG_4M-MTP --served-name qwen27-jang4m-mtp-ab --port 8798 --cache off --max-num-seqs 1 --max-tokens 320 --repeats 1 --warmup 0 --load-timeout-s 420 --out build/current-native-mtp-speed-ab-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-20260523 --disable-prompt-reuse`

Artifact:

- `build/current-native-mtp-speed-ab-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-20260523/result.json`

Result:

- Same artifact, same MLLM/VL loader, cache off.
- Baseline AR: `25.61 tok/s`.
- Native MTP D3: `46.96 tok/s`.
- Speedup: `1.83x`.
- Output equivalence: `all_content_equal=true`, `all_full_text_equal=true`.
- MTP accepted `218/228` drafted tokens (`95.6%`) and adapted D3 -> D2 after
  24 cycles.
- No listeners/processes remained on `8798` or `8799`.

Interpretation:

- Native MTP decode itself is working and fast on the current artifact.
- The release-open MTP row is still real, but it is specifically long-prompt
  prefill throughput on the required MLLM/VL route:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-source-bypass-fix-20260523.json`
  is still `review` with PP `[287.23, 282.97, 262.85]` vs the 600 floor.
- Do not "fix" this by forcing text-only or disabling real VL routing. That
  would hide the artifact contract. The next useful investigation is MLLM/VL
  text-only prefill overhead versus the text-loader SingleBatch path.

I updated the tracked release manifest to include the same-artifact A/B command
and artifact, with a regression test so it does not get dropped:

- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
- `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`

  -> `69 passed`;

## [2026-05-22 19:07 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen native-MTP VLM adapter fixes; speed row still open

I fixed two source-level Qwen native-MTP VLM adapter issues and proved them with
targeted tests plus a live row. Important: this does **not** clear MTP speed.

Code changed:

- `vmlx_engine/patches/mlx_vlm_mtp/qwen35_vl.py`
  - fresh text-only Qwen VLM attention no longer builds
    `mx.arange(cache.offset, ...)` inside every attention layer to recover a
    scalar RoPE offset. It now uses `cache.offset` directly, matching the
    mlx-lm text route;
  - Qwen VLM `GatedDeltaNet.__call__` no longer returns to the upstream
    unpatched path for `n_confirmed == 0`. Normal prefill/decode now uses the
    patched path and advances the hybrid SSM cache, matching the speculative
    MTP rollback path.
- `tests/test_native_mtp_autodetect.py`
  - added guards for scalar text RoPE offset and normal-path SSM cache advance.

Verification:

- each new test failed before the source change;
- focused suite:
  `tests/test_native_mtp_autodetect.py tests/test_native_mtp_contract.py tests/test_native_mtp_bench_harness.py tests/test_mllm_continuous_batching.py`
  -> `124 passed`.

Live source speed artifact:

- `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-vlm-cache-advance-fix-20260523.json`

Live result:

- `status=review`;
- PP rows: `[286.87, 281.27, 263.18]`;
- sampled decode: `22.81 tok/s`;
- greedy native-MTP decode: `35.15 tok/s`;
- MTP active: `runtime_scope=text+vl`, effective depth `3`, acceptance
  `169/180` drafted tokens (`93.9%`), final adapted depth `1`.

Interpretation:

- These are real adapter correctness/perf-cleanup fixes.
- They restore/maintain the best MLLM prefill range we had after the cache-bypass
  fix, but the row is still below the 600 pp/s release floor.
- Do not mark MTP speed release-cleared.
- Do not fake-clear it by forcing the native-MTP+VL artifact onto the text-only
  loader; the artifact has vision weights and must keep the real MLLM/VL route.
- Remaining useful investigation: why MLLM/VL text-only prefill stays around
  260-287 pp/s while the comparable Qwen text-loader SingleBatch row is
  760-910 pp/s.

## [2026-05-22 19:28 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - MTP speed trace; PP floor is broader than MTP/VL

I added an env-gated MLLM prefill timing trace and reran the Qwen MTP speed row.

  - `VMLINUX_MLLM_PREFILL_TRACE=1` logs named prefill segments:
    preprocess, cache lookup, cache prepare, forward, logits eval, clear cache,
    sampler call, cache submit, token item, SSM capture, cache merge;

    bad diagnostic reference to a non-existent `_native_mtp_runtime` field.

  -> `126 passed`.

- MTP/VL trace:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-prefill-trace3-20260523.json`
- Text-loader comparison:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-text-baseline-20260523.json`

Results:

- MTP/VL trace row:
  `status=review`, PP `[152.24, 152.98, 156.03]` with trace-sync overhead,
  sampled decode `25.42 tok/s`, greedy native-MTP decode `35.89 tok/s`.
- Important trace split:
  - 1429 prompt tokens: `logits_eval_ms=8650`;
  - 5577 prompt tokens: `logits_eval_ms=34746`;
  - 22165 prompt tokens: `forward_ms=141593`.
- The small buckets are genuinely small:
  preprocessing/cache lookup/cache prepare/sampler call/cache submit/SSM
  capture/cache merge are not the PP bottleneck.
- Fresh text-loader Qwen 27B JANG_4M comparison:
  PP `[284.17, 292.06, 263.41]`, sampled decode `23.52 tok/s`, greedy
  `27.99 tok/s`.

Interpretation:

- Native MTP decode remains good and faster than the text-loader greedy row.
- The PP release floor is still open, but the fresh comparison shows the
  comparable Qwen 27B text-loader row also misses 600 pp/s. So this is not
  proven to be an MTP/VL-only regression anymore.
- Do not mark MTP prefill speed release-cleared.
- Do not fake-clear by forcing the MTP+VL artifact to text-only, disabling VL,
  hiding cache features, or changing generation defaults. The remaining work is
  either recalibrating the Qwen 27B JANG_4M PP floor against current hardware/
  artifact reality, or a real model/runtime/kernel optimization in the language
  forward path.

  path carefully without losing real VL/media/MTP semantics.

- `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`
