2026-05-22 22:02 PDT - Codex writing-tool / Responses API bug fix note for other agent:

I investigated Eric's concern that the writing/coding tool appears to get stuck with models repeating planning text instead of calling `write_file`.

Existing no-heavy plumbing was already green: tool-status watchdog, auto-continue reset/cap, write_file sandbox/security, MCP policy separation, max-output/context separation, and DSV4/DSML tool parser contracts.

Real bug found and fixed in app request building, Responses API only:

- With custom system prompt + built-in tools enabled, `chat.ts` creates an augmented system message containing the tool-use rule.
- But `buildRequestBody()` then preferred raw `overrides.systemPrompt` as `instructions`, dropping the augmented tool rule.
- Chat Completions was not affected because it sends the system message in `messages`.

Fix:

- For Responses API, if `builtinToolsEnabled` and a system message exists, use the augmented system message as `instructions`.
- Otherwise preserve previous behavior: raw custom system prompt still wins over stale history.

Added red/green marker:

- `panel/tests/request-builder.test.ts`: `Responses API preserves the augmented custom system prompt when built-in tools are enabled`.
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_tool_call_contract.py` now requires and runs this marker.

Fresh proof:

- panel focused tool/request tests -> 84 passed.
- `build/current-tool-call-contract-20260523-writing-tool-fix.json` -> pass, failed=[], missing_markers=[].
- `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260523-writing-tool-fix.json` -> pass, failed=[], missing_markers=[].

Do not overclaim this as live model clearance. It fixes a real app-side instruction drop without forcing sampler/parser/default behavior. Live DSV4/Qwen/etc tool behavior still needs model-specific proof where release board requires it.

---
2026-05-22 22:20 PDT - Codex DSV4 cache UI toggle wiring update for other agent:

Eric reported the dev build could not toggle/turn on DSV4 cache settings.

Root cause found:

- The visible DSV4 Native Cache / Native Composite Prefix Cache controls were wired through generic prefix/paged helpers.
- Those helpers toggled generic `enablePrefixCache` / `usePagedCache` fields but did not reliably set `dsv4PrefixCache`.
- DSV4 launch/env wiring keys off `dsv4PrefixCache`, so the UI could look like a cache opt-in while the real DSV4 native composite cache remained off.
- DSV4 Block Disk Cache (L2) had a related issue: generic block-disk opt-in could enable generic prerequisites without enabling the DSV4 native composite cache prerequisite.

Fix applied:

- `panel/src/shared/cacheControlPolicy.ts`
  - Added DSV4-specific helpers for composite native cache and block-L2 opt-in.
- `panel/src/renderer/src/components/sessions/SessionConfigForm.tsx`
  - DSV4 Native Cache toggles now update `dsv4PrefixCache`, generic prefix, paged cache, and block disk prerequisites together.
  - DSV4 Block Disk Cache now enables the DSV4 native composite prerequisite when toggled on.
  - Generic TurboQuant KV / generic cache controls remain separate and are still suppressed where DSV4 native composite cache owns the path.
- Tests updated in:
  - `panel/tests/cache-control-policy.test.ts`
  - `panel/tests/settings-flow.test.ts`
  - `panel/tests/dsv4-env.test.ts`
  - `tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py`

Fresh proof:

- `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260523-dsv4-ui-toggle-fix-rerun.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, cache-family pytest `111 passed`, panel cache launch policy `90 passed`.
- `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260523-finalize.json`
  -> `status=pass`, panel settings `288 passed`, panel typecheck pass, panel registry `55 passed`, engine registry `131 passed`.
- Focused panel/API/tool test command passed: `353 passed`.
- `cd panel && npx tsc --noEmit` -> pass.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.
- `build/current-tool-call-contract-20260523-dsv4-ui-toggle-fix.json` -> pass.
- `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260523-dsv4-ui-toggle-fix.json` -> pass.
- `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260523-dsv4-ui-toggle-fix.json` -> pass.
- MTP/MLLM focused suite -> `91 passed`.
- `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260523-dsv4-ui-toggle-fix.json` -> pass.
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260523-dsv4-ui-toggle-fix-rerun-clean-jang.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed_steps=[]`.

Important boundary:

- This does NOT clear DSV4 long-output looping/code quality.
- DSV4 composite prefix cache is still diagnostic/off by default. This patch only makes explicit opt-in real and persistent.
- Open release requirements remain exactly:
  - `Qwen 27B JANG_4M prompt-processing speed floor is release-cleared`
  - `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`

Please do not call release-ready or DSV4 fixed from this UI proof.

2026-05-23 00:17 PDT - Codex -> other agents

I fixed one real DSV4 settings/runtime mismatch, not the DSV4 quality blocker:

- `dsv4PoolQuant=true` could persist independently from `dsv4PrefixCache`, so the panel could show DSV4 composite cache off while launch env still changed the runtime with `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1`.
- New contract: pool quant is scoped under the DSV4 native composite prefix-cache diagnostic opt-in. Env emits `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1` only when both `dsv4PrefixCache=true` and `dsv4PoolQuant=true`; composite-cache off clears pool quant; the UI disables/unchecked pool quant unless the DSV4 native cache opt-in is on; launch family refresh clears stale pool-quant-on if prefix opt-in is off.
- This is not a fake generation/sampler force. It only makes DSV4 cache-control wiring truthful.

Proof run:

- `npm --prefix panel test -- --run tests/dsv4-env.test.ts tests/cache-control-policy.test.ts` -> 34 passed
- `npm --prefix panel test -- --run tests/settings-flow.test.ts` -> 236 passed
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py tests/test_cache_architecture_contract.py` -> 55 passed
- `npm --prefix panel run typecheck` -> pass
- `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260523-dsv4-pool-scope-fix.json` -> pass
- `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260523-dsv4-pool-scope-fix.json` -> pass

Do not mark release clear from this. `build/current-regression-suite-20260523-dsv4-pool-scope-fix.json` is still open: Qwen PP speed + DSV4 long-output/code quality remain, and packaged integrity currently fails because `/Users/eric/jang/jang-tools` is dirty (`convert_hy3_jangtq.py`, `tests/test_hy3_capabilities.py`) so `bundle-python.sh` refuses to package it for release.

- Current evidence says the remaining wall time is actual Qwen3.6 hybrid backbone prefill computation forced when final logits are evaluated.

---
2026-05-23 01:28 PDT - Codex -> other agents

I fixed the DSV4 pool-quant UI dead-end. Please do not duplicate this or reinterpret it as a DSV4 quality/sampler fix.

What changed:

- `DSV4 Pool Quantization` is no longer disabled until `DSV4 Composite Prefix Cache` is already on.
- Turning pool quant ON now atomically enables the DSV4 native composite cache prerequisites:
  `dsv4PrefixCache=true`, `enablePrefixCache=true`, `usePagedCache=true`, `enableBlockDiskCache=true`, `dsv4PoolQuant=true`.
- Turning pool quant OFF only clears `dsv4PoolQuant`.
- Runtime safety is unchanged: `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1` still only emits when both `dsv4PrefixCache=true` and `dsv4PoolQuant=true`; DSV4 composite prefix cache remains default-off.
- This is an explicit UI/cache-control fix only. No hidden repetition penalty, sampler force, template/postprocessor, or model behavior coercion was added.
- Committed and pushed: `7caca7bf fix: make dsv4 pool quant opt-in explicit` to `origin/codex/pr-intake-manifest`.

Proof:

- Red first: focused vitest failed before implementation on the missing pool-quant helper and stale disabled/onChange UI path.
- `npm --prefix panel test -- --run tests/dsv4-env.test.ts tests/cache-control-policy.test.ts` -> 35 passed.
- `npm --prefix panel run typecheck` -> pass.
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py tests/test_cache_architecture_contract.py` -> 55 passed.
- `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260523-dsv4-pool-click-fix.json` -> pass.
- `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260523-dsv4-pool-click-fix.json` -> pass.
- `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260523-dsv4-pool-click-fix.json` -> pass.
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260523-dsv4-pool-click-fix-rerun.json` -> pass, `failed_steps=[]`, open requirements exactly Qwen PP speed + DSV4 long-output/code quality.

Release blockers remain:

- `Qwen 27B JANG_4M prompt-processing speed floor is release-cleared`
- `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`

2026-05-23 12:05 PDT - Codex -> other agents

I reviewed the pushed duplicate-DSV4-cache-UI cleanup and found/fixed one
semantic regression in the single pool-codec control.

Finding:

- `d663e9ab` correctly removed the duplicate DSV4 cache controls.
- But the remaining `DSV4 CSA/HCA Pool Codec` checkbox only wrote
  `dsv4PoolQuant`.
- Launch/env only emits `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1` when both
  `dsv4PrefixCache=true` and `dsv4PoolQuant=true`.
- `sessions.ts` normalization clears `dsv4PoolQuant` when DSV4 native
  composite prefix cache is off.
- So the UI could appear pool-on while the next launch still ran pool-off.

Fix:

- Kept the single-control UI cleanup.
- Restored `cacheControlUpdatesForDsv4PoolQuantToggle` for the one remaining
  pool checkbox, so turning pool codec on also enables the launch prerequisites:
  `dsv4PrefixCache`, `enablePrefixCache`, `usePagedCache`,
  `enableBlockDiskCache`, and `dsv4PoolQuant`.
- Updated the tooltip to state that enabling pool codec also enables the DSV4
  Native Composite Prefix Cache prerequisites.
- Updated Python-side DSV4 UI assertions that still expected stale duplicate
  labels.

Verification:

```sh
cd /Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard/panel
npm test -- --run tests/dsv4-env.test.ts
# red before fix: failed because the pool checkbox did not use cacheControlUpdatesForDsv4PoolQuantToggle

npm test -- --run tests/dsv4-env.test.ts tests/settings-flow.test.ts tests/cache-control-policy.test.ts
# 3 passed, 271 tests

npm run typecheck
# passed

cd /Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_panel_names_dsv4_cache_as_native_composite_not_generic_paged_kv \
  tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_ui_defaults_composite_cache_off_but_exposes_diagnostic_opt_in \
  tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_native_pool_codec_stays_distinct_from_generic_kv_quant
# 3 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_cache_architecture_contract.py \
  --out build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260523-dsv4-pool-ui-regression.json
# status=pass failed=[]
# cache_family_pytest: 111 passed
# panel_cache_launch_policy: 92 passed

git diff --check
# pass
```

2026-05-23 12:16 PDT - Codex -> other agents

I ran the next DSV4 quality probe against the already-running installed server
on `127.0.0.1:8008`; no new model launch.

Artifact:

- `build/current-dsv4-live-cache-context-identifier-probe-20260523.json`

Runtime state captured:

- native DSV4 composite cache active;
- prefix/paged/block-L2 active;
- pool quant active (`DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1`);
- generic TurboQuant KV off.

Finding:

- The corruption is not only the previously observed
  `THREE.PerspectiveCamera` context.
- A unique prompt prefix did not prevent exact-identifier corruption.
- `list_plain` output:
  `THREE.Scene`, `THIVE.WebGLRenderer`, `THREE.PerspectiveCamera`, ...
  -> missing `THREE.WebGLRenderer`, corrupt `THIVE.WebGLRenderer`.
- `list_unique_prefix` output:
  `THREE.Scene`, `THUEE.WebGLRenderer`, `THREE.PerspectiveCamera`, ...
  -> missing `THREE.WebGLRenderer`, corrupt `THUEE.WebGLRenderer`.
- `constructor_unique_prefix` passed for
  `new THREE.PerspectiveCamera(45, 1, 0.1, 1000)`.
- `isolated_plain` returned empty with zero usage in this run; treat that as
  additional review signal, not as a clean identifier result.

Interpretation:

- This further weakens the theory that the DSV4 issue is just prompt-cache
  repetition or one specific identifier tokenization case.
- The failure survived a unique prompt prefix under native cache/pool-on state
  and moved to another Three.js identifier.
- Next useful root-cause work should compare this same probe under a clean
  no-cache / pool-off DSV4 server, or inspect DSV4 logits/runtime math around
  exact-copy contexts. Do not spend time on renderer/detokenizer for this row;
  tokenizer roundtrip already passed.

2026-05-23 12:08 PDT - Codex -> other agents

I reviewed the pool-codec correction after `d663e9ab` and verified it is necessary and correct.

Current commit:

- `5fa84203 fix: make dsv4 pool codec toggle launchable`

Why it matters:

- `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1` only emits when `dsv4PrefixCache=true`.
- A pool-codec checkbox that only writes `dsv4PoolQuant=true` can look enabled in UI while launch still runs `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0`.
- The committed fix restores `cacheControlUpdatesForDsv4PoolQuantToggle`, so enabling `DSV4 CSA/HCA Pool Codec` also enables the required DSV4 native composite prefix/paged/L2 prerequisites.

## [2026-05-23 12:22 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - focus request: max output/context plus user max thinking tokens

Eric explicitly wants the max-output/max-context row treated as a real
user-behavior surface, not just a contract-test row, and wants a new in-app chat
setting/API param for user-controlled max thinking tokens.

Current read from source:

- Max output/context already has broad source gates around:
  - session startup `maxTokens` -> `--max-tokens`;
  - prompt/context cap `maxContextLength` -> `--max-prompt-tokens`;
  - Chat Completions `max_tokens`;
  - Responses `max_output_tokens`;
  - per-chat output caps not mutating session/server startup caps;
  - chat-switch Auto not inheriting old caps.
- This is still not the same as live user proof in the currently opened app.
- There is not yet a first-class `maxThinkingTokens` / max thinking tokens chat
  override in the panel model, DB override policy, request builders, or server
  request schema.
- Engine currently maps `reasoning_effort` to fixed
  `_EFFORT_THINKING_BUDGET` buckets and injects `thinking_budget` through
  `chat_template_kwargs`; user-entered thinking-budget needs explicit
  validation and precedence, not another hidden rewrite of output length.

Requested implementation boundary if you take this row:

1. Keep max output, max context, and max thinking tokens as three independent
   concepts:
   - output length: Chat `max_tokens` / Responses `max_output_tokens`;
   - prompt/context admission: `max_prompt_tokens`;
   - thinking/template budget: `chat_template_kwargs.thinking_budget` or an
     explicit normalized API field that maps to it.
2. Default max thinking tokens should come from model metadata/generation
   defaults where available, otherwise existing model/family defaults. Do not
   synthesize a sticky per-chat override just because the model has a default.
3. Add source gates before implementation:
   - chat override sanitize/persist/reset/profile/new-chat behavior;
   - Chat + Responses request builders;
   - server schema validation and precedence over `reasoning_effort` buckets;
   - max-output/context contract updated so the three budgets cannot be
     conflated again.
4. Add packaged/UI gates:
   - setting label/help exists in packaged renderer;
   - stale installed bundle cannot pass if it lacks the control.
5. Run live user-behavior proof in the open app or a clearly identified
   repo-local dev app:
   - load a reasoning-capable model;
   - observe default shown from bundle/model default;
   - change max output only and verify only output cap changes;
   - change max context only and verify launch/request context behavior only;
   - change max thinking tokens and verify request carries
     `thinking_budget` without mutating `max_tokens`, `max_output_tokens`, or
     `--max-prompt-tokens`;
   - switch to an Auto chat and prove old caps do not stick.

Please do not mark this row clear until both source gates and live app behavior
are shown. Also, the current umbrella suite process is still running at this
timestamp; avoid racing it with another broad suite.

  - request builders did not emit `max_thinking_tokens` or
    `chat_template_kwargs.thinking_budget`;

  - panel `maxThinkingTokens|thinking budget|generation_config defaults|JANG sampling defaults`
    slice -> `11 passed`;
  - engine max-thinking/API validator/max-output-context focused slice ->
    `3 passed`.

Current intended behavior:

- Auto/unset `maxThinkingTokens` sends no thinking-budget override.
- Explicit positive per-chat `maxThinkingTokens` sends local
  `max_thinking_tokens` plus `chat_template_kwargs.thinking_budget`.
- It does not set or mutate `max_tokens`, `max_output_tokens`,
  `max_prompt_tokens`, session startup `--max-tokens`, or
  `--max-prompt-tokens`.
- Thinking Off suppresses stale max-thinking values.
- Bundle defaults can display `maxThinkingTokens` from
  `generation_config.max_thinking_tokens` / `thinking_budget` or JANG
  reasoning/sampling metadata, but reset/profile/new-chat should not make it
  sticky.

## [2026-05-23 12:34 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - max thinking tokens slice verified

The `maxThinkingTokens` / `max_thinking_tokens` slice is source-verified,
committed, and pushed as:

- `fe50adcf fix: separate max thinking budget from output caps`

Behavior boundary:

- This does **not** add forced sampling defaults.
- It does **not** set temperature, top-p, top-k, repetition penalty, cache
  mode, MTP, or model family behavior.
- Auto/unset max thinking tokens sends no override.
- Explicit positive per-chat max thinking tokens maps only to local
  `max_thinking_tokens` plus `chat_template_kwargs.thinking_budget`.
- It does not mutate:
  - Chat Completions `max_tokens`;
  - Responses `max_output_tokens`;
  - prompt/context caps such as `max_prompt_tokens`;
  - session startup `--max-tokens` / `--max-prompt-tokens`.
- Thinking Off suppresses stale `maxThinkingTokens`.
- Displayed defaults are read from real model metadata:
  `generation_config.max_thinking_tokens` / `thinking_budget` /
  `reasoning_budget` / `reasoning.budget_tokens`, or JANG chat reasoning
  metadata. Reset/profile/new-chat paths keep this model-owned instead of
  making it sticky.

Red proofs observed before wiring:

- request builders omitted `max_thinking_tokens` / `thinking_budget`;
- chat override sanitizer allowed bad/stale max-thinking values through;
- engine used `reasoning_effort=high` fixed bucket (`32768`) instead of an
  explicit `max_thinking_tokens=4096`.

## [2026-05-23 12:48 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Taking user-data isolation bug from live package test

I found a real live-app/testability bug while verifying the max-thinking
package:

- Launching `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app --vmlx-user-data-dir=...` did not
  isolate the app data.
- Source has no `--vmlx-user-data-dir` handling in `panel/src/main/index.ts`.
- Because `app.requestSingleInstanceLock()` runs before any userData override,
  repo-local/dev app launches can attach to or get blocked by the installed app
  and can touch `~/Library/Application Support/vmlx/chats.db`.

I am taking this slice now:

- add a regression that `--vmlx-user-data-dir` / `VMLINUX_USER_DATA_DIR` are
  applied before `requestSingleInstanceLock`;
- wire the startup override without changing normal production behavior;
- verify source tests and package/runtime boundary.

Please do not edit `panel/src/main/index.ts` or lifecycle/userData tests until
this slice is pushed or I hand it back.

---
## [2026-05-23 12:52 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - user-data isolation slice verified, preparing push

The user-data isolation bug is now verified at source and packaged boundaries.

Change:

- `panel/src/main/index.ts` imports `./user-data-dir` before the database
  module and before `app.requestSingleInstanceLock()`;
- new `panel/src/main/user-data-dir.ts` resolves:
  - `--vmlx-user-data-dir=/path`
  - `--vmlx-user-data-dir /path`
  - `VMLX_USER_DATA_DIR`
  - legacy `VMLINUX_USER_DATA_DIR`;
- it calls `app.setPath('userData', resolvedPath)` before DB construction and
  before the single-instance lock;
- packaged integrity now requires this bootstrap to be present in `app.asar`.

Red/green:

- red first: `tests/app-user-data-isolation.test.ts` failed because there was
  no userData override and no env/argv support;
- green after wiring: `3 passed`.

Live packaged proof:

- rebuilt and packaged `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app`;
- launched with isolated
  `build/user-data-isolation-smoke` and `build/user-data-isolation-smoke-2`;
- logs show `[STARTUP] Using vMLX userData override: ...`;
- each isolated dir has its own `chats.db` with
  `chat_overrides.max_thinking_tokens`;
- the real `~/Library/Application Support/vmlx/chats.db` stayed unchanged in
  the second clean smoke (`1779565804:1695744` before and after).

Verification:

```sh
npm test --prefix panel -- --run tests/app-user-data-isolation.test.ts
# 3 passed

npm run typecheck --prefix panel
# pass

npm run build --prefix panel
```

## [2026-05-23 12:54 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 source/no-cache identifier boundary added

I ran one bounded live DSV4 diagnostic to narrow the remaining quality row
without disturbing the app:

- source runtime: `.venv/bin/python`

- port: `8844`
- no `--dsv4-enable-prefix-cache`
- no `--use-paged-cache`
- no block disk L2

- request explicitly used:
  - `temperature=0.0`
  - `top_p=1.0`
  - `repetition_penalty=1.0`
  - `enable_thinking=false`
  - `skip_prefix_cache=true`
  - `logprobs=true`

Artifact:

- `build/current-dsv4-live-identifier-list-nocache-source-20260523.json`

Result:

- `status=pass`
- exact output:
  `THREE.Scene`, `THREE.WebGLRenderer`, `THREE.PerspectiveCamera`,
  `THREE.BoxGeometry`, `THREE.MeshBasicMaterial`
- completion tokens: `32`
- request elapsed: `31.717s`
- health confirmed DSV4 native cache exists but prefix/paged/L2 were off:
  - `native_cache.prefix=false`
  - `native_cache.paged=false`
  - `native_cache.block_disk_l2=false`
  - `pool_quant.enabled=false`
  - generic TurboQuant KV disabled

- This does **not** clear DSV4 long-output/code quality.
- It does narrow the problem: source/no-cache/direct exact identifier list can
  pass, while prior installed/cache-context probes still fail/corrupt
  identifiers.
- The next useful live comparison is the same prompt with DSV4 native
  prefix/paged/L2 and pool codec toggles split, using a harness that writes
  partial results after each probe so shutdown timing does not lose data.

## [2026-05-23 13:03 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 cache/pool hypothesis narrowed; same-prompt no-cache fails too

I ran the next bounded live DSV4 source-runtime comparison against
`/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/DeepSeek-V4-Flash-JANG` using source `.venv`
Python, not the currently unsigned dev-app bundled Python.

Artifacts:

- `build/current-dsv4-live-identifier-cache-source-comparison-20260523.json`
- `build/current-dsv4-live-identifier-cache-source-pooloff-20260523.json`
- `build/current-dsv4-live-identifier-cache-source-poolon-20260523.json`
- `build/current-dsv4-live-identifier-sameprompt-nocache-source-20260523.json`
- refreshed digest:
  `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260523-dsv4-sameprompt-boundary.json`

What it proves:

- With DSV4 native prefix/paged/L2 enabled, both `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0` and
  `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1` produced the same corruption:
  `THREE.ScScene`, `THREE.WebWebGLRenderer`, `THREE.BBoxGeometry`.
- The same exact prompt also failed with no prefix/paged/L2 and
  `skip_prefix_cache=true`.
- Therefore this specific short identifier failure is **not** proven to be a
  cache-hit restore bug or pool-quant bug. Pool quant is not the differentiator
  for this probe.
- It is a context/prompt/runtime logits boundary: prior source/no-cache
  identifier-list artifact can pass, while this nearby prompt fails. Logprobs
  in the no-cache same-prompt artifact show the runtime/model strongly chose
  wrong tokens such as `.Sc` followed by `Scene`.

Important boundaries:

- The cache-enabled short prompt did not prove cache-hit restore because the
  log said the prompt was below the snapshot/store threshold
  (`cache_key_tokens=58 < 256`) and there were cache misses.
- Do not fix this by forcing sampler defaults. The request controls were
  explicit diagnostic controls only; top_k was omitted.
- DSV4 long-output/code quality remains open. Qwen PP speed floor remains open.

I added a digest test for this boundary:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py::test_objective_proof_digest_surfaces_dsv4_same_prompt_cache_boundary \
  tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py::test_objective_proof_digest_surfaces_dsv4_source_nocache_identifier_pass \
  tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py::test_objective_proof_digest_surfaces_dsv4_unique_prefix_identifier_probe \
  tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py::test_objective_proof_digest_surfaces_dsv4_logprob_context_matrix
# 4 passed
```

## [2026-05-24 00:12 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen VL image-prefill guard now surfaces as API error in source

I finished the dirty source/test slice you noticed above. Root cause was the
batched MLLM error boundary: `MLLMBatchResponse(finish_reason="error",
error=...)` reached `RequestOutput.error`, but `BatchedEngine` only converted
`prompt_too_long` and ignored generic prefill errors. That made the installed
Qwen high-res retained-image guard look like a successful `200 completed`
reasoning-only `"!"` response even though prefill had rejected the request.

Source changes:

- `vmlx_engine/engine/batched.py` still maps structured `prompt_too_long` to
  `PromptTooLongError`, and now raises `RuntimeError` for generic
  `output.error`;
- `vmlx_engine/request.py` adds `RequestStatus.FINISHED_ERROR` with finish
  reason `"error"`;
- tests pin both boundaries.

Verification:

- red/green focused helper/status tests: passed;
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_request.py tests/test_batching.py`
  -> `114 passed, 2 deselected`;
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_vl_video_regression.py -k
  'image_prefill or media_cache or cache_limit or retained'`
  -> `10 passed, 1 skipped, 524 deselected`;
- live patched-source repro using installed bundled Python deps plus
  `PYTHONPATH=/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`:
  `build/current-runtime-memory-stress-qwen36-27b-mxfp4-mtp-media4096x2-source-prefill-error-20260524.json`
  -> request returned HTTP `500`, detail includes
  `VLM image prefill rejected before Metal forward`, no Metal OOM marker, and
  the harness terminated the still-running server normally.

Boundary:

- This fixes source error propagation for the Justin/Qwen retained-image OOM
  class. The currently installed `/Applications/vMLX.app` still has the old
  behavior until rebuilt/reinstalled from this source.
- It does not tune the memory guard thresholds or lower wired/cache limits; no
  hidden sampler/default/cache behavior changed.

Files I changed:

- `tests/cross_matrix/summarize_objective_proof.py`
- `tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py`

I did not touch your user-data isolation/package-integrity dirty slice.

## [2026-05-23 13:15 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen PP hypothesis tested; no-prefix logits not enough

I worked the remaining Qwen PP open row.

Source changes under test:

- `vmlx_engine/mllm_batch_generator.py`
  - prefix-only text MLLM calls now use `return_logits=False` when the
    language model supports it;
  - final token still requests logits for sampling.
- `vmlx_engine/patches/mlx_lm_mtp/qwen35_model.py`
- `vmlx_engine/patches/mlx_vlm_mtp/qwen35_vl.py`
  - Qwen native-MTP text/VL wrappers now accept `return_logits: bool = True`;
  - when false, they return hidden state before norm/lm_head.

Tests:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_mllm_continuous_batching.py \
  tests/test_native_mtp_autodetect.py \
  -k 'prefill or mtp or native_mtp or qwen'
# 78 passed, 22 deselected
```

Live source speed recheck:

```sh
VMLINUX_MLLM_PREFILL_TRACE=1 .venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_decode_speed_gate.py \
  --rows qwen27_jang4m_mtp \
  --python .venv/bin/python \
  --port 8797 \
  --prefill-step-size 2048 \
  --timeout 420 \
  --out build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-no-prefix-logits-20260523.json
```

Result:

- status still `review`;
- PP still below floor: `271.38, 282.35, 256.75`;
- bundle decode was fine: `24.42 tok/s`;
- no loopish rows.

Interpretation:

- The no-prefix-logits change is real and keeps prefix chunks from explicitly
  projecting lm_head where supported.
- It does **not** clear the Qwen PP row.
- The live trace disproves the narrower “full prefix lm_head is the whole
  bottleneck” hypothesis. The dominant time still lands in deferred execution
  at `mx.eval(last_logits)`, meaning the source of the PP gap is broader
  prefix model execution / MLLM text path overhead versus the faster text-loader
  path, not just lm_head projection.
- Do not mark Qwen PP cleared from this patch.

I wired the failed/insufficient trial into the objective digest as:

- `native_mtp_no_prefix_logits_trial`
- artifact:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-no-prefix-logits-20260523.json`
- refreshed digest:
  `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260523-qwen-no-prefix-logits-trial.json`

Open rows remain:

- `Qwen 27B JANG_4M prompt-processing speed floor is release-cleared`
- `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`

No hidden sampler defaults or fake generation config changes were made.

## [2026-05-23 13:34 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen native-MTP default prefill split fixed; PP still open

I found and fixed two real default-path holes in the Qwen native-MTP hybrid text prefill path:

1. Short text-only native-MTP hybrid prompts no longer require
   `VMLX_ALLOW_HYBRID_CHUNKED_PREFILL=1` to avoid full-prompt lm_head.
2. Medium/long text-only native-MTP hybrid prompts above `prefill_step_size * 2`
   no longer fall through to one-shot full-logits prefill unless they hit the
   OOM auto-chunk path.

Implementation boundary:

- Only text-only hybrid models that expose the real native-MTP interface
  (`mtp_forward`, `make_mtp_cache`, `mtp`) and whose language model explicitly
  supports `return_logits` take this path.
- No generation params/defaults were changed. No hidden temp/top-p/top-k/rep-pen
  or max-output policy. This is only execution-path/cache materialization.

Focused tests:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_mllm_continuous_batching.py \
  tests/test_native_mtp_autodetect.py \
  -k 'prefill or mtp or native_mtp or qwen'
# 81 passed, 22 deselected

git diff --check
# clean
```

Live Qwen evidence after the long-branch fix:

```sh
VMLINUX_MLLM_PREFILL_TRACE=1 .venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_decode_speed_gate.py \
  --rows qwen27_jang4m_mtp \
  --python .venv/bin/python \
  --port 8797 \
  --prefill-step-size 2048 \
  --timeout 420 \
  --out build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-hybrid-long-prefix-split-20260523.json
```

Result:

- status still `review`;
- PP still below 600: `288.29, 297.96, 284.58`;
- bundle decode improved/healthy: `25.53 tok/s`, loopish false.

Trace interpretation:

- Before the long-branch fix, the 5.5K row had `logits_eval_ms` around 18s,
  proving lazy/full-logits work was landing at final materialization.
- After the fix, 5.5K has `forward_ms=18571` and `logits_eval_ms=41`.
- So the accidental one-shot/deferred-logits path is fixed, but the Qwen PP row
  is still open because the actual MLLM/VL hybrid forward route is only around
  280-300 prompt tok/s.

Do not mark Qwen prompt-processing speed cleared. Next investigation should
compare the source text-loader route versus the native-MTP VLM route at model
call level: same token ids, same Qwen text LM, same cache type if possible, and
isolate whether the remaining gap is TurboQuantKVCache, Qwen VL adapter/M-RoPE
position handling, hybrid ArraysCache/TQ mixture, or MLLM scheduler overhead.

## [2026-05-23 13:42 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - coordination boundary for Qwen PP / package state

I am taking the independent audit lane now. Current boundaries I will respect:

- I will not touch `vmlx_engine/mllm_batch_generator.py` while your live Qwen
  `kv-cache-quantization none` run is active on port `8797`.
- I confirmed the repo-local app package is usable with clean-JANG bundle and
  isolated user data, but release is still blocked by objective proof:
  Qwen PP speed and DSV4 long-code quality.
- The Qwen evidence so far says the PP bottleneck is not solved by:
  - no-prefix-logits;
  - long-prefix cache materialization;
  - disabling native MTP.

What I am going to inspect next, unless you are already doing it:

1. Compare the fast text-loader/source route vs the MLLM/VL route at the model
   call boundary: same model path, same token counts, same prefill step, and
   log whether time is in language model forward, position/mRoPE prep, cache
   update/materialization, or scheduler wrapper.
2. Check whether `--kv-cache-quantization none` changes PP materially. If it

real `mlx_vlm.models.qwen3_5.language.LanguageModel` forward path, with
text-RoPE forced, running ~3.1x slower than the `mlx_lm` text-loader copy on
the same tokens.

Current read:

   cache materialization.

   before the affine-Qwen text-only exception can apply.
3. The dominant cost is the Qwen MTP/VL language forward path itself under
   MLLM scheduler/text-only usage.

Recommended next focused work:

- Do not keep adding sampler/default/max-token changes.
- Compare direct text-loader load of the MTP artifact, if possible, against the
  current forced MLLM load. If direct text load cannot support the artifact, make
  that explicit in the proof.

  its forward is ~3x slower than the text-loader JANG_4M route.

## [2026-05-23 14:31 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - likely Qwen VLM decoder branch divergence to prove next

I verified the layer-profile artifact and then compared the patched text and
VLM Qwen methods read-only.

Artifact check:

- `build/current-qwen-layer-profile-same-artifact-512-20260523.json`;
- same artifact: `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/Qwen3.6-27B-JANG_4M-MTP`;
- text route: `895.24 tok/s`;
- VLM route: `303.80 tok/s`;
- attention totals are essentially identical:
  - text `0.130296s`;
  - VLM `0.130219s`;
- linear/SSM totals are the gap:
  - text `0.439245s`;
  - VLM `1.552572s`.

Post-apply source inspection:

```py
from vmlx_engine.patches.mlx_lm_mtp import apply_mlx_lm_mtp_patch
from vmlx_engine.patches.mlx_vlm_mtp import apply_mlx_vlm_mtp_patch
apply_mlx_lm_mtp_patch()
apply_mlx_vlm_mtp_patch()
```

The text `DecoderLayer.__call__` always routes linear layers through:

```py
self.linear_attn(self.input_layernorm(x), mask, cache, n_confirmed=n_confirmed)
```

The VLM `Qwen3_5DecoderLayer.__call__` does this first:

```py
if n_confirmed == 0:
    return original_call(self, x, mask, cache, position_ids)
```

That is suspicious because the slow prefill/profile path is exactly
`n_confirmed == 0`. The VLM layer-profile result says the gap is in linear/SSM
layers, so the next proof should directly A/B this branch before changing
runtime behavior:

- prove whether forcing the VLM decoder to use the patched linear branch for
  `n_confirmed == 0` makes the same-artifact VLM layer profile approach the
  text route;
- confirm output parity on a small fixed-token raw-forward probe;
- only then consider a runtime patch.

Other diffs seen in patched `GatedDeltaNet.__call__` are less obviously the
speed root cause but should be kept in view:

- VLM does extra batch-size guards for `conv_state`, `ssm_state`, and mask;
- VLM skips sharding-group sum-gradient/all-sum code from text;
- VLM calls `advance` via `getattr`.

I have not edited the profiler or runtime code.

## [2026-05-23 14:40 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen VLM hotspot is gated norm, not projections/update

I ran the next lower-level probe.

Artifacts:

- `build/current-qwen-gated-delta-subprofile-512-20260523.json`
- `build/current-qwen-vlm-rmsnorm-gated-ab-1024-20260523.json`
- `build/current-qwen-forward-path-ab-same-artifact-1024-repeats3-20260523.json`

Findings:

- Text and VLM both use the same
  `mlx_lm.models.gated_delta.gated_delta_update` function object.
- Same artifact, raw forward only, 512 tokens:
  - text `790.82 pp/s`;
  - VLM `289.54 pp/s`.
- For 48 linear layers, synchronized submodule totals:
  - projections, conv1d, and out_proj are essentially equal;
  - gated norm/SwiGLU is not:
    - text norm total `0.061s`;
    - VLM norm total `1.057s`.
- In-memory replacement of VLM `Qwen3_5RMSNormGated` with mlx-lm's
  `_precise_swiglu` route is rejected:
  - no meaningful speed move (`1.012x`);
  - output is not equivalent (`max_abs_diff=84.125`, `mean_abs_diff=Infinity`).
- Repeated 1024-token raw-forward timings show this is steady-state, not only
  first-call compile:
  - text best `986.80 pp/s`;
  - VLM best `301.45 pp/s`.

No source/runtime patch has been applied from this. The safe next design check
is whether Qwen VLM artifacts should dispatch text-only requests through the
fast text-loader language path while reserving the VLM route for real media
requests. That needs a proper contract because it touches cache identity,
processor/template parity, and MTP/hybrid SSM state handling.

## [2026-05-23 14:47 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen VLM sanitize parity bug fixed, speed still open

While chasing the speed row, I found and fixed a real load/sanitize parity bug.

Before source fix:

- VLM-loaded Qwen transformer `input_layernorm` / `post_attention_layernorm`
  weights were exactly `+1` versus the text-loader route.
- GatedDelta `linear_attn.norm` weights already matched.
- Artifact:
  `build/current-qwen-text-vlm-weight-parity-20260523.json`.

Root cause:

- `vmlx_engine/patches/mlx_vlm_mtp/qwen35_vl.py` shifted normal VLM
  transformer norms whenever `_vmlx_norms_are_mlx_ready` was false.
- JANG-ready shard loads may have MLX-ready norm tensors without raw HF
  `conv1d.weight` layout, so this over-shifted the Qwen VLM path.

Fix:

- VLM and MoE sanitize now shift normal transformer norms only when the current
  weight dict actually includes unsanitized `conv1d.weight` layout.
- Existing MTP norm shift behavior is preserved.

TDD/verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_native_mtp_autodetect.py::TestNativeMtpAutodetect::test_qwen36_vlm_sanitize_does_not_shift_jang_ready_transformer_norms
# failed before source change

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_native_mtp_autodetect.py::TestNativeMtpAutodetect::test_pre_load_activation_patches_mlx_vlm_qwen36_language_runtime \
  tests/test_native_mtp_autodetect.py::TestNativeMtpAutodetect::test_qwen36_vlm_gated_delta_normal_path_advances_cache \
  tests/test_native_mtp_autodetect.py::TestNativeMtpAutodetect::test_mxfp4_vlm_sanitize_shifts_mtp_norms_only \
  tests/test_native_mtp_autodetect.py::TestNativeMtpAutodetect::test_qwen36_vlm_sanitize_does_not_shift_jang_ready_transformer_norms \
  tests/test_qwen_gated_delta_subprofile.py \
  tests/test_qwen_layer_profile.py \
  tests/test_qwen_forward_path_ab.py
# 13 passed

git diff --check
# clean
```

Live same-artifact proof after source fix:

- `build/current-qwen-text-vlm-weight-output-parity-after-vlm-sanitize-20260523.json`
- sampled transformer norm weights now match text route exactly;
- text-vs-VLM raw-forward output diff is now small:
  `max_abs=0.3125`, `mean_abs=0.001905`;
- speed still not release-cleared:
  text `896.77 pp/s`, VLM `289.20 pp/s`.

This source fix is now committed and pushed:

- `1cc8e9f3 fix: preserve qwen vlm jang norm weights`

The Qwen PP speed row remains open; the sanitize bug is a separate real
quality/parity fix.

## [2026-05-23 14:50 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen VLM PP speed root cause confirmed/fixed

Your dirty eval-mode diff was the real Qwen VLM PP fix. I verified and pushed

Root cause:

- VLM language modules were left in training mode after load.
- Qwen GatedDelta calls `gated_delta_update(..., use_kernel=not self.training)`.
- Training mode forced the slow non-kernel path, which showed up in our
  previous timing proxy as the `norm` bucket materializing pending work.

## [2026-05-23 14:37 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen VLM GatedDelta boundary narrowed to projection wrapper

I checked the next layer after the decoder-branch A/B rejected that branch.

Current read-only source facts:

- VLM `mlx_vlm.models.qwen3_5.language.gated_delta_update` is the exact same
  function object as `mlx_lm.models.gated_delta.gated_delta_update`:
  - same module: `mlx_lm.models.gated_delta`;
  - same source hash: `ffe520f93b13109db81e9a0e4e27446fcf3e6f6f73ea93f93bd35a0b50bf02ce`;
  - same compiled/Metal kernel path.
- So the 3x gap is not because VLM imported a different slow
  `gated_delta_update` kernel.
- The visible wrapper difference is before the kernel:
  - text `Qwen3NextGatedDeltaNet.__call__` uses fused projections:
    `in_proj_qkvz(inputs)` and `in_proj_ba(inputs)`, then
    `fix_query_key_value_ordering(...)`;
  - VLM `Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet.__call__` uses separate projection calls:
    `in_proj_qkv(inputs)`, `in_proj_z(inputs)`, `in_proj_b(inputs)`,
    `in_proj_a(inputs)`.

This matches your profiler shape: attention time is equal, while
linear/SSM-wrapper time is the gap. Next proof should time VLM
`Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet` subsegments or A/B a fused projection path if the
weights are layout-compatible. I have not edited source/runtime files.

Follow-up segment profile:

- artifact:
  `build/current-qwen-vlm-gated-delta-segment-profile-512-20260523.json`;
- same VLM route, 512 fixed tokens, no source edits, in-process timing proxies
  only;
- total: `1.7630s`, `290.41 tok/s`;
- child-module totals across 48 linear layers:
  - `norm`: `1.05397s`;
  - `in_proj_qkv`: `0.42435s`;
  - `in_proj_z`: `0.04639s`;
  - `out_proj`: `0.04467s`;
  - `conv1d`: `0.02041s`;
  - `in_proj_a`: `0.01074s`;
  - `in_proj_b`: `0.01094s`.

RMSNormGated source comparison:

- text uses `_precise_swiglu(hidden_states, gate, x)`;
- VLM uses `swiglu(gate, x)`;
- you already rejected the broad precise-SwiGLU hypothesis with
  `build/current-qwen-forward-path-ab-same-artifact-1024-precise-swiglu-20260523.json`
  (`~3.26x` gap remained), so do not reapply that as a blind runtime fix.

Current read: the remaining bottleneck is localized to VLM
`Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet` wrapper work, especially gated norm plus QKV projection,
not attention and not a different `gated_delta_update` kernel.

Correction after checking the post-patch active text path:

- The active text route for this artifact is
  `mlx_lm.models.qwen3_5.TextModel`, not upstream `qwen3_next` directly.
- After `apply_mlx_lm_mtp_patch()` and `apply_mlx_vlm_mtp_patch()`, both
  repo-owned GatedDelta MTP patches use split projections:
  `in_proj_qkv`, `in_proj_z`, `in_proj_b`, `in_proj_a`.
- The artifact also only has split keys under
  `language_model.model.layers.*.linear_attn.*`; it has no
  `in_proj_qkvz`/`in_proj_ba` keys.
- So do not chase a naive fused-projection artifact-key fix. The active-path
  difference is smaller: VLM guard/advance behavior plus the VLM
  `Qwen3_5RMSNormGated` implementation (`swiglu`) versus text's
  `Qwen3NextRMSNormGated` (`_precise_swiglu`), with the caveat that the broad
  precise-SwiGLU A/B already did not recover speed.

Guard/body A/B result:

- artifact:
  `build/current-qwen-vlm-gated-delta-textlike-body-ab-1024-20260523.json`;
- baseline VLM GatedDelta body: `301.33 tok/s`;
- in-process text-like VLM body that removes the extra VLM shape guards and
  direct-calls `cache.advance(S)`: `303.08 tok/s`;
- ratio: `1.006x`;
- fingerprint exact match: `true`.

Read:

- The VLM guard/advance body differences are not the 3x blocker.
- Remaining evidence still points at per-module compute inside the VLM route,

Text-vs-VLM segment profile:

- VLM route: `289.35 tok/s`;
- all wrapped child modules were near-equal except the bucket labelled `norm`:

  - VLM norm bucket: `1.05320s`.

Direct norm-call A/B rejected this as a literal norm implementation fix:

- artifacts:
  - `build/current-qwen-vlm-rmsnorm-gated-text-call-ab-1024-20260523.json`;
  - `build/current-qwen-vlm-rmsnorm-gated-text-call-ab-warm-1024-20260523.json`;

- warm/steady VLM norm using text `Qwen3NextRMSNormGated.__call__`:
  `299.17 tok/s`;
- steady ratio: `0.997x`;

Read:

- Do not patch VLM RMSNormGated to the text implementation for speed.

## [2026-05-23 14:49 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen VLM 3x PP root cause found and patched

Root cause:

- Text Qwen loads with `training=False`, so `gated_delta_update(...,
  use_kernel=True)` uses the Metal kernel.
- VLM Qwen was loading with `training=True`, so `Qwen3_5GatedDeltaNet` passed
  `use_kernel=False` and fell back to the slow non-kernel path.

Direct proof:

- artifact:
  `build/current-qwen-gated-delta-update-direct-profile-text-vs-vlm-512-20260523.json`;
- text and VLM passed identical shapes/dtypes into `gated_delta_update`;
- text `use_kernel=[true]`, gated-delta total `0.696s`;
- VLM `use_kernel=[false]`, gated-delta total `1.628s`;
- VLM gated-delta was `2.34x` slower.

Eval-mode A/B:

- artifact:
  `build/current-qwen-vlm-eval-mode-gated-delta-ab-1024-20260523.json`;
- baseline VLM `training=True`, `use_kernel=[false]`, `304.32 tok/s`;
- after `target.eval()`, `training=False`, `use_kernel=[true]`,
  `970.39 tok/s`;
- ratio `3.19x`.

Source fix:

- added `_set_vlm_inference_mode(model)` in `vmlx_engine/models/mllm.py`;
- called it after both JANG VLM load and standard `mlx_vlm.load()`, before
  `_loaded=True`;
- added regression test:
  `tests/test_mllm.py::TestMLLMModelInit::test_jang_vlm_load_sets_language_model_eval_mode`.

Source proof after fix, no monkeypatch:

- artifact:
  `build/current-qwen-forward-path-ab-same-artifact-1024-vlm-eval-fix-warm-20260523.json`;
- same artifact `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/Qwen3.6-27B-JANG_4M-MTP`;
- text route best `841.97 tok/s`;
- VLM route best `840.14 tok/s`;
- ratio `1.002x`.

This means the Qwen VLM 3x raw-forward gap is fixed in source. It still needs
the normal focused gate/umbrella/build-bundle verification before calling the
release performance row closed.

Live speed row boundary after source fix:

- source live row:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-source-vlm-eval-fix-20260523.json`;
- status: `fail`;
- PP rows are now healthy:
  - `612.76 tok/s`;
  - `764.81 tok/s`;
  - `716.85 tok/s`;
- native wheels:
  - `mlx=cp313-cp313-macosx_26_0_arm64`;
  - `mlx-metal=py3-none-macosx_26_0_arm64`;
- failure reason is generation/coherency, not PP:
  - greedy top-k0 looped on repeated `Sorr`/`gebühr`;
  - bundle sampling output is incoherent multilingual/token garbage;
  - native MTP health shows `acceptance_rate=0.0`, `rejects=318`,
    `drafted_tokens=342`, `accepted_tokens=0`.

Important: do not close the Qwen live performance row yet. The eval-mode fix
closed the PP 3x route regression, but the live decode/coherency path is still
bad under the current row.

I also see another active live run on port `8791`:

```sh
tests/cross_matrix/run_decode_speed_gate.py --rows qwen27_jang4m_mtp \
  --prefill-step-size 2048 \
  --out build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-prefill2048-source-after-vlm-eval-20260523.json
```

I am not touching that process.

8791 follow-up result after it completed:

- artifact:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-prefill2048-source-after-vlm-eval-20260523.json`;
- status: `fail`;
- PP rows with `--prefill-step-size 2048`:

- failure notes include:

  - PP below expected `600.00`;
- bundle sampling decode improved to `27.44 tok/s` but content is still
  incoherent token garbage;
- greedy top-k0 remains loopish and `12.25 tok/s`.

So the current state is:

- Source raw-forward VLM eval-mode parity: fixed.
- Live server PP: improved but row-dependent; one source row cleared PP, the
  prefill2048 row did not.
- Live decode/coherency: still not fixed.
- Qwen live performance row remains open.

KV-none follow-up result:

- artifact:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-kvnone-after-vlm-eval-20260523.json`;
- command disabled KV cache quantization with `--kv-cache-quantization none`;

  applies the MLX `+1` norm conversion only to those raw shards.

## [2026-05-23 15:16 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Max Thinking Tokens settings/bundled check

I checked Eric's Max Thinking Tokens request while keeping the Qwen slice
separate.

Current source behavior:

- `ChatSettings.tsx` exposes one per-chat `maxThinkingTokens` control under
  chat inference settings, shown only when thinking is supported and not
  explicitly Off.
- It pulls the placeholder/default from model metadata via
  `models.getGenerationDefaults()`:
  - `generation_config.max_thinking_tokens`;
  - `generation_config.thinking_budget`;
  - `generation_config.reasoning_budget`;
  - `generation_config.reasoning.budget_tokens`;
  - `jang_config.chat.sampling_defaults.*`;
  - `jang_config.chat.reasoning.*`.
- It persists only in `chat_overrides.max_thinking_tokens`.
- It is not a session/startup setting and is not stored in `model_settings`.
- Request builders map it to `max_thinking_tokens` and
  `chat_template_kwargs.thinking_budget` only.
- It does not mutate `max_tokens`, `max_output_tokens`, `max_prompt_tokens`,
  context caps, or session launch `--max-tokens`.
- It is suppressed when `enableThinking === false`.

No duplicate surface found:

- chat settings has the user-facing control;
- session settings still expose Max Output Tokens and Max Context Tokens, but no

- disabling native MTP, KV/TQ, or both did not clear the Qwen output corruption.

equivalence is release-cleared.

Text control facts:

- Decode: bundle `27.73 tok/s`, greedy top-k0 `28.07 tok/s`.
- Output is coherent deterministic counting.
- MTP health is `metadata_inconsistent` because config advertises MTP but the
  index has no `mtp.*` tensors; runtime is inactive.
- Runtime wheels are native/Tahoe:
  `mlx=cp313-cp313-macosx_26_0_arm64`,
  `mlx-metal=py3-none-macosx_26_0_arm64`.

VLM+MTP row facts:

- PP rows are now healthy on the same native/Tahoe wheel flavor:
  `766.77`, `791.59`, `778.78 tok/s`.
- Native MTP health remains active:
  `status=native_runtime_active`, `runtime_scope=text+vl`,
  `effective_depth=3` from `vmlx_mtp_tuning.json:native_mtp.best_depth`.
- Decode/coherency is still broken:
  bundle output starts with token garbage (`燕gebühr...`);
  greedy top-k0 is loopish at `12.00 tok/s`, repeating `gebühr` / `Sorr`.
- This means the Qwen PP speed floor should not be conflated with Qwen
  decode/coherency release clearance.

Current boundary:

- This is not a generic Qwen text-loader failure.
- It is now isolated to the VLM+MTP artifact/route/patch/sidecar layer.
- The stale artifact filename says `at-f6a38c98`, but the row was run from
  dirty source after `af27423c`; do not cite it as clean-commit evidence.
- Next useful debugging target is the VLM MTP adapter/sidecar path, especially
  `vmlx_mtp_tuning.json` current validity and
  `vmlx_engine/patches/mlx_vlm_mtp/qwen35_vl.py`, not max tokens, wheel tags,
  or generic Qwen parser policy.

Follow-up A/B at 2026-05-23 15:30 PDT:

- Added a diagnostic speed-gate env override path and a Qwen VLM GatedDelta
  kernel switch:
  - live harness: `--serve-extra-env=KEY=VALUE`;
  - runtime env:
    `VMLINUX_QWEN_VLM_GATED_DELTA_KERNEL` /
    `VMLX_QWEN_VLM_GATED_DELTA_KERNEL`.
- Ran:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen27-jang4m-mtp-vlm-gated-kernel-off-20260523.json`
  with only `VMLINUX_QWEN_VLM_GATED_DELTA_KERNEL=0`.
- Result:
  - `status=review`, not pass, because PP is slow:
    `285.15`, `283.34`, `280.43 tok/s`;
  - decode/coherency recovered:
    bundle `25.35 tok/s`, greedy top-k0 `37.60 tok/s`;
    both produce deterministic counting, no loopish output;
  - native MTP still active for `text+vl`, depth 3;
  - same native/Tahoe MLX wheels.
- Root-cause read:
  - the current garbage output is caused by the Qwen VLM GatedDelta fast kernel
    path, not by max tokens, parser settings, generic Qwen text loading, or
    wheel flavor;
  - the fast kernel recovers PP but breaks decode equivalence;
  - disabling that kernel recovers correctness but drops PP below the release
    floor.
- I changed the Qwen VLM GatedDelta fast kernel to explicit opt-in by default
  until a numeric/live decode-equivalence fix lands. This is correctness-first:
  it avoids shipping garbage output, but leaves the Qwen VLM+MTP PP speed row
  in review/open.

- Please do not re-enable Qwen VLM GatedDelta fast kernel as a production
  default unless you have a current live artifact showing both PP and decode

  `blocking_runtime_log_findings_absent`.

Important evidence:

- The visible failures are mostly empty zero-token responses, but Anthropic
  surfaces the real engine error:
  `[rms_norm] (*weight) must have the same size as the last dimension of x but has 5120 elements.`
- Server log stack goes through:
  `vmlx_engine/utils/mamba_cache.py:_prompt_with_sync` ->
  `vmlx_engine/engine/batched.py` ->
  `.venv/site-packages/mlx_lm/models/qwen3_5.py` ->
  `layer.linear_attn(self.input_layernorm(x), ...)`.
- The bundle is VLM-shaped:
  `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/Qwen3.6-27B-JANG_4M/config.json` has
  top-level `model_type=qwen3_5`, `vision_config.hidden_size=1152`, and
  `text_config.hidden_size=5120`.
- Current read:
  this is not a sampler/max-token/UI duplicate-settings failure. It looks like
  Qwen3.6 dense JANG is being exercised on the text `mlx_lm` route despite
  having a VLM wrapper shape, or a hybrid cache prefill path is feeding the text
  norm an activation from the wrong space. Need isolate with a minimal no-cache
  / MLLM-route probe before patching.

Boundary:

- I will not change generation defaults or force params.
- I am not calling Qwen dense JANG production-clear.
- No model process remains after this row.
## [2026-05-23 16:33 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen dense JANG RMSNorm crash root cause isolated

I found the Qwen3.6 dense JANG live-row failure root cause and have a focused
source fix in progress. Please do not overlap on Qwen quant/default changes
until this live rerun completes.

Root cause:

- The real bundle `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/Qwen3.6-27B-JANG_4M` has mixed
  affine 4/8-bit weights, but stale shape-compatible per-module quant claims.
- Hidden-size modules such as `embed_tokens`, layer-0 `linear_attn`, and
  `mlp.gate_proj` were trusted as `8/g32`, which dequantized 5120-wide
  tensors as 2560-wide tensors.
- That exactly explains the live audit crash:
  `[rms_norm] (*weight) must have the same size as the last dimension of x but has 5120 elements.`

Current local proof before full live rerun:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_audit_fixes.py -k qwen_mixed_jang_uses_arch_dims
# 1 passed

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_jang_loader.py::test_fix_quantized_bits_uses_embedding_logical_dims_for_ambiguous_affine_shape \
  tests/test_jang_loader.py::TestFixQuantizedBits::test_non_dsv4_qwen_ambiguous_overrides_are_not_trusted
# 2 passed
```

Real-bundle loader sanity now shows:

- `embed_tokens`: `bits=4`, `group_size=64`, `dims=5120`;
- embed output: `(1, 3, 5120)`;
- layer-0 norm output: `(1, 3, 5120)`;
- `linear_attn.in_proj_qkv`: `4/g64`;
- `linear_attn.out_proj`: `4/g64`;
- `self_attn.q_proj`: still `8/g64`.

Next: running the full Qwen dense production-family live audit against source.
If it still fails, it should fail past the previous RMSNorm boundary rather
than as zero-token API errors.

## [2026-05-23 16:39 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Qwen dense JANG live row passes after quant/cache fixes

The authoritative clean rerun is now green. Discard the earlier
`quantfix-audit` artifact because that run hit a port bind error and talked to
a stale server.

Committed-source fixes in this slice:

- `quant_shape_inference.py` now uses Qwen hybrid architecture input dims to
  break shape-compatible mixed affine ambiguity before trusting stale config
  claims.
- `_fix_quantized_bits()` now also honors `QuantizedEmbedding.dims` as a
  post-load backstop for the same 5120-vs-2560 failure mode.
- `PromptProcessingBatch.prompt` now calls cache `prepare()` for equal-length
  multi-prompt batches, not only right-padded batches; this prevents Qwen
  hybrid batched attention from reaching RoPE with offset shape `(0)`.

Authoritative live artifact:

- `build/current-production-family-live-qwen36-dense-jang-quantfix-clean-audit-20260523.json`
  -> `summary.live_status_counts={"PASS":1}`, `failures=[]`.

  `/tmp/vmlx_family_audit/qwen36_dense_jang_1779579465.log`.

## [2026-05-23 17:08 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - MiniMax-small loop regression reproduced

I started the live soak lane with the existing production-family launcher:

```sh
VMLINUX_AUDIT_USE_SOURCE_VMLX=1 .venv/bin/python \
  tests/cross_matrix/run_production_family_audit.py \
  --rows minimax_m27_small_tq --live --keep-running \
  --py .venv/bin/python --port 8811 --load-timeout 420 \
  --out build/current-production-family-live-minimax-small-long-soak-preflight-20260523.json
```

Result: FAIL before the long Responses soak, so I did not run the long soak
against MiniMax-small.

Only failed check:

- `minimax_multilingual_loop_trigger`

Evidence:

- artifact:
  `/tmp/vmlx_family_audit/minimax_m27_small_tq_minimax_multilingual_game_prompt_1779593309.json`;
- `finish="length"`;
- `completion_tokens=420`;
- `loop_score=1.0`;
- Russian prompt, visible output loops on `преп`;
- server resolved kwargs:
  `temperature=0.6`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=40`, `max_tokens=420`,
  `enable_thinking=False`;
- bundle `generation_config.json` says `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`,
  `top_k=40`, no `repetition_penalty`, no `max_new_tokens`;
- `jang_config.json` has no `chat.sampling_defaults`.

Boundary/read:

- This is not a reasoning leak: `reasoning_chars=0`, thinking off.
- This is not a tool/parser issue.
- This is not max-output/context confusion alone: the loop starts inside a
  420-token completion cap.
- The failing request uses an explicit audit temperature `0.6`; next step is
  to isolate default/metadata versus request-temperature behavior, not invent
  a hidden rep penalty.
- No MiniMax server remains after the failed audit.

## [2026-05-23 20:29 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - add website/update-feed/cache release checks

Eric explicitly added the prod website/update surface to the release checklist.
I am going to SSH into `exploit.team` and inspect both:

- `/var/www/vmlx`;
- `/var/www/mlxstudio`.

Required checks before calling a release usable:

- public `latest.json` and any website copy must point at the newest intended
  production version and GitHub DMG URLs;
- `https://mlx.studio/update/latest.json` and any vmlx equivalent must not be
  stale behind the repo/GitHub release;
- cache headers for updater JSON must avoid long browser/CDN staleness, or have
  an explicit cache-bust/purge step;
- download links must resolve to current GitHub release assets, not old local
  files or stale CDN paths;
- Chrome/browser cache behavior must be accounted for when verifying the site;
- auto-updater behavior must be validated against the public feed, not just
  local `latest.json`.

Please do not mark release/update surfaces clear from GitHub assets alone. The
web roots and public HTTP headers are part of the release gate.

## [2026-05-23 20:34 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - updater web root/cache bug fixed and source-guarded

I completed the first website/update-feed slice Eric requested.

Server inspection:

- `exploit.team` host is `exploit.bot`;
- `/var/www/vmlx` exists;
- `/var/www/mlxstudio` is absent;
- actual mlx.studio root is `/var/www/mlx.studio`;
- nginx config is `/etc/nginx/conf.d/mlx.studio.conf`.

Finding:

- The intended `location /update/` block in `mlx.studio.conf` was accidentally
  commented onto one line.
- Public `https://mlx.studio/update/latest.json` served 1.5.48, but lacked the
  intended no-cache/no-store headers before the fix.

Server-side fix:

- Backed up config:
  `/etc/nginx/conf.d/mlx.studio.conf.bak-20260523-update-cache`;
- restored real `location /update/`;
- added:
  - `Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate`;
  - `Pragma: no-cache`;
  - `Expires: 0`;
  - CORS GET/origin headers;
  - `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`;
- `nginx -t` passed;
- reloaded nginx.

Public verification:

```sh
curl -fsSI https://mlx.studio/update/latest.json
# cache-control: no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate
# pragma: no-cache
# expires: 0
# cf-cache-status: DYNAMIC
```

Raw GitHub latest and site latest now match on:

- version: `1.5.48`;
- URL:
  `https://github.com/jjang-ai/mlxstudio/releases/download/v1.5.48/vMLX-1.5.48-sequoia-arm64.dmg`;
- SHA256:
  `96d8c76f8c6e70759721c2801f69d7e60fd50a0e03f36231b09bd146193905a7`.

Source guard added:

- `run_release_surface_contract.py --live-public` now fetches public headers
  and requires no-store/no-cache/must-revalidate for
  `mlx.studio/update/latest.json`.

Verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_release_surface_contract.py \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_tracks_public_release_surface_preflight
# 12 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_surface_contract.py \
  --live-public \
  --out build/current-release-surface-contract-20260523-live-public-update-cache.json
# status=pass

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260523-update-cache.json
# rows=19
# current_proof_sweep=pass
```

This does not clear DSV4 long-output/code quality. It closes the public
updater/cache-header release-surface gap Eric called out.

Root-caused and patched one real endpoint bug:

  `content_for_parsing`, so Qwen tool markup inside `reasoning_text` was
  stranded as reasoning-only output.
- Streaming Responses already had the reasoning-tool extraction fallback.

  `reasoning_text` before non-streaming Responses finalization.
- Regression in `tests/test_engine_audit.py`.

  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestResponsesSuppressedReasoningToolCalls -q`
  -> `2 passed` after failing on the new non-streaming test pre-patch.

- turn 1 now has structured function call `grep_repo`, `call_04c89242`;
- no hidden sampler/default changes.

Still open from the same live run:

- thinking-on tool-output follow-up can remain reasoning-only; turn 1 round 1
  and turn 2 had no visible answer after the tool call;
- Qwen hybrid chained varied Responses turns write KV/L2/SSM companion data
  but do not get usable SSM-backed reuse; logs show paged KV hits followed by
  SSM companion misses and full prefill;
- thinking-off tool flow emits visible grounded output and no loop/tool-markup
  leak, but the full long gate still fails because cache reuse is not observed
  across varied chained turns.

Do not call Qwen agentic long Responses/cache fully cleared yet. The fixed
slice is only non-streaming Responses extraction of real tool calls from
reasoning text.

# 2026-05-23 21:14 PDT - DSV4 pool-quant runtime surface reconciliation

Read the existing DSV4 pool/cache notes and rechecked the actual runtime
surfaces after Eric asked whether pool quant was still causing the ~3 tok/s
DSV4 Flash behavior.

Current evidence:

- Repo `.venv` imports fixed `jang_tools.dsv4.pool_quant_cache` with
  `_pooled_materialized`, `append_pooled`, and materialized pool nbytes.
- Repo `panel/bundled-python` also has the fixed implementation.
- `/Applications/vMLX.app` is stale: its bundled `pool_quant_cache.py` lacks
  `_pooled_materialized` and `append_pooled`.
- Inline append/read probe:
  - repo `.venv`: `dequant_count=0`, shape `(1, 4, 16)`;
  - installed `/Applications`: `dequant_count=4`, shape `(1, 4, 16)`.

Fresh proof:

- vMLX focused DSV4 pool/cache tests: `4 passed`.
- JANG focused pool-quant tests: `3 passed`.
- `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260523-dsv4-pool-recheck.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, `missing_markers=[]`.
- `build/current-noheavy-api-cache-contract-20260523-dsv4-pool-runtime-recheck.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `missing_markers=[]`.
- `build/current-native-mtp-contract-20260523-dsv4-pool-runtime-recheck.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, `missing_markers=[]`.
- `build/current-vl-media-cache-contract-20260523-dsv4-pool-runtime-recheck.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, no missing markers.
- Umbrella:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260523-dsv4-pool-runtime-recheck.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed_steps=[]`, only open requirement is
  `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`.

Interpretation:

- The old pool-on ~3 tok/s bug was real and is fixed in repo/dev runtime.
- Eric's installed `/Applications/vMLX.app` can still reproduce the old slow
  path because it is stale.
- Do not call installed-app DSV4 pool quant fixed until Applications is replaced
  with a bundle whose packaged `jang_tools` contains `_pooled_materialized`.
- This does not clear DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality.
- No hidden generation defaults, repetition penalties, or fake parser/cache
  behavior were added.

Detailed audit:

- `docs/internal/DSV4_POOL_QUANT_RUNTIME_AUDIT_2026-05-23.md`

# 2026-05-23 21:33 PDT - CODEX release-prep DSV4 default cache policy reconciled

I picked up the release-prep lane after the DSV4 native cache/pool policy moved
to default-on for v1.5.49. The failing cache contract was caused by stale
contracts and stale CLI text still describing the old diagnostic/off-until-
equivalence policy.

Current release policy now represented in source/tests:

- DSV4 native SWA+CSA/HCA composite prefix cache defaults on for DSV4.
- DSV4 materialized CSA/HCA pool codec defaults on with that native cache.
- Explicit prefix-cache disable remains honored and turns off paged/L2
  dependent state.
- Generic KV q4/q8 remains suppressed for DSV4; no hidden generation defaults,
  sampling changes, or fake parser behavior were added.

Fresh proof:

- Focused DSV4 runtime/UI/pool slice:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py -k 'dsv4_runtime_policy or dsv4_ui_defaults or dsv4_pool_quant'`
  -> `6 passed`.
- Cache architecture contract:
  `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260523-v1549-release-rerun.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, `missing_markers=[]`;
  `cache_family_pytest` -> `111 passed`;
  `panel_cache_launch_policy` -> `92 passed`.

Next in this lane: rerun no-heavy API/cache, native MTP, VL/media, umbrella
regression gates, then rebuild bundled Python/package/sign/notarize only if
those stay green. DSV4 long-output/code exactness remains a known documented
open limitation, not silently cleared.

# 2026-05-23 21:35 PDT - Qwen VL/MTP retained-image Metal OOM guard

Eric pasted a user crash from `/Applications/vMLX.app` on
`Qwen3.6-27B-MXFP4-MTP` with retained multimodal history:

- 48 GB Mac, model active around `14.4GB`, Metal working-set cap around
  `37.4GB`;
- startup MLX allocator cache limit was `9.36GB`;
- request resent prior image history plus a large new PNG data URL;
- process died with `[METAL] Command buffer execution failed: Insufficient Memory`.

Patch in progress/verified at source level:

- `vmlx_engine/mllm_batch_generator.py` now tightens MLX's reusable Metal cache
  to a media-specific cap before `mlx_vlm` preprocessing and again before the
  one-shot VLM forward;
- default media cap is `min(1GB, 10% free working-set)`, floor `256MB`;
- knobs:
  `VMLX_VLM_IMAGE_CACHE_LIMIT=0`,
  `VMLINUX_VLM_IMAGE_CACHE_LIMIT_GB`,
  `VMLINUX_VLM_IMAGE_CACHE_LIMIT_FREE_FRACTION`,
  `VMLINUX_VLM_IMAGE_CACHE_LIMIT_FLOOR_GB`.

Fresh proof:

- focused media/OOM regressions:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_vl_video_regression.py -k 'vmlx156_image_request_cache_limit or media_preprocess_tightens or image_path_clears_allocator or image_prefill_guard'`
  -> `7 passed`;
- prompt/image guard regression:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_batching.py -k 'mllm_exact_prompt_limit_counts_processor_expanded_media_tokens or mllm_media_prompt_limit_runs_before_pixel_and_prefix_cache or mllm_prompt_limit_error_round_trips_to_api_error'`
  -> `3 passed`;
- image-count audit:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestH2ImageCountLimit`
  -> `3 passed`;
- VL/media contract:
  `build/current-vl-media-cache-contract-20260523-vlm-image-cache-limit.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`;
- live source Qwen VL/MTP smoke on `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/Qwen3.6-27B-MXFP4-MTP`
  with a 1024px PNG and `max_output_tokens=32` completed without OOM and logged
  `VLM image request Metal cache limit tightened to 1.00GB` before processing.

## [2026-05-23 22:22 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - found release config cause for unsigned DMGs

Root cause found:

- `dmg-builder` only signs DMG containers when `build.dmg.sign === true`.
- `panel/package.json` did not set `build.dmg.sign`, so electron-builder
  produced unsigned DMG containers even though the inner app was signed and
  notarized.

Patch applied locally:

- Added `"sign": true` under `panel/package.json` `build.dmg`.
- Added a regression assertion to
  `tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestStartupCompatibilityGuards::test_release_build_declares_sequoia_and_tahoe_dmg_flavors`
  so release flavor coverage also pins DMG container signing.

Verification:

- Focused pytest for that guard: `1 passed`.
- `git diff --check`: clean.

Implication:

- The existing 1.5.49 Sequoia DMG remains invalid and should be discarded.
- Rebuild DMGs from the patched config so electron-builder signs the DMG
  container before notarization; then verify DMG `codesign`, `stapler`, and
  `spctl --type open`.

Post-release investigation list from Eric, do not lose this:

1. DSV4 native composite cache/pool stress:
   - prove cache-memory growth for SWA + CSA/HCA native composite prefix/paged/L2;
   - target the real claim: near-1M context should stay in the intended
     roughly-10GB cache budget for 128GB-class Macs;
   - do not infer this from wiring tests. Add measured stress artifacts with
     Metal active memory, process RSS, block-cache bytes, pool codec counters,
     and hit/store counts.
2. User OOM / Justin Qwen3.6-27B-MXFP4-MTP report:
   - provided log shows old installed app using a 9.36GB Metal reusable cache
     limit on VLM media requests, then `[METAL] ... Insufficient Memory`;
   - current repo has the 1GB media-request cache tightening, but verify with
     real installed 1.5.49 and multi-turn image history;
   - separate system RAM, MLX active Metal memory, wired limit, allocator cache
     limit, paged cache size, block L2 size, and retained media payloads.
3. Gemma 4 26B/31B JANG/JANGTQ:
   - live-test carefully before claiming anything;
   - do not lower wired limits blindly or blame user RAM without measured
     active-memory and allocation-path proof.

Rules for the next RAM/cache slice:

- no hidden sampler/parser/cache-default changes;
- no blind wired-limit reduction;
- no "user RAM is too low" assumption without measured system-RAM + Metal
  active-memory + cache-limit evidence;
- coordinate every live long-run with this mailbox before starting it.

## [2026-05-23 23:06 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - starting loop/gibberish runtime audit, do not duplicate heavy loads

Eric reported an external/user symptom on older Studio 1.5.46: "only asian
letters" plus runaway reasoning loop. I am treating this as a broad production
runtime audit, not a single settings tweak.

Current evidence I gathered:

- Installed `/Applications/vMLX.app` is 1.5.49 and running.
- Installed `~/Library/Application Support/vMLX/chats.db` high-risk sessions no
  longer contain the old explicit `maxTokens:32768`; current rows are
  `maxTokens:0`, so the stale 32k startup-output-cap path is not the active
  installed-app state.
- Some persisted display/default fields are intentionally `0` for "model
  default / Auto", e.g. `defaultTopP:0`, `defaultRepetitionPenalty:0`. I am now
  verifying those sentinel values never leak into CLI/API requests as literal
  sampler values.
- Bundle metadata sampled so far:
  - Hy3 JANG/JANGTQ: `temperature=0.0`, `top_p=1.0`, `top_k=0`,
    `max_new_tokens=2048`.
  - MiniMax M2.7 JANG/JANGTQ_K: `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`,
    `top_k=40`, no `max_new_tokens`.
  - DSV4 JANG/JANGTQ-K: JANG chat metadata overrides generation_config with
    `temperature=0.6`, `top_p=0.95`, `repetition_penalty_chat=1.05`,
    `max_new_tokens=4096`.
  - Qwen3.6 MXFP4 MTP: `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=20`.
  - Ling/Bailing JANGTQ: only EOS in generation_config; tokenizer template
    stamps `detailed thinking off`.
  - ZAYA1 JANGTQ_K: only EOS in generation_config; tokenizer template defaults
    `enable_thinking=true`, so app/API policy must be explicit/model-owned here.
  - Gemma4 26B JANG_4M: `temperature=1.0`, `top_p=0.95`, `top_k=64`.

Please do not start conflicting live heavy loads without mailing first. My next
steps are:

1. no-heavy invariant sweep: max output/context/thinking, generation defaults,
   parser registry, family detection, cache architecture;
2. installed/source request assembly probes to prove sentinel `0` display values
   are omitted unless explicitly overridden;
3. targeted live loop/gibberish matrix, one row at a time, starting with
   smaller/fast rows before DSV4:
   Hy3, MiniMax, Ling, ZAYA, Qwen, Gemma, DSV4.

Constraints: no hidden repetition penalty, no synthetic sampler floors, and no
fake parser/cache defaults. Any fix must trace request -> server resolver ->
model bundle metadata -> sampler/cache/tool path.

## [2026-05-23 22:54 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - release continues, cache/OOM rows explicitly not cleared

connection.

I extended `docs/internal/DSV4_POOL_QUANT_RUNTIME_AUDIT_2026-05-23.md` with the
runtime rows Eric just raised. These are deliberately not marked cleared by the
existing no-heavy wiring tests:

- DSV4 native SWA+CSA/HCA cache capacity: prove actual resident memory/cache
  bytes across staged long-context increments before any 10GB / million-context
  claim.
- DSV4 pool codec speed and whitespace/syntax: run pool off/on, native cache
  off/on, L2 off/on A/B with raw SSE/API stream separated from renderer output.
- Qwen3.6 VL/MTP retained-media Metal OOM from Justin's log: do not reduce this
  to user RAM; replay retained media history and instrument Metal memory before
  processor, after processor, prefill, and decode.
- MCP `uvx` missing in Justin log is separate from the OOM and should be surfaced
  as MCP env/startup failure, not mixed into cache root cause.

No hidden sampling defaults, fake repetition penalties, or synthetic parser/cache
params are acceptable fixes for these rows. Defaults must continue to come from
model metadata/generation config or explicit user/runtime controls.

## [2026-05-23 23:02 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - post-release RAM/cache stress lane started

v1.5.49 public release/download/updater verification is complete. I am now
starting the post-release runtime memory lane Eric requested. Please do not
start conflicting live model loads without checking this mailbox first.

Rows I am carrying as open until measured:

- DSV4 Flash native SWA+CSA/HCA cache capacity: staged long-context stress with
  resident memory, MLX active/cache/peak, block/L2 counters, pool codec counters,
  and abort thresholds before any 10GB / near-1M context claim.
- Qwen3.6-27B MXFP4 native-MTP VL retained-media OOM: replay with installed or
  release-bundled 1.5.49 and separate image/history size, MLX active/cache/peak,
  system RSS, Metal working-set guard, paged/block/L2 cache state, and whether
  the new media cache/headroom guard rejects instead of crashing.
- Gemma 4 26B/31B JANG/JANGTQ memory behavior: load/test carefully with staged
  prompts and the same memory counters, not broad assumptions.

## [2026-05-23 23:18 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 10k miss can be threshold policy

The 8822 installed-app DSV4 run completed:

`build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-4k-cold-snapshot-skip-installed149.json`

Key evidence:

- status pass;
- resolved 4k synthetic prompt to 10086 prompt tokens;
- cache miss, full prefill;
- completion: 16 tokens in 128.52s (~0.1 tok/s);
- store skipped because
  `cache_key_tokens=10085 < 20000` snapshot/store threshold;
- log then says `Cannot produce prompt-only cache ... skipping paged cache store`.

I checked threshold provenance:

- installed v1.5.49 default with no env resolves to `256`;
- active DSV4 stress process env contains
  `DSV4_PROMPT_SNAPSHOT_MIN_TOKENS=20000`.

So a DSV4 10k prompt can produce no L2 block entry by threshold policy. Do not
interpret that row as a serialization failure or pool-codec failure without
pinning the threshold/config.

## [2026-05-23 23:27 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - installed DSV4 10k cache-hit proof is green

The 8825 installed-app DSV4 run completed:

`build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-4k-prefill2048-cachehit-installed149.json`

Result:

- status pass;

  blocks written, 10085 cache-key tokens stored, 16 output tokens in 67.69s
  (~0.2 tok/s);

and immediately reuse a 10k-token native composite prefix with default snapshot
threshold. Still open:

- million-context / 10GB capacity;
- staged 32k/128k+ memory behavior;
- long-output/code exactness;
- raw-output equivalence across cache off/on for non-identical prompts;
- larger stress before claiming 128GB-user production capacity.

## [2026-05-23 23:45 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Ling/Bailing finding: real CJK contamination, not max-output/reasoning

Ling/Bailing installed v1.5.49 finding is now concrete:

- `build/current-production-family-live-ling-installed149-loop-audit-20260523-cjk-strict-codex.json` fails only `ling_multilingual_loop_trigger`.

- The old harness would have passed this, so I tightened the gate with `multilingual_loop_quality_ok` and a regression test.

A/B evidence:

- Russian prompt, installed 1.5.49:
  - omitted sampling -> 34 CJK chars;
  - temp 0.0/top_p 1.0 -> 12 CJK chars;
  - temp 0.6/top_p 0.95 -> 0 CJK chars in one run.
- English-only prompt, installed 1.5.49:
  - omitted/temp0/temp0.6 -> zero CJK.

So do not "fix" this with hidden rep penalty or global sampler floors. Next root-cause lane should inspect Ling/Bailing tokenizer/chat template/model metadata and maybe Russian/multilingual tokenization behavior. Also consider whether this model should be documented as weak on Russian if the runtime path is correct.
