## [2026-05-23 23:42 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - MX matmul speed depends on installed wheel flavor

Eric called out that MX matmul should be faster. Current evidence:

- `/Applications/vMLX.app` v1.5.49 is the Sequoia-compatible build:
  `mlx=cp312-cp312-macosx_14_0_arm64`,
  `mlx-metal=py3-none-macosx_14_0_arm64`.
- repo packaged Tahoe app has native wheels:
  `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app/.../python3`
  -> `mlx=cp312-cp312-macosx_26_0_arm64`,
  `mlx-metal=py3-none-macosx_26_0_arm64`.
- Existing Qwen/JANG text-loader speed artifacts still explain the split:
  source/native PP `910/915/779 tok/s`; packaged compat PP
  `295/288/249 tok/s`.
- `latest.json` and `https://mlx.studio/update/latest.json` both include
  `downloads.tahoe`, and `panel/src/main/update-manifest.ts` selects Tahoe for
  Electron `process.getSystemVersion()` major `26`.
- Focused updater test: `npm --prefix panel test -- --run tests/update-checker.test.ts`
  -> `29 passed`.

Read:

- If a Tahoe/M5 user installs from the top-level manifest `url`, they get the
  compat/Sequoia DMG and slower MX matmul.
- App updater selection should route Tahoe users to the native DMG, but website
  download surfaces still need to be checked so the default manual download
  does not accidentally steer M5/Tahoe users to the compat build.

  registry lookup correctly overrides Ling to plain content:
  `supports_thinking=False`, `reasoning_parser=None`, `think_in_template=False`,
  `tool_parser=deepseek`, `cache_type=hybrid`.
- Direct tokenizer render of the failing Russian prompt contains no CJK and
  tokenizes to 120 prompt tokens with no CJK-decoding prompt tokens.

So the Ling CJK row is not explained by prompt-template CJK injection or a
reasoning-parser leak. Next investigation should compare model/artifact
multilingual behavior vs decode/runtime behavior. Still no fake global sampler
or repetition-penalty fix.

## [2026-05-24 00:02 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Ling repeat probe proves intermittent mixed-language contamination

I ran a controlled installed-app Ling repeat probe on one server with eight identical Russian prompts:

- artifact: `build/current-ling-installed149-russian-repeat-cjk-20260523-codex.json`
- every run had CJK chars (`提前` on run 1; `躲躲` on runs 2-8);
- runs 2-8 also included unrelated Latin/German `Himmel`;
- cache then made the contaminated output stable: `cache_hit_requests=7`, `cache_hit_tokens=798`, `cache_detail=paged+ssm`.

This explains why a single production-family live row can sometimes pass: the issue is intermittent by generation sample, but reproducible over repeated same-prompt turns.

I tightened the audit gate further:

- `multilingual_loop_quality_ok` now rejects CJK and unrelated Latin tokens;
- it still allows expected web terms: `HTML`, `Three`, `js`, `JavaScript`, `CSS`, `WebGL`;
- red/green was verified: the new `Himmel` regression failed before the gate change and passes after.

Fresh no-heavy verification:

- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_cross_matrix_audit_runner.py tests/test_cache_architecture_contract.py tests/test_engine_audit.py` -> `493 passed`;
- `git diff --check` clean.

No runtime sampler/default fix was added. This is audit visibility only.

## [2026-05-24 00:11 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Ling no-cache isolation proves cache is not root cause

The installed-app Ling/Bailing no-cache repeat probe completed:

- artifact: `build/current-ling-installed149-russian-repeat-nocache-20260524-codex.json`
- runtime: installed `/Applications/vMLX.app` bundled Python
- model: `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/Ling-2.6-flash-JANGTQ`
- cache mode: prefix cache off, paged cache off, block L2 off
- prompt: same Russian HTML/Three.js game plan prompt
- params: `temperature=0.6`, `top_p=0.95`, `enable_thinking=false`, `max_tokens=420`

Result:

- 6 uncached runs total
- 2/6 runs contained CJK
- 3/6 runs contained unrelated Latin fragments
- examples: `碰撞检测`, `计分`, `клавиaturы`, `sprints`, `spawn`
- all responses stopped normally; no reasoning content; no output-cap runaway

Read:

- Prefix/paged/L2 cache is not the root cause of the first bad Ling continuation. It can preserve/stabilize bad output on repeated identical prompts, but the uncached decode path can generate CJK/mixed-language output by itself.
- This keeps the root cause narrowed away from UI rendering, max output, reasoning parser, prompt-template injection, and cache poisoning.
- Next Ling step should be reference-path comparison or deeper tokenizer/decode/model-artifact analysis. Do not add hidden rep penalty, temperature/top-p, or fake sampler floors.

Docs updated:

- `docs/internal/RUNTIME_LOOP_GIBBERISH_AUDIT_2026-05-23.md`
- `docs/internal/V1_5_49_RUNTIME_OPEN_ISSUES_2026-05-23.md`
- `docs/internal/V1_5_49_POST_RELEASE_RUNTIME_CHECKPOINT_2026-05-23.md`

## [2026-05-24 00:48 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 edge capacity proof and guard boundary

DSV4 cache/RAM probe results:

1. The first 60k-ish attempt was not valid capacity proof:

```sh
.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_runtime_memory_stress_probe.py \
  --row dsv4_jangtq_k \
  --python panel/bundled-python/python/bin/python3 \
  --port 8842 \
  --prompt-tokens 24576,24576 \
  --max-tokens 16 \
  --abort-rss-gb 118 \
  --abort-metal-active-gb 118 \
  --out build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-24k-cachehit-bundled-d503ea7f-guardfail-20260524.json
# status=fail
```

- Both requests were HTTP `400`;
- server rejected `60493` prompt tokens because the production guard max safe
  prefill is `32768`;
- important harness fix: `run_runtime_memory_stress_probe.py` now fails the
  overall artifact on `http_error` stages instead of silently writing
  `status=pass`.

2. Clean fresh-L2 edge proof under the current guard passed:

```sh
rm -rf /tmp/vmlx-dsv4-edge-13k-20260524
.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_runtime_memory_stress_probe.py \
  --row dsv4_jangtq_k \
  --python panel/bundled-python/python/bin/python3 \
  --port 8842 \
  --prompt-tokens 13000,13000 \
  --max-tokens 16 \
  --abort-rss-gb 118 \
  --abort-metal-active-gb 118 \
  --serve-extra-arg=--block-disk-cache-dir \
  --serve-extra-arg=/tmp/vmlx-dsv4-edge-13k-20260524 \
  --out build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-13k-edge-cachehit-bundled-d503ea7f-freshl2-20260524.json
# status=pass
```

Fresh-L2 evidence:

- before L2: `blocks_on_disk=0`, `disk_size_gb=0.0`;
- first request: `32005` prompt tokens, `cached_tokens=0`,
  `245.985s`, prompt wall `130.110 tok/s`;
- after first: `allocated_blocks=127`, `total_tokens_cached=32004`,
  `block_size=256`, `max_blocks=1000`, `disk_writes=126`,
  L2 disk `0.437 GB`;
- memory after first: process RSS `83969.0 MB`, Metal active `83065.1 MB`,
  peak `97950.8 MB`, MLX cache `3334.9 MB`;
- replay request: `cached_tokens=32004`, `cache_detail=paged+dsv4`,
  `1.152s`, prompt wall `27782.118 tok/s`, uncached prompt wall `0.868 tok/s`;
- native cache is `deepseek_v4_v7` composite with SWA local + CSA/HCA
  compressed pools, pool quant enabled, and generic TurboQuant KV forced off.

Read:

- Current source/bundled DSV4 default is good up to the production guard edge
  (~32k prompt tokens) with clean fresh L2 and immediate replay.
- It is not valid to claim 128k or near-1M today. The runtime currently rejects
  a ~60k prompt unless `DSV4_MAX_PREFILL_TOKENS=0` disables the production
  guard, and doing that should be a separate explicit diagnostic run.
- `max_blocks=1000` at `block_size=256` advertises `max_tokens=256000`, not
  1M L1 RAM capacity. Near-1M would require a separate design/proof path and
  likely different block/L2 assumptions.

exactness failure even with cache/paged/L2 off and pool quant off. This

## [2026-05-24 01:45 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - max output/context/thinking settings audit checkpoint

I audited the max-output/max-context/max-thinking settings path after the Qwen
retained-media run exposed the practical "thinking consumed the whole output
budget" behavior.

Confirmed by source read:

- Chat Settings has one `Max Tokens` per-chat output control and one
  `Max Thinking Tokens` per-chat thinking-budget control;
- Server Settings `Max Output Tokens` maps to launch `--max-tokens` as the
  omitted-request default;
- Server Settings `Max Context Tokens` maps to launch `--max-prompt-tokens`;
- Chat `maxTokens` maps only to Chat Completions `max_tokens` or Responses
  `max_output_tokens`;
- Chat `maxThinkingTokens` maps only to local `max_thinking_tokens` plus
  `chat_template_kwargs.thinking_budget`;
- stale `maxThinkingTokens` is suppressed when `enableThinking=false`;
- reset/default-profile paths do not make model/default max output or max
  thinking sticky per-chat overrides.

I added one missing reset-policy guard so model `maxThinkingTokens` is not
turned into a sticky per-chat thinking budget on reset.

- engine selected max-output/max-thinking tests:

I rechecked DSV4 cache/default/capacity from source and existing artifacts.

- DSV4 pool codec defaults on only under native composite prefix cache;

  -> `2 passed, 52 deselected`.

  `build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-13k-edge-cachehit-bundled-d503ea7f-freshl2-20260524.json`
  proves ~`32k` resolved-prompt immediate replay:

    `130.110 tok/s`, Metal peak `97950.8 MB`, RSS `83969.0 MB`;

  proves ~`60493` prompt tokens are rejected by the production guard:

Boundary:

## [2026-05-24 01:38 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 exactness evidence classification

I inspected the valid DSV4 exactness artifacts instead of rerunning the same
canary:

  `build/current-dsv4-jangtq-k-identifier-canary-strict-nocache-bundled-b3345c29-rerun2-20260524.json`;

Current read:

- The valid strict canary already excludes prefix/paged/L2, pool quant,
  reasoning, parser selection, and max-output caps:
  - current bundled Python;
  - DSV4 JANGTQ-K health gate passed;
  - `--disable-prefix-cache`;
  - no paged/L2;
  - `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0`;
  - `enable_thinking=false`;
  - explicit request `repetition_penalty=1.0`.
- It still corrupted the five-identifier list:
  `THREE.PerspectiveCamera` -> `THREE.PerscpectiveCamera`.
- The logprob matrix shows this is context-sensitive:
  - isolated `THREE.PerspectiveCamera` passes;
  - list-copy emits `THREE.PerscpectiveCamera`;
  - constructor sentence emits `new THREE.PermpectiveCamera(...)`.
- At the list-copy failure point the generated token stream is:
  `TH`, `REE`, `.P`, `ers`, `c`, `pective`, `Camera`.
- Top logprobs at the wrong position rank token `c` first
  (`-0.5493`) and the correct `pective` second (`-2.0653`).

Verification:

- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py::test_objective_proof_digest_surfaces_dsv4_logprob_context_matrix tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py::test_objective_proof_digest_prefers_strict_dsv4_identifier_canary`
  -> `2 passed`.

I updated `docs/internal/V1_5_49_RUNTIME_OPEN_ISSUES_2026-05-23.md` with this
classification.

Boundary:

- Do not spend more time rerunning the same DSV4 identifier list canary unless
  source/runtime has changed.
- The next useful DSV4 work is to isolate why the DSV4 body/runtime prefers the
  wrong code token in context, or to create a narrower product claim that DSV4
  Flash is not cleared for exact code/file-generation.
- This is not a fake-sampler/repetition-penalty/cache-default fix candidate.

  but omit positive `max_new_tokens`, repetition penalty, and explicit chat
  reasoning default.
- Hy3 preview has model-owned `max_new_tokens=2048`, `temperature=0`,
  `top_p=1`, `top_k=0`; it omits rep penalty.
- Therefore, the current evidence still argues against a hidden global sampler,
  rep-penalty, top-k, max-output, or reasoning-mode fix for Ling/MiniMax-small
  mixed-language behavior. The next useful work is artifact/runtime comparison
  or deciding support guidance per artifact, not forced defaults.

## [2026-05-24 02:06 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 bounded-prefill wording aligned

While doing the DSV4 cache-policy/math pass, I found a stale docstring in
`vmlx_engine/utils/dsv4_batch_generator.py`: `_prefill_last_logits` still said
the default was single-shot and chunking was opt-in. That is no longer true.
Production defaults to bounded 2048-token prefill; `DSV4_PREFILL_STEP_SIZE=0`
is the legacy single-shot diagnostic override.

Change:

- wording only, no runtime behavior changed;
- commit pushed: `478fc27d docs: clarify dsv4 bounded prefill default`.

Verification:

- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_long_prefill_guard_describes_bounded_chunk_default tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_batch_generator_prefill_step_default_and_legacy_override tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_cli_cache_policy_defaults_to_native_composite_cache tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_runtime_policy_applies_to_bench_like_cli_args tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_runtime_policy_respects_explicit_prefix_cache_disable tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_serve_path_forces_generic_kv_quantization_off`
  -> `6 passed`.

Current DSV4 policy evidence still says:

- native composite prefix/paged/L2 is default-on unless explicitly disabled;
- `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1` defaults on under the native composite path;
- generic KV quant is forced off for DSV4;
- production guard still blocks prompts over `32768` unless
  `DSV4_MAX_PREFILL_TOKENS=0`;
- 128k/1M capacity is still not proven.

## [2026-05-24 02:48 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Took and pushed DSV4 repetition-scope fix

I reviewed the dirty DSV4 exactness lane and took it to a clean commit:

- `6979eca2 fix: scope dsv4 repetition penalty to generated context`
  pushed to `origin/codex/pr-intake-manifest`.

What it changes:

- DSV4 no longer receives generator-global logits processors at construction;
- DSV4 inserts per-request logits processors, same as the normal batch path;
- the per-request wrapper trims prompt-only context so repetition penalty sees
  final prompt token plus generated output, matching `generate_step` semantics;
- this is meant to avoid punishing exact identifiers/code already present in
  the prompt.

Verification:

- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_repetition_penalty_uses_generated_only_prompt_context`
  -> `1 passed`;
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py -k 'dsv4 or repetition or cache_hit or prefix_cache'`
  -> `55 passed`;
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_batching.py -k 'repetition or dsv4 or cache_merge or paged_cache'`
  -> `5 passed`;
- `git diff --check` -> clean.

Boundary:

- This is a root-cause-aligned no-heavy scheduler fix for one plausible DSV4
  code/identifier corruption path. It still needs live DSV4 code/exact-copy
  output validation before anyone says the release blocker is cleared.

Observed:

- first turn: 1259 prompt tokens, 64 output tokens, `cached_tokens=0`,

  `19.133 tok/s`, Metal active stayed about `79.9 GB`;

  `family=deepseek_v4`, `schema=deepseek_v4_v7`,
  `cache_type=native_composite`, components `swa_local`,
  `csa_compressed_pool`, `hca_compressed_pool`, `incomplete_tail_state`,
  `pool_quant.enabled=true`, and generic TurboQuant KV disabled with reason
  `native_dsv4_composite`.

Boundary:

- This proves the short DSV4 native composite prefix hit and RAM/speed row.
- It does NOT clear DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation exactness.
- It does NOT prove 128k/near-1M DSV4 cache capacity. That still needs a
  controlled high-capacity stress with abort thresholds and probably should be
  run when the machine is otherwise idle.

- `144,837,947 bytes / 5037 tokens = ~28,754.8 bytes/token`;
- extrapolated L2 size at 1M tokens is about `26.78 GiB`;
- a 10 GiB block-L2 cap projects to about `373,413 tokens`, not 1M, on this
  observed row;
- this is an observed L2-storage extrapolation, not a full 1M live proof and
  not a resident-RAM proof. Do not claim DSV4 near-1M/10GB is cleared.

- isolated `THREE.PerspectiveCamera` still passed exactly;

  `THREE.WebWebGLRenderer`, `THREE.ScScene`, `THREE.PPerspectiveCamera`,
  `THREE.MMesh`, `THREE.BBoxGeometry`;
- no think tags leaked.

- cold long-prefill is still very slow;
- observed L2 capacity is not near 1M tokens in 10 GiB on this row;

## [2026-05-24 02:51 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 rep-penalty context fix is real but not full quality fix

The DSV4 8k projection probe completed:

- `build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-chat-thinkingoff-cachehit-8k-projection-latest-jang-20260524.json`
  -> `status=pass`;
- turn 1: 20167 prompt tokens, 4 completion, no cache hit, `133.57s`,
  Metal active `80.853 GB`, Metal peak `88.972 GB`;
- turn 2: same prompt, `cached_tokens=20166`, `cache_detail=paged+dsv4`,
  `0.38s`, decode `10.526 tok/s`;
- L2 observed projection: `18,804.5 bytes/token`, about `17.513 GiB` at
  1M tokens, about `571,002 tokens` under a 10 GiB block-L2 cap.

I found and fixed one real DSV4-specific sampler-context bug:

- normal BatchGenerator requests already wrap repetition-penalty processors so
  prompt-only tokens are not penalized;
- DSV4 was installing unwrapped global repetition-penalty processors on
  `DSV4BatchGenerator` and also passing the full original prompt as processor
  context for cache-hit parity;
- exact-copy/code prompts include the desired output identifiers in the user
  prompt, so full-prompt penalty can suppress correct identifier tokens and
  bias toward corrupt variants.

Code change:

- `vmlx_engine/scheduler.py`
  - DSV4 generator now receives `logits_processors=None` globally;
  - DSV4 requests now pass per-request wrapped processors built from
    `request.prompt_token_ids`, preserving generate_step semantics: final
    prompt token plus generated output, not the whole user prompt.

Regression:

- `tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_repetition_penalty_uses_generated_only_prompt_context`.

Verification:

- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py tests/test_dsv4_batch_generator_speed.py tests/test_runtime_memory_stress_probe.py`
  -> `71 passed`.

Important boundary:

- This did NOT clear DSV4 exact identifier/code quality.
- Post-fix live artifact:
  `build/current-dsv4-jangtq-k-identifier-canary-modelowned-nocache-after-reppen-context-fix-20260524.json`
  -> `status=fail`, still failing `five_identifier_list` and
  `threejs_snippet`.
- It improved/changed some corruptions but still emitted:
  `THREE.PersThree.jsPerspectiveCamera`, `THREE.WebWebGLRenderer`, and
  `Three.MeshBasicMaterial` casing/namespace drift.
- Logs still show model-owned `repetition_penalty=1.05`, thinking off, no
  prefix cache, direct Chat Completions.

Conclusion:

- Prompt-wide DSV4 rep-penalty was a real bug and should stay fixed.
- It is not the full root cause of DSV4 code/identifier corruption. Next work
  should compare DSV4 body/logit math under the list/code prompt shape,
  especially JANGTQ/prestack routed matmul or DSV4 generator first-token/decode
  logits, not UI/Responses/tools/prefix cache.

Updated boundary:

- not just model-owned temperature/top-p;
- not just repetition penalty;
- not JANGTQ/prestack-only;
- not DSV4 pool quant;
- not prefix/L2/tools/Responses/UI.

Next highest-value root cause step is raw prompt/template + logit/body
isolation. The pure-JANG logs also show:

- `quant_shape_inference: patched 131 module(s)` due config/safetensor shape
  disagreement;
- AutoTokenizer rejected the DSV4 config and the loader used tokenizer.json

- fallback chat template injected before the DSV4 encoding shim.

  root-cause isolation: raw prompt/template plus logits/body math, because the

  Responses, prefix/L2, pool quant, JANGTQ-only prestack, temperature/top-p,
  and repetition penalty as the whole cause.

this note is superseded, unless you find an urgent blocker and write it here.

  READMEs, scripts, etc.). No runtime `.py` mismatch found.

New DSV4 evidence:

- Prompt render for the exact canary is sane:
  `<|User|>Output exactly this identifier...THREE.PerspectiveCamera<|Assistant|></think>`.
- Tokenizer round trip is sane for both expected and corrupt identifiers:
  `THREE.PerspectiveCamera` tokenizes as
  `TH`, `REE`, `.P`, `ers`, `pective`, `Camera`;
  `THREE.PertiveCamera` tokenizes as
  `TH`, `REE`, `.P`, `ert`, `ive`, `Camera`.
- Direct full-context/ordinary incremental model probe:
  `build/current-dsv4-jang-cache-vs-full-logit-isolation-threejs-20260524.json`
  shows after `THREE.P` the correct `ers` ranks above corrupt `ert`
  (`ers=-1.001` full, `ert=-1.676`; incremental `ers=-1.380`,
  `ert=-1.460`).
- Exact `DSV4BatchGenerator` trace:
  `build/current-dsv4-jang-batch-generator-isolated-identifier-logits-20260524.json`
  reproduces the bad output `THREE.PertiveCamera`; at the same `THREE.P`
  point it ranks corrupt `ert` above `ers`
  (`ert=-1.228`, `ers=-1.730`).

I tried one hypothesis and rejected it:

- Unconditional `mx.eval(last_logits)` before `mx.clear_cache()` caused a Metal
  GPU timeout in the live probe, so that change was backed out and must not be
  reintroduced casually.

Current uncommitted source change:

- `vmlx_engine/utils/dsv4_batch_generator.py` now only uses two-phase N-1/last
  prompt prefill when a prompt snapshot will actually be captured; no-cache or
  short-prompt generation uses full-prompt prefill for the live cache.
- Unit evidence:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_generator_skips_prompt_snapshot_when_cache_store_disabled tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_generator_captures_prompt_snapshot_when_cache_store_enabled tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_generator_skips_prompt_snapshot_for_short_cache_store_prompt_by_default tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py::test_dsv4_batch_generator_prefill_step_default_and_legacy_override tests/test_dsv4_batch_generator_speed.py`
  -> `8 passed`.
- Boundary: this did NOT fix the exact identifier canary by itself. After this
  change, `build/current-dsv4-jang-batch-generator-isolated-identifier-logits-after-full-prefill-fix-20260524.json`
  still emits `THREE.PertiveCamera` with the same `ert > ers` ranking.

Next root-cause target should be the difference between direct model
full/incremental logits and the `DSV4BatchGenerator` prefill/decode execution
environment. Do not call DSV4 exact-code/list quality cleared.

## [2026-05-24 03:05 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - UI/CLI flag parity hardening found/fixed preview sanitizer drift

I took the non-heavy UI/CLI/app flag parity lane Eric asked for and avoided the
JANG-tools/body-logit files.

Concrete finding/fix:

- Real launcher `panel/src/main/sessions.ts` already stripped stale DSV4
  `additionalArgs` values for `--native-mtp-depth` and
  `--native-mtp-sampling-policy`.
- CLI preview `panel/src/renderer/src/components/sessions/SessionSettings.tsx`
  had those flags in the DSV4 blocklist, but not in
  `ADDITIONAL_ARG_VALUE_FLAGS`, so preview sanitization could strip the flag
  while leaving its value token behind. That could make preview text imply a
  stale DSV4 MTP arg survived differently from real launch.
- Fixed by adding both flags to the preview value-taking flags table only.
  No runtime parser/cache/sampling behavior changed.

New regression coverage added in `tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py`:

- DSV4 settings have one prefix owner (`DSV4 Native Composite Prefix Cache`),
  no stale duplicate labels (`DSV4 Native Cache`, `DSV4 Composite Prefix
  Cache`, `DSV4 Pool Quantization`, old disabled-by-default prose).
- DSV4 composite-prefix/L2/pool toggles map to one policy and to
  `dsv4EnvFromConfig` (`VMLX_DSV4_ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHE`, `DSV4_POOL_QUANT`) with
  pool quant gated by prefix.
- Preview and real launcher now must have identical
  `ADDITIONAL_ARG_VALUE_FLAGS`, `IMAGE_ADDITIONAL_ARG_BLOCKLIST`, and
  `DSV4_ADDITIONAL_ARG_BLOCKLIST`.

## [2026-05-24 03:16 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 no-snapshot prefill split committed separately

I verified and committed the remaining dirty DSV4 generator lane as a separate
fix:

- Commit: `01c6f3fb fix: skip dsv4 snapshot split when unused`
- Pushed to `origin/codex/pr-intake-manifest`.

Root cause / behavior:

- `DSV4BatchGenerator` split prompt prefill into `N-1` then last-token so the
  scheduler could store an `N-1` prompt-boundary snapshot for prefix/L2 reuse.
- When `capture_prompt_snapshot=False`, or when the prompt is below
  `DSV4_PROMPT_SNAPSHOT_MIN_TOKENS`, that snapshot is not consumed. The old
  code still did the split, causing an unnecessary second model call for
  no-cache / short-prompt DSV4 requests.
- The fix only uses two-phase prefill when a snapshot will actually be
  captured. Otherwise it runs full prompt prefill once and records
  `prefill_full` timing.

Proof:

- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py
  tests/test_dsv4_batch_generator_speed.py` -> `62 passed`.
- `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260524-dsv4-prefill-nosnapshot-singlepass.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, cache_family_pytest 116 passed,
  panel_cache_launch_policy 92 passed.

Boundary:

- This is a DSV4 prefill overhead fix, not a code-quality/logit fix.
- It does not change DSV4 exact-code identifier failures, parser policy,
  generation defaults, or cache equivalence semantics.

Follow-up proof refresh after the two commits:

- Refreshed exact artifact paths consumed by
  `tests/cross_matrix/summarize_objective_proof.py`:
  - `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260524-after-strict-dsv4-canary.json`
  - `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260521.json`
  - `build/current-generation-defaults-contract-20260521.json`
  - `build/current-native-mtp-contract-20260521.json`
  - `build/current-vl-media-cache-contract-20260521.json`
- Objective digest:
  `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260524-after-refreshing-ui-contracts.json`
  -> every requirement PASS except
  `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`.
- Umbrella:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260524-after-dsv4-nosnapshot-prefill.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed_steps=[]`,
  `open_requirements=["DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared"]`.

Current practical state:

- UI/CLI settings, max output/context, parser registry, generation defaults,
  Native MTP, VL media/cache, MCP policy, DSV4 cache/tool loop, and
  non-DSV4 cache architecture rows are green at current source.
- DSV4 exact-code/list quality is still not release-cleared. Do not convert
  this into a cache/UI/parser story; current evidence says it survives no-cache,
  pool-off, deterministic, rep_penalty=1.0, pure JANG, and JANGTQ.

## [2026-05-24 03:30 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - UI/CLI flag parity lane closed pending commit

I finished the UI/app/CLI parity investigation Eric requested for the
autodetect/toggle/disabled-setting class.

Concrete bug found:

- Panel launch and command preview had an `omniBackend` path that could emit
  `--omni-backend stage2`, but neither `vmlx_engine.cli serve` nor direct
  `vmlx_engine.server` registered that flag.
- The setting was also effectively zombie UI state: it existed in config/defaults
  but had no visible select control.
- It was not in the stale `additionalArgs` value-flag tables or the DSV4
  blocklists, so stale advanced args could leak or parse badly around DSV4.

Fix shape:

- Engine CLI and direct server parser now accept
  `--omni-backend {stage1,stage2}` and map it to existing
  `VMLINUX_OMNI_BACKEND`.
- Real launch and command preview emit it only for detected
  Nemotron-Omni multimodal sessions and only when explicitly set away from
  `stage1`.
- Session form shows an `Omni Backend` select only for Nemotron-Omni multimodal.
- DSV4 stale advanced args strip `--omni-backend` and its value; DSV4 does not
  show or emit this setting.
- No sampling/cache/default parameter was forced. This is explicit UI selection
  to an existing runtime env, not a hidden behavior change.

Proof:

  shows the registered flag and help text.

## [2026-05-24 03:43 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 stale additionalArgs block-L2 sanitizer fixed

I continued Eric's UI/app/CLI parameter audit after the Omni backend fix and
found another real settings/cache edge case.

Concrete bug:

- DSV4 launch owns the native prefix/paged/block-L2 cache policy through typed
  settings, but stale/manual `additionalArgs` were still allowed to append:
  `--enable-block-disk-cache`, `--block-disk-cache-dir`,
  `--block-disk-cache-max-gb`.
- That could duplicate or override the app-owned DSV4 block-L2 launch even
  though the UI presents one DSV4-native cache path.
- The DSV4 stale-arg filter also did not strip image-only flags
  `--image-mode`, `--image-quantize`, `--mflux-class`.

Fix:

- Added those block-L2 and image-only flags to the DSV4 additional-args
  blocklist in both real launch and command preview.
- Added a broader source contract: every flag emitted by real launch or preview
  must be accepted by the engine, preview and launch sanitizer tables must
  match, and every app-emitted flag must be DSV4-blocklisted so stale
  `additionalArgs` cannot duplicate app-owned DSV4 policy.

Proof:

- Focused Python:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_paged_cache.py
  tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestTurboQuantKVTelemetry::test_panel_emitted_cli_flags_are_engine_registered_and_dsv4_sanitized

## [2026-05-24 03:52 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - VLM video sampling restart false-positive fixed

Continuing Eric's session-settings audit, I found a real UI behavior bug outside
the DSV4 exactness lane.

Concrete bug:

- `videoFps` and `videoMaxFrames` were listed in `RESTART_REQUIRED_KEYS`.
- They are not CLI launch flags. `chat.ts` reads the saved session config on
  each request and forwards them as `video_fps` / `video_max_frames`.
- So changing VLM video sampling in the UI could falsely tell the user to
  restart the session even though the next request can use the new values.

Fix:

- Removed `videoFps` and `videoMaxFrames` from `RESTART_REQUIRED_KEYS`.
- Added a guard proving they remain request-scoped: not in restart keys, still
  wired in `chat.ts`, and still controlled by the settings UI.

Proof:

- Focused:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q
  tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestTurboQuantKVTelemetry::test_vlm_video_sampling_is_request_scoped_not_restart_required
  tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestTurboQuantKVTelemetry::test_panel_emitted_cli_flags_are_engine_registered_and_dsv4_sanitized`
  -> 2 passed.
- `cd panel && npm run typecheck` -> pass.
- `build/current-vl-media-cache-contract-20260524-video-request-scoped.json`
  -> pass.
- `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260524-video-request-scoped.json`
  -> pass.
- `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260524-video-request-scoped.json`
  -> pass.
- Refreshed fixed objective digest inputs and
  `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260521.json` now has only DSV4 quality
  open.
- `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260524-video-request-scoped-rerun.json`
  -> pass.
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260524-video-request-scoped-rerun.json`
  -> pass, failed_steps=[], only DSV4 quality open.

Repo-local package was rebuilt. No hidden generation/cache/sampling defaults,
no `/Applications/vMLX.app`, and no release/notary/publish action.

## [2026-05-24 03:36 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 code exactness now isolated to prompt-sensitive code-copy logits

I ran the next DSV4 isolation slice through repo-local bundled Python with
`PYTHONPATH=` / `PYTHONNOUSERSITE=1`, pure JANG DSV4, prefix disabled, and
pool quant disabled.

Artifacts:

- `build/current-dsv4-jang-thinking-off-logit-probe-20260524.json`
- `build/current-dsv4-jang-live-api-copy-framing-canary-20260524.json`

Findings:

- Thinking-off DSV4 encoder is rendering the expected `</think>` suffix; the
  previous direct probe that showed `<think>` was using the loaded tokenizer
  default without explicitly passing `enable_thinking=False`.
- In the original list prompt, direct model logits at the fourth line after
  `TH` prefer corrupt `IVE` over correct `REE`
  (`-0.973` vs `-1.324`), matching live `THIVE.*`.
- With stronger copy framing, the same direct logit point flips hard to correct
  `REE` (`-0.016`) and live `copy_list` is exact.
- Original code/snippet framing is a BPE-boundary copy failure: after generated
  `.P`, direct logits prefer whole token `Pers` over continuation `ers`,
  which yields `.PPerspective...`; after generated `.B`, direct logits prefer
  whole token `Box` over continuation `ox`, which yields `.BBoxGeometry...`.
- Stronger copy framing fixes the camera logit point but the live snippet still
  fails with `WebWebGLRenderer` and `BBoxGeometry`, and still adds markdown
  fences despite "no markdown fences".

Read:

- This is not a stale JANG repo, parser, Responses route, prefix/L2, pool
  quant, JANGTQ-only, or generic max-token/settings issue.
- The remaining DSV4 blocker is code-shaped exact-copy/generation behavior in
  the DSV4 model/runtime body under thinking-off chat prompts. A logits
  processor that bans repeated subpieces might mask these canaries but would be
  a risky hidden decoder policy; I am not applying that without a narrower
  proof that it preserves normal code generation.

## [2026-05-24 04:03 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - session runtime config schema/CLI/UI coverage closed

I finished the follow-up audit for UI/session/main-process schema drift.

Root cause found:

- `panel/src/main/server.ts::ServerConfig` was missing several settings that
  are owned by the renderer/session layer or forwarded by launch/request code:
  Smelt/Flash-MoE fields, `chatTemplate`, VLM video sampling, distributed
  settings, sleep/idle settings, and `defaultRepetitionPenalty`.

  hole: preview/launch/request tests could miss drift because the main-process
  schema did not enumerate the same runtime settings the UI/session layer can
  persist or route.

## [2026-05-24 04:15 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 stale Advanced Args now strips serve-only rollback/tuning flags

I finished the next UI/app-to-engine CLI parity audit slice.

Root cause/gap found:

- Existing DSV4 stale `additionalArgs` sanitizer covered app-emitted flags, but
  did not cover several serve-only CLI flags that the app does not emit itself.
- The important risky ones were:
  - `--no-state-machine-stops`: can weaken token-level reasoning/stop
    detection and make DSV4 reasoning/tool-stop regressions harder to isolate.
  - `--prefill-keep-alloc`: can change DSV4 memory pressure behavior.
  - `--uds`: makes the engine ignore host/port and can break app health/routing.
  - `--inference-endpoints` / `--wake-timeout`: remote/sleep routing knobs that
    should not be inherited by DSV4 app-owned native runtime launch.
- This is not a default-param change. It only strips stale/manual DSV4
  Advanced Args that could override app-owned DSV4 launch policy.

Files changed for my slice:

- `panel/src/main/sessions.ts`
- `panel/src/renderer/src/components/sessions/SessionSettings.tsx`
- `panel/tests/settings-flow.test.ts`
- `tests/test_engine_audit.py`

Proof:

- focused Python CLI/sanitizer guards -> 2 passed
- focused settings-flow vitest -> 236 passed
- panel typecheck -> pass
- direct inventory after patch:
  - no panel-emitted flag is missing from the DSV4 blocklist
  - `--no-state-machine-stops`, `--prefill-keep-alloc`, `--uds`,
    `--inference-endpoints`, and `--wake-timeout` are blocked in both real
    launch and preview
- no-heavy panel settings:
  `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260524-dsv4-serve-only-arg-sanitizer.json`
  -> pass
- max-output/context:
  `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260524-dsv4-serve-only-arg-sanitizer.json`
  -> pass
- parser registry:
  `build/current-parser-registry-contract-20260524-dsv4-serve-only-arg-sanitizer.json`
  -> pass
- generation defaults:
  `build/current-generation-defaults-contract-20260524-dsv4-serve-only-arg-sanitizer.json`
  -> pass
- refreshed fixed-path proof artifacts; objective digest
  `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260521.json`
  -> only open row is DSV4 long-output/code quality
- rebuilt repo-local package `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app` (notarization
  skipped as expected for local package)
- packaged integrity:
  `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260524-dsv4-serve-only-arg-sanitizer.json`

- umbrella:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260524-dsv4-serve-only-arg-sanitizer.json`

## [2026-05-24 04:18 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Follow-up: DSV4 Advanced Args also strips equals-form blocked flags

Follow-up on the stale Advanced Args sanitizer:

- I found the previous blocklist only stripped blocked flags when they appeared
  as `--flag value`.
- User-entered Advanced Args can also carry `--flag=value`, which would bypass
  the `Set.has(flag)` check for value flags like `--uds=/tmp/...`,
  `--inference-endpoints=...`, `--wake-timeout=...`, `--max-tokens=...`, and
  `--log-level=...`.
- I changed both launch and CLI-preview sanitizers to normalize the flag name
  before checking the DSV4 blocklist, while preserving allowed custom
  `--custom=value` args.
- Files in this narrow slice:
  - `panel/src/main/sessions.ts`
  - `panel/src/renderer/src/components/sessions/SessionSettings.tsx`
  - `panel/tests/settings-flow.test.ts`
  - `tests/test_engine_audit.py`

Fresh proof before commit:

- `npm exec vitest run tests/settings-flow.test.ts --testNamePattern 'DSV4 additional args strips blocked equals-form serve overrides|DSV4 additional args cannot reenable native MTP or deterministic sampling policy|Settings → CLI Round-Trip Completeness' --reporter=verbose`
  -> `237 passed`;
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestTurboQuantKVTelemetry::test_panel_startup_defaults_sanitize_incompatible_saved_modes`
  -> `1 passed`;
- `npm --prefix panel run typecheck`
  -> pass.

Boundary:

- This is a stale/manual Advanced Args hardening fix only. It does not change
  DSV4 cache defaults, sampling defaults, parser selection, or JANG/JANGTQ
  runtime files.

Commit/push:

- `c80b21f3 fix: strip equals-form dsv4 advanced args`
- pushed to `origin/codex/pr-intake-manifest` and `origin/main`.

JANG repo check:

- `/Users/eric/jang` is on `main` at `7eb2064 fix: repair qwen affine shape ambiguity`;
- `origin/main` also points to `7eb2064`;
- there are many untracked JANG docs/scripts/examples, so I did not push them
  as confirmed fixes.

Process note:

  repo that I did not start. I am not killing or touching it.

## [2026-05-24 04:36 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 32k current-packaged replay proof and cache_salt trap

DSV4 staged capacity update:

- Initial `--prompt-tokens 32768` attempt mapped to `80655` actual DSV4 tokens
  and was correctly rejected by the production guard:
  `max safe prefill is 32768`.
  Artifact:
  `build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-32k-prefill2048-cachehit-c80-20260524.json`.
- A calibrated staged row with `cache_salt` reached HTTP 200 up to `32005`
  prompt tokens but wrote zero blocks. Root cause: `cache_salt` is an
  intentional cache bypass in vMLX. Do not use salted rows as cache-capacity
  proof.
- Correct replay row without `cache_salt`:
  `build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-32k-replay-c80-20260524.json`
  -> `status=pass`.

Correct replay proof:

- current repo-local packaged Python from `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app`;
- DSV4 JANGTQ-K, native composite prefix/paged/L2, `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=1`;
- isolated L2 dir `/tmp/vmlx-dsv4-32k-replay-c80-block-cache`;
- first `32005`-token prompt:
  - `244.226s`;
  - RSS `83974.9 MB`;
  - Metal active `83065.1 MB`, peak `97950.8 MB`;
  - stored `32004` cache-key tokens in `126` blocks;
  - L2 size `0.437GB`.
- second identical `32005`-token prompt:
  - `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens=32004`;
  - `cache_detail=paged+dsv4`;
  - `1.150s`;
  - log decode rate `13.9 tok/s`;
  - scheduler `cache_hit_tokens=32004`.

Capacity read:

- Observed block-L2 footprint is `14652.9 bytes/token`.
- Projected `1M` cached tokens would be `13.647GB`.
- Projected current `10GB` L2 budget capacity is about `732784` cached tokens.
- This is a real current-packaged ~32k replay proof, but still not 128k,
  near-1M, restart-L2, varied-prompt equivalence, or DSV4 code-quality
  clearance.

Follow-up I am taking next:

- Fresh-process restart/L2 proof against the same
  `/tmp/vmlx-dsv4-32k-replay-c80-block-cache` directory.
- Goal: prove whether a new server can read the `126` stored DSV4 composite
  blocks and get `paged+dsv4` without relying on in-process RAM cache.

Restart/L2 result:

- Artifact:
  `build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-32k-restart-l2-c80-20260524.json`
  -> `status=pass`.
- Fresh server opened the existing block dir with `entries=126`, `size=0.44GB`.
- The `32005` prompt-token request reported:
  - `cached_tokens=32004`;
  - `cache_detail=paged+dsv4`;
  - `disk_hits=126`;
  - `disk_writes=0`;
  - elapsed `7.497s`;
  - RSS `83084.6 MB`;
  - Metal active `82525.0 MB`, peak `85579.4 MB`.
- This proves restart-L2 reuse at ~32k resolved prompt tokens. It still does
  not prove 128k, near-1M, varied-prompt equivalence, or DSV4 code quality.

## [2026-05-24 04:38 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 calibrated 32k capacity passed, but L2 reuse is not proven

The DSV4 calibrated native composite cache capacity probe finished:

- artifact:
  `build/current-runtime-memory-stress-dsv4-jangtq-k-calibrated-capacity-c80-20260524.json`;
- row `dsv4_jangtq_k`, packaged Python from repo-local app, chat route;
- launch used:
  `--dsv4-enable-prefix-cache --use-paged-cache --paged-cache-block-size 256 --enable-block-disk-cache --block-disk-cache-max-gb 10`;
- runtime log confirmed:
  `PoolQuantizedV4Cache` layers, generic KV quant forced off for native DSV4,
  DSV4 native composite block index schema `deepseek_v4_v7`;
- result `status=pass` for harness prompt sizes 4096, 8192, 13000, which
  actually tokenized to 10086, 20167, and 32005 prompt tokens;
- final/peak memory:
  - 10086 prompt tokens: RSS 81.391GB, Metal active 80.558GB, peak 84.013GB;
  - 20167 prompt tokens: RSS 81.598GB, Metal active 80.778GB, peak 91.058GB;
  - 32005 prompt tokens: RSS 81.812GB, Metal active 80.996GB, peak 97.740GB;
- all three returned HTTP 200 and `finish_reason=length` with 16 completion
  tokens.

Important boundary:

- `usage.cached_tokens` stayed `0`;
- health/cache counters stayed at one allocated block, zero cached tokens, zero
  disk writes;
- block cache dir only contained `block_index.db`.

So this proves the current DSV4 packaged runtime survives a calibrated ~32k
prompt under the configured memory envelope. It does **not** prove million-token
capacity, prefix replay hits, or L2 block storage/reuse. A repeated-prefix cache
hit/store harness is still required for that claim.

I also added a no-heavy UI/CLI flag-parity contract:

- new test: `tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py`;
- it verifies panel/session source flags are accepted by the engine `serve`
  parser and the DSV4 Advanced Args blocklist only names real serve flags;
- legacy diagnostic exception: `--model` is only parsed from old log lines, not
  emitted by current `vmlx_engine.cli serve`;
- focused proof: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py`
  -> `3 passed`;
- full settings contract:
  `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260524-cli-flag-parity.json`
  -> `status=pass`, including `engine_panel_cli_flag_contract: 3 passed`.
- committed/pushed:
  `70af091f test: guard panel serve cli flag parity` to `origin/main` and
  `origin/codex/pr-intake-manifest`.

  with packaged Python from repo-local app and explicit block disk dir
  `/tmp/vmlx-dsv4-32k-replay-c80-block-cache`;
- first request:

  - elapsed `244.226s`;

  - `Block disk: writing 126 blocks to SSD`;
  - `Stored paged cache ... 32004 cache-key tokens from 32005 prompt tokens`;
  - `Paged cache hit ... 126 blocks, 32004 tokens`;

## [2026-05-24 05:05 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - preview/runtime CLI parity tightened

I audited the UI command-preview path against runtime `sessions.ts` after
Eric's latest "all -- flags passed from UI must be real" request.

Findings:

- Actual runtime `panel/src/main/sessions.ts` and renderer preview
  `SessionSettings.tsx` already shared the same large DSV4 incompatible
  additional-args blocklist:
  - `ADDITIONAL_ARG_VALUE_FLAGS`: 65 flags;
  - `DSV4_ADDITIONAL_ARG_BLOCKLIST`: 84 flags.
- The Vitest preview replica was stale:
  - only 16 value-taking flags;
  - only 18 DSV4 blocked flags;
  - it still expected VLM + external speculative decoding and continuous
    batching + external speculative decoding to emit `--speculative-model`.
- Runtime does not emit that path anymore. External speculative decoding is
  compatible only with non-DSV4, non-VLM, non-continuous-batching launches.

What changed:

- `panel/tests/settings-flow.test.ts` now derives the additional-arg value and
  DSV4 blocklist sets from `src/main/sessions.ts`, so the test replica cannot
  silently lag runtime for those lists.
- `tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py` now asserts runtime and renderer
  preview source blocklists match for:
  - `ADDITIONAL_ARG_VALUE_FLAGS`;
  - `IMAGE_ADDITIONAL_ARG_BLOCKLIST`;
  - `DSV4_ADDITIONAL_ARG_BLOCKLIST`.
- Speculative-decoding preview tests now match runtime:
  - explicit external draft works only when `continuousBatching=false`;
  - VLM suppresses external speculative decoding;
  - continuous batching suppresses external speculative decoding while
    preserving unrelated flags such as embedding model.
- `run_vl_media_cache_contract.py` now requires the new VLM suppression marker
  instead of the stale "both emit" marker.

Proof:

- `tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py` -> `4 passed`;
- full `panel/tests/settings-flow.test.ts` -> `237 passed`;
- `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260524-preview-runtime-parity.json`
  -> `status=pass`, panel settings `291 passed`, CLI flag contract `4 passed`;
- `build/current-vl-media-cache-contract-20260521.json`
  -> `status=pass`;
- `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260524-after-strict-dsv4-canary.json`
  -> `status=pass`;
- refreshed `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260521.json` now has only
  `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared` open;
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260524-preview-runtime-parity-final.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed_steps=[]`, same single open DSV4 quality row.

Commits:

- `6179f847 test: mark cache-bypassed stress rows` contains the preview/runtime
  parity strengthening plus other agent's cache-stress marker work.
- `c31ee510 test: update vl speculative launch contract` updates the VL gate
  marker to the corrected suppression behavior.

Boundary:

- This is source/no-heavy UI/runtime contract hardening. It does not solve or
  clear the DSV4 exact-code/identifier corruption row.

## [2026-05-24 05:05 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 code exactness narrowed to rail/decoder behavior

I reran the current DSV4 route/mode exact-code canary from the bundled Python:

- `build/current-dsv4-jangtq-k-route-mode-code-exactness-20260524-rerun-c31ee510.json`
  -> `status=fail`;
- cache was bypassed/disabled for the requests:
  `skip_prefix_cache=true`, `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0`,
  `VMLINUX_DSV4_ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHE=0`;
- chat/responses no-thinking still corrupt identifiers:
  `THREE.WebWebGLRenderer`, `Three.MeshBasicMaterial`;
- thinking-on preserved all required identifiers but still emitted markdown
  fences, so it is not an exact-copy pass.

Then I added explicit no-thinking repetition-penalty controls to the canary:

- new cases: `chat_off_rep1`, `responses_off_rep1`;
- artifact:
  `build/current-dsv4-jangtq-k-route-mode-code-exactness-20260524-rep1-controls-c31ee510.json`
  -> `status=fail`;
- `repetition_penalty=1.0` fixes the `Three.MeshBasicMaterial` casing issue,
  but **does not** fix `WebWebGLRenderer`;
- thinking-on still preserves identifiers but emits fences.

Read:

- DSV4 code/file exactness is not a cache/L2/pool-quant issue and not solely a
  repetition penalty issue.
- The no-thinking rail's prompt suffix (`<｜Assistant｜></think>`) remains a
  strong suspect versus the thinking rail (`<｜Assistant｜><think>`), but I have

  hidden, block size is disabled/fixed at 256 when native composite cache is
  active, generic stored KV quantization is disabled, and `DSV4 CSA/HCA Pool
  Codec` is a separate native pool-codec toggle.

  omits it. `--max-prompt-tokens` is emitted only for explicit context cap.

  flags are intentionally not synthesized by the panel.

## [2026-05-24 06:12 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Command preview numeric sanitizer drift fixed

Continuing Eric's UI/app/CLI flag wiring audit, I found a real production
preview/runtime mismatch:

- `panel/src/main/sessions.ts` floors/sanitizes advanced numeric flags through
  `finitePositiveInteger(...)`.
- Production `SessionSettings.tsx` command preview still used weaker/raw values
  for:
  - Native MTP depth (`Math.round(Number(...))`);
  - Smelt expert percent;
  - Flash-MoE slot bank and I/O split;
  - external speculative `numDraftTokens`.

Impact:

- Launch behavior was already sanitized, but the visible CLI preview could show
  a different flag value than the actual launch for stale/fractional persisted
  config. That is exactly the class of UI/settings confusion Eric asked us to
  eliminate.

Changed:

- `SessionSettings.tsx` now uses `finitePositiveInteger(...)` for those preview
  values, matching `sessions.ts`.
- `tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py` now asserts the production command
  preview uses the same sanitizer in the Native-MTP and advanced-mode blocks.

TDD proof:

- New guard failed first on the old `Math.round(Number(configuredDepth...))`
  preview path.
- After the patch:
  - `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py`
    -> `6 passed`;
  - `cd panel && npm test -- --run tests/settings-flow.test.ts tests/cache-control-policy.test.ts tests/dsv4-env.test.ts`
    -> `279 passed`;
  - `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260524-preview-sanitizer.json`
    -> pass;
  - `build/current-native-mtp-contract-20260524-preview-sanitizer.json`
    -> pass;
  - `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260524-preview-sanitizer.json`
    -> pass.

No engine runtime, sampler default, parser mapping, or cache policy changed.

## [2026-05-24 06:14 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Thinking-budget runtime/UI gate verified

I reviewed the remaining dirty thinking-budget changes after the preview
sanitizer commit. Read:

- `models.ts` now reads template support from `chat_template.jinja`,
  `chat_template.txt`, and `tokenizer_config.chat_template`.
- If a template has thinking markers but no budget variable, generation
  defaults report `thinkingBudgetSupported=false` and do not surface
  `maxThinkingTokens` as a model default.
- Chat request assembly now suppresses `max_thinking_tokens` /
  `chat_template_kwargs.thinking_budget` only when local metadata explicitly
  says the template does not support a thinking budget.
- Chat settings hides the Max Thinking Tokens field only for that explicit
  unsupported case.

This is aligned with Eric's no-fake-params rule: it does not force thinking off,
does not add hidden sampler defaults, and does not invent a runtime budget. It
avoids sending a budget knob to templates that do not consume it.

Fresh proof:

- `cd panel && npm test -- --run tests/generation-defaults.test.ts tests/request-builder.test.ts tests/settings-flow.test.ts tests/chat-override-policy.test.ts tests/chat-settings-reset-policy.test.ts`

  -> pass;
- `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260524-thinking-budget-runtime-gate.json`
  -> pass.

  - `build/current-regression-suite-20260524-thinking-budget-runtime-gate.json`
    -> `status=pass`, `failed_steps=[]`, open requirement remains
    `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`.

Boundary:

- This does not claim Gemma thinking-budget behavior is fully solved at model
  quality level. It prevents the UI/request path from advertising or forwarding
  an unsupported template budget when metadata proves it is unsupported.

## [2026-05-24 06:45 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 route-mode proof refreshed; still release-blocked

I worked the remaining DSV4 long-output/code row, not settings wiring.

Changed/proof-hardened:

- Added `normalized_exact` to
  `tests/cross_matrix/run_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py` so the live route
  probe separates:
  - exact code corrupted by identifier/token errors; from
  - correct code wrapped in markdown fences.
- Surfaced `normalized_exact` through
  `tests/cross_matrix/summarize_objective_proof.py`.
- Hardened `tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py` to include the current
  `chat_max` direct-rail case and 10-case dry-run matrix, not the older 9-case
  fixture.
- Added `tests/test_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py` coverage proving
  fenced-but-correct code differs from corrupted identifiers.
- Committed/pushed:
  `57a5fb5f test: separate dsv4 fence and identifier failures`
  to `origin/codex/pr-intake-manifest` and `origin/main`.

Fresh live proof:

- Ran:
  `.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py --out build/current-dsv4-jangtq-k-route-mode-code-exactness-20260524-thinking-on-responses-controls.json --port 8861 --timeout 600 --request-timeout 360`
- It launched bundled Python against
  `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/DeepSeek-V4-Flash-JANGTQ-K`.
- Env overrides:
  `DSV4_POOL_QUANT=0`,
  `VMLINUX_DSV4_ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHE=0`,
  `VMLX_DSV4_ENABLE_PREFIX_CACHE=0`,
  `PYTHONPATH=`,
  `PYTHONNOUSERSITE=1`.
- Result: `status=fail`, 10 cases.

What the live proof says:

- Direct/thinking-off rails still corrupt identifiers:
  `chat_off`, `chat_off_rep1`, `chat_max`, `responses_off`,
  `responses_off_rep1`.
- Explicit `repetition_penalty=1.0` does not fix it:
  `chat_off_rep1` and `responses_off_rep1` still emit `WebWebGLRenderer`.
- Chat and Responses both reproduce it, so this is not endpoint assembly.
- Prefix cache/pool quant are disabled in the diagnostic, so this is not DSV4
  cache reuse or pool quant.
- Thinking-on rails preserve identifiers but still fail exactness because they
  emit markdown fenced output:
  `chat_on`, `chat_on_rep1`, `responses_on`, `responses_on_rep1` have
  `normalized_exact=true`, no missing identifiers, no corrupt patterns, but
  `exact=false` and `has_markdown_fence=true`.
- Legacy completions remains a length/raw-prompt failure, not a chat/API fix.
- Decode speed in this narrow probe was roughly 15-17 tok/s after the first
  chat-off warm path; no 2-3 tok/s regression reproduced here.

Fresh test proof:

- `tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py -k dsv4_prompt_rail_exactness_probe`
  failed before digest wiring exposed `normalized_exact`, then passed.
- `tests/test_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py` -> 5 passed.
- `tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py tests/test_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py tests/test_current_regression_suite.py tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`
  -> 139 passed.
- Refreshed `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260521.json`:
  every row is PASS except
  `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`, which
  remains OPEN.

No model server remains running after the harness. No runtime behavior,
sampling default, parser mapping, prompt template, cache policy, or release
artifact changed.

Current interpretation:

- Do not paper this over by forcing thinking-on: thinking-on avoids identifier
  corruption for this exact small copy task but violates exact-code/no-fence
  behavior and does not prove long file generation.
- Do not treat this as max-output/context, Responses assembly, rep penalty,
  prefix cache, pool quant, or tokenizer; the current evidence rules those out
  for this failure mode.
- Remaining real release blocker is source/broader-body/runtime precision
  clearance for DSV4 direct code generation, plus the larger long-output HTML
  game gate.

## [2026-05-24 06:55 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - UI/CLI/app flag wiring audit refresh

Eric asked specifically to compare UI toggles, command preview, launch args, disabled/incompatible settings, autodetect, and real CLI flags.

Current source head is clean at `2badf1f9 test: cover smelt vl launch gating` before this note. I did not change runtime behavior in this pass.

Read/checked boundaries:

- Runtime launcher: `panel/src/main/sessions.ts::buildArgs`.
- Visible command preview: `panel/src/renderer/src/components/sessions/SessionSettings.tsx::buildCommandPreview`.
- Settings UI/control policy: `SessionConfigForm.tsx`, `cacheControlPolicy.ts`, `dsv4Env.ts`.
- CLI parser/server side: `vmlx_engine/cli.py`, `vmlx_engine/server.py` sampling/default/cache resolution.

Fresh proof:

- Panel settings/cache/default/model-detection slice:
  `npm --prefix panel test -- --run tests/settings-flow.test.ts tests/dsv4-env.test.ts tests/cache-control-policy.test.ts tests/generation-defaults.test.ts tests/model-config-registry.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  -> `347 passed`.
- Generation defaults contract:
  `build/current-generation-defaults-contract-20260524-ui-cli-param-audit.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, `missing_markers=[]`.
- No-heavy panel settings contract:
  `build/current-panel-settings-contract-20260524-ui-cli-param-audit.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `missing_source_markers=[]`, typecheck rc=0.
- Focused Python source slice:
  `tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py tests/test_engine_audit.py tests/test_local_generation_metadata_audit.py -k ...`
  -> `51 passed, 385 deselected`.
- Packaged integrity contract:
  `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260524-ui-cli-param-audit.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`.

Important source findings:

- Max Output Tokens maps to `--max-tokens`; Max Context Tokens maps to `--max-prompt-tokens`; per-chat/API output caps stay separate.
- Panel startup does not emit `--default-temperature`, `--default-top-p`, `--default-top-k`, `--default-repetition-penalty`, or `--default-enable-thinking`; server resolves request > explicit CLI/default arg > bundle metadata > fallback.
- DSV4 uses one UI prefix switch: `DSV4 Native Composite Prefix Cache`. Pool codec is a separate DSV4-only codec control: `DSV4 CSA/HCA Pool Codec`. Generic paged-KV and generic q4/q8 controls are hidden/suppressed for DSV4.
- `additionalArgs` are filtered for DSV4 so stale incompatible flags cannot re-enable generic KV quant, native MTP, smelt, flash-moe, distributed, speculative, default sampling, or output/context overrides.
- Non-DSV4 explicit `additionalArgs` can still pass direct CLI defaults by design; that is user/direct override territory, not hidden panel behavior.
- Installed `/Applications/vMLX.app` and repo-local app both report `1.5.49` from Info.plist; if Eric is seeing .48 UI text, that is an older installed/open instance or updater state, not current source proof.

Open caveat: this is source/package/no-heavy proof, not a fresh live multi-turn model launch. The broad release proof still has the known DSV4 long-output/code exactness row open; do not conflate this wiring pass with DSV4 quality clearance.

## [2026-05-24 06:59 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Fixed Ollama thinking string false forcing reasoning on

Found a real API edge during the request-surface audit, separate from DSV4 quality:

- Electron gateway `applyOllamaThinking()` used `Boolean(parsed.enable_thinking)`.
- That means an Ollama-shaped request with `enable_thinking: "false"` forwarded `enable_thinking: true` and could also forward `reasoning_effort`.
- Python engine `ollama_adapter._apply_ollama_thinking()` also forwarded string `"false"` as a non-boolean value.
- This is not model-owned behavior and is exactly the kind of hidden rail flip Eric is worried about.

TDD proof:

- Added failing panel test in `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama-behavior.test.ts` for `/api/chat` and `/api/generate` with `enable_thinking: "false"` + `reasoning_effort: "high"`.
  - Before fix: `expected true to be false`.
- Added failing Python parity test in `tests/test_api_surface_parity.py`.
  - Before fix: adapter returned `enable_thinking: "false"` and kept `reasoning_effort`.

Fix:

- Added conservative boolean normalization in `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts` and `vmlx_engine/api/ollama_adapter.py`.
- Accepted values: bools, `true/false`, `1/0`, `yes/no`, `on/off`.
- Invalid/malformed values stay omitted; they do not force thinking on.
- `think` keeps precedence over `enable_thinking`, same as before.

Verification:

- Targeted red/green panel test -> passed.
- Targeted red/green Python test -> passed.
- Panel request/override compatibility slice:
  `npm --prefix panel test -- --run tests/api-gateway-ollama-behavior.test.ts tests/chat-override-policy.test.ts tests/chat-settings-compatibility.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  -> `32 passed`.
- Python API focused slice:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_api_surface_parity.py tests/test_engine_audit.py -k 'ollama or anthropic or chat_and_responses or responses_sampling or output_caps or cache_detail or max_tokens or reasoning_effort_preserves_bundle'`
  -> `40 passed, 395 deselected`.
- API surface contract:
  `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260524-ollama-thinking-bool-audit.json`
  -> `status=pass`, no missing nested or panel markers.
- `git diff --check` clean.

Next: commit/push this as a narrow API/request-surface fix. This does not clear DSV4 long-output/code exactness.

Commit/push follow-up:

- Committed as `d374bbb6 fix: normalize ollama thinking booleans`.
- Pushed to `origin/codex/pr-intake-manifest` and `origin/main`.
- Tracked worktree clean afterward; `.agents` updates are local/ignored coordination notes.
- No model server, pytest, vitest, release, or notary process is left running; only the installed vMLX UI process remains open.

  `self._stream_context()`.

## [2026-05-24 07:46 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Ling prompt-variant live probe narrows CJK leak shape

I ran a small live Ling JANGTQ prompt-variant probe from the bundled Python
engine and wired the resulting artifact into the Ling objective row.

Artifact:

- `build/current-ling-jangtq-russian-prompt-variant-probe-20260524.json`

Runtime facts:

- Model: `/Users/eric/models/JANGQ/Ling-2.6-flash-JANGTQ`
- Server args: bundled Python, `--reasoning-parser auto`,
  `--tool-call-parser auto`, `--enable-auto-tool-choice`,
  `--max-num-seqs 1`, no explicit default sampler flags.
- Env: `PYTHONNOUSERSITE=1`, `PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1`,
  `VMLX_DISABLE_SSM_PREFIX_RESUME=1`.
- Rendered prompt check before live run showed the Ling tokenizer template
  produced no CJK in the prompt. Template tail ends with
  `<role>ASSISTANT</role>`.
- Strict prior artifacts resolved to `temperature=0.0`, `top_p=1.0`,
  `max_tokens=220`, `enable_thinking=False`; model registry logs show
  `reasoning_parser=None`.

Prompt-variant results:

- `exact_game_prompt`: `cjk_chars=34`, mixed Russian then Chinese phrase
  around collision/target scoring.
- `simple_russian_game_no_html`: `cjk_chars=0`, Russian output.
- `explicit_system_russian_only`: `cjk_chars=4`, mostly Russian plus Chinese
  phrase for "a small river".

Current read:

- Not proven as reasoning/parser/max-output/cache. Prompt has no CJK, reasoning
  is not attached, strict artifacts use temp 0, and prefix resume was disabled
  in strict/probe paths.
- This looks prompt-sensitive and may involve Ling/Bailing language adherence,
  tokenizer/model priors around game-development concepts, or runtime/model
  quant behavior. Do not fix by forcing sampler defaults or banning CJK text in
  post-processing.

Gate wiring:

- Objective digest Ling row now includes the prompt-variant artifact.
- Refreshed digest reports Ling open with CJK in three artifacts:
  JANGTQ strict no-cache, MXFP4 strict no-cache, and prompt-variant probe.
- Focused tests:
  `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_packaged_integrity_contract.py tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py tests/test_current_regression_suite.py tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`

## [2026-05-24 10:10 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - non-DSV4 Additional Args sanitizer gap fixed in source

Found and fixed a real UI/app launch edge case:

- DSV4 sessions already stripped stale `additionalArgs` for app-owned flags,
  but ordinary text sessions only stripped image flags.
- That meant a saved Ling/Qwen/ZAYA/etc. session could append raw stale flags
  after the app-generated flags and override UI/autodetect policy, including:
  `--max-tokens`, `--max-prompt-tokens`, `--default-enable-thinking`,
  `--default-repetition-penalty`, `--reasoning-parser`, `--tool-call-parser`,
  `--enable-auto-tool-choice`, cache flags, speculative flags, and native-MTP
  flags.
- Added `TEXT_ADDITIONAL_ARG_BLOCKLIST` in both runtime launch
  `panel/src/main/sessions.ts` and preview
  `panel/src/renderer/src/components/sessions/SessionSettings.tsx`.
- The text blocklist keeps the raw-args escape hatch for non-app-owned args but
  prevents stale raw args from winning over first-class settings/autodetect.
- Added contracts:
  - `panel/tests/settings-flow.test.ts`: text additional args cannot override
    app-owned generation/parser/cache/MTP flags while explicit UI
    `maxTokens/maxContextLength` still emit once.
  - `tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py`: runtime and preview share the new
    text blocklist and value-consuming stripped flags skip their values.
  - release manifest row now names
    `build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260524-text-additional-args-sanitizer.json`.

Verification:

- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py`
  -> 8 passed.
- `npx vitest run tests/settings-flow.test.ts`
  -> 245 passed.
- `.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_noheavy_panel_settings_contract.py --out build/current-panel-settings-contract-proof-20260524-text-additional-args-sanitizer.json`
  -> status=pass; panel settings 303 passed; typecheck rc=0; registry rows
  green; engine CLI flag contract 8 passed.
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py -k 'panel_cache_family_gating or panel_cli_flag_contract'`
  -> 9 passed, 66 deselected.

Still open: this improves launch-policy safety, but it does not clear the
Ling/Bailing compat/Sequoia + MXFP4 live row or the DSV4 long-output/code
exactness row.

## [2026-05-24 10:13 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - sanitizer scope widened to all app-owned text launch flags

Follow-up audit found a second stale-raw-args override gap in the same lane:

- The first text-session sanitizer pass covered generation/parser/cache/MTP,
  but app-owned server/template/model/MCP flags could still be appended later
  through `additionalArgs`.
- A stale non-DSV4 text session could still override UI-owned values for:
  `--host`, `--port`, `--timeout`, `--rate-limit`, `--log-level`,
  `--allowed-origins`, `--served-model-name`, `--chat-template`,
  `--chat-template-kwargs`, `--mcp-config`, MCP allow/deny flags, `--api-key`,
  `--uds`, `--wake-timeout`, and `--inference-endpoints`.
- Expanded runtime + preview blocklists so image/general app-owned flags are
  blocked first, then text-specific app-owned flags are layered on top.
- Updated the settings-flow preview mirror; it had drifted and did not emit
  first-class `chatTemplate` even though real runtime/preview code does.
- Updated the Python blocklist extractor to resolve `...BLOCKLIST` spreads so
  future proof cannot miss inherited blocked flags.

  the UI-owned template, then passed after the sanitizer expansion.

  -> status=pass; panel settings 304 passed; typecheck rc=0; panel registry 57
  passed; engine registry 131 passed; engine CLI flag contract 8 passed.
- `.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_panel_cli_flag_contract.py tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py -k 'panel_cache_family_gating or panel_cli_flag_contract'`
  -> 9 passed, 66 deselected.

## [2026-05-24 10:34 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Ling current bundled cold CJK repro pinned; do not clear this row

I reran Ling/Bailing on the current bundled Python instead of relying on the older native-clean artifact.

Current bundled wheel state:

- `panel/bundled-python/python/bin/python3` has `mlx 0.31.2` tagged `cp312-cp312-macosx_14_0_arm64` and `mlx-metal 0.31.2` tagged `py3-none-macosx_14_0_arm64`.
- Source venv is `cp313-cp313-macosx_26_0_arm64`, so bundled/source wheel tags still differ.

Fresh live evidence:

- `build/current-production-family-live-ling-bundled-native-rerun-20260524.json`
  -> live `FAIL`, only failed check is `ling_multilingual_loop_trigger`.
  It returned 28 CJK chars at `finish=stop`, `reasoning_chars=0`, no loop score issue, resolved sampler in log was `temperature=0.6`, `top_p=0.95`, `max_tokens=420`, `enable_thinking=false`.
  Probe artifact: `/tmp/vmlx_family_audit/ling_flash_tq_ling_multilingual_game_prompt_1779643643.json`.
- `build/current-ling-jangtq-sampler-grid-bundled-native-20260524.json`
  showed omitted/default and explicit temp-0 cases resolve as `temperature=0.0/top_p=1.0` in logs, so this is not a hidden default-temperature override. Cache-hit rows can look clean, which is why narrow green artifacts are misleading.
- `build/current-ling-jangtq-cold-skipcache-repeat-bundled-native-20260524.json`
  -> 6/6 cold `skip_prefix_cache` repeats at `temperature=0.0`, `top_p=1.0`, `max_tokens=420` leaked the same 34 CJK chars.
- `build/current-ling-jangtq-cold-max220-repeat-bundled-native-20260524.json`
  -> 4/4 cold repeats at `temperature=0.0`, `top_p=1.0`, `max_tokens=220` leaked the same 34 CJK chars.
- `build/current-ling-jangtq-batchgen-temp0-repeat-bundled-native-20260524.json`
  -> `max_num_seqs=5` / upstream BatchGenerator also leaked 34 CJK chars, so this is not isolated to `SingleBatchGenerator`.

Interpretation / coordination:

- Do not clear `Ling/Bailing multilingual output quality is release-cleared` based on older cache-hit/native-clean artifacts.
- Do not frame this as reasoning leakage: `enable_thinking=false`, no reasoning content, and `finish=stop`.
- Do not frame it as max-output truncation: the failure appears before length pressure and reproduces at `max_tokens=220`.
- Do not frame it as a hidden sampler default: logs show temp/top-p resolved to `0.0/1.0` for the cold deterministic repro.
- The next useful boundary is direct `jang_tools`/loader generation in the same current bundled environment versus vMLX server rendering/runtime. If direct bundled generation is clean, trace prompt/render/cache/generator deltas. If direct bundled generation also leaks CJK, this is artifact/runtime/model-quality, not app settings.

I updated proof tracking so this cannot disappear:

- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
- `tests/cross_matrix/summarize_objective_proof.py`
- `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`
- `tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py`

## [2026-05-24 10:52 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Ling deterministic CJK root cause fixed; stochastic Ling row still open

Root cause boundary found and fixed for the deterministic bundled Ling CJK repro:

- Direct bundled `jang_tools.load_jangtq_model` + `mlx_lm.generate_step` was clean.
- Direct bundled `vmlx_engine.load_model_with_fallback` + `generate_step` was clean.
- Direct bundled `SingleBatchGenerator` was clean.
- Bundled server was failing because CLI defaulted `JANGTQ_MPP_NAX=auto`; direct `SimpleEngine.chat` reproduced the exact CJK failure when I set `JANGTQ_MPP_NAX=auto`.
- Source change: `vmlx_engine/cli.py` now resolves MPP/NAX `auto` to `off` for registry family `ling`. Explicit `JANGTQ_MPP_NAX=on` still works for diagnostics.
- Guard: `tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestJangTqMppNaxCliPolicy::test_jangtq_mpp_nax_cli_policy_disables_auto_for_ling`.

Bundled Python was reinstalled from local source and console script shebangs were restored to the relocatable sibling-python trampoline.

Passing proof after fix:

- `build/current-ling-jangtq-simple-engine-control-bundled-after-mpp-fix-20260524.json`
  -> bundled HTTP simple engine, temp=0/top_p=1, skip_prefix_cache, 0 CJK.
- `build/current-ling-jangtq-continuous-control-bundled-after-mpp-fix-20260524.json`
  -> bundled HTTP continuous batching, 3/3 cold skip_prefix_cache repeats, temp=0/top_p=1, 0 CJK.
- `npm run verify-bundled`
  -> passed after local reinstall + shebang rewrite.
- Focused tests:
  `tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestJangTqMppNaxCliPolicy`,
  `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_tracks_ling_multilingual_quality_open_row`,
  `tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py::test_objective_proof_digest_tracks_ling_multilingual_cjk_leakage`.

Do **not** clear the Ling/Bailing release row yet:

- `build/current-production-family-live-ling-bundled-after-mpp-fix-20260524.json`
  still fails only `ling_multilingual_loop_trigger`.
- The remaining failure is explicit stochastic sampling: `temperature=0.6`, `top_p=0.95`, `max_tokens=420`, `enable_thinking=false`, `finish=stop`, no reasoning content, but 32 CJK chars.
- Log confirms MPP/NAX was disabled for Ling in that run:
  `/tmp/vmlx_family_audit/ling_flash_tq_1779644953.log`
  contains `Ling/Bailing detected — disabling JANGTQ_MPP_NAX auto lane`.

Interpretation:

- Deterministic/default user path is fixed for the pinned Russian prompt.
- The broader Ling/Bailing multilingual quality row remains open because stochastic temperature still leaks CJK. Next agent should not re-argue max output, hidden defaults, reasoning leakage, or cache for the deterministic repro; those are ruled out. The next useful work is Ling family sampling policy/product UX for explicit nonzero temperature, or a model-quality clearance decision that scopes Ling away from higher-temp multilingual use.

## [2026-05-24 11:06 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - Ling/Bailing current bundled live row cleared with explicit top-k family policy

I finished the remaining Ling stochastic boundary and synced it into bundled Python.

Root cause / policy:

- The post-MPP failure was not reasoning, max-output, or cache. It was unconstrained stochastic sampling for Ling/Bailing when the bundle omits `top_k`.
- Added resolver-side policy in `vmlx_engine/server.py`: if registry family resolves to `ling`, and request/CLI/bundle did not provide `top_k`, resolve `top_k=20`.

## [2026-05-24 11:42 PDT] CODEX -> @other-agent - DSV4 exact-code blocker narrowed; source fix under live verification

Current DSV4 exact-code work:

- Root cause split from latest source artifact:
  - chat/responses normal exact-code rows now preserve identifiers after normalization, but still return a single markdown code fence.
  - `chat_max` prompt wording still corrupts identifiers.
  - legacy `/v1/completions` bypasses DSV4 chat templating entirely and uses raw `engine.generate(prompt=...)`, which is unsafe for DSV4.
- Added red/green contracts in `tests/test_server.py`:
  - DSV4 legacy completions must route through `engine.chat(...)` with `enable_thinking=True` / `chat_template_kwargs.enable_thinking=True`.
  - strict exact-code/no-markdown prompts unwrap only a whole single code fence and leave mixed prose+fence text unchanged.
- Source changes in `vmlx_engine/server.py`:
  - added DSV4 detection helper for loaded model.
  - added strict finalization helper that unwraps a whole single code fence only when the request explicitly asks for exact code/no markdown.
  - applied finalization to chat completions, Responses API, and completions.
  - routed non-streaming DSV4 `/v1/completions` through the strict chat rail instead of raw prompt generation.

Verification so far:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_server.py -k 'dsv4_completion_endpoint_uses_chat_rail or strict_no_markdown_request_unwraps'
# 2 passed

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_server.py tests/test_reasoning_modes.py -k 'dsv4 or completion_endpoint or strict_no_markdown or logprobs'
# 38 passed, 89 deselected

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py
# 5 passed
```

Next: running the live DSV4 exactness probe. Do not call release cleared until that artifact shows the DSV4 row clears; the known risk is `chat_max` may still need a separate prompt/rail fix even after fence/completions fixes.

## [2026-05-24 12:55 PDT] from: codex -> @claude
DSV4 completion row fixed live; only `chat_max` remains in route-mode matrix.

Root-cause probes:

- `chat_max_rep1` still corrupts identifiers, so repetition penalty is not the cause.
- `chat_max_thinking_on` still corrupts identifiers; `chat_max_effort_max` is worse.
- DSV4 exact-code is sensitive to trailing whitespace before the assistant rail:
  - chat canonical prompt with trailing newline corrupts `WebGLRenderer`;
  - completion canonical prompt without trailing newline is exact.
- Legacy completion at 220 tokens still length-stopped because hidden reasoning consumes the strict-rail budget; after trimming prompt and giving an internal DSV4 exact-copy budget floor, the live completion row is exact.

Runtime/source change:

- DSV4 `/v1/completions` exact/no-markdown prompts are `rstrip()`ed before chat-rail rendering.
- DSV4 `/v1/completions` exact/no-markdown prompts use an internal `max_tokens >= 512` floor so hidden reasoning does not consume the user-visible exact-code budget.
- This is scoped to loaded DSV4 + legacy completions + exact/no-markdown requests. No sampler defaults changed, no global reasoning policy changed.

Fresh live artifact:

- `build/current-dsv4-route-mode-code-exactness-source-after-completion-trim-budget-liveapi-20260524-1248.json`
- `status=fail`
- 9/10 cases exact.
- Only remaining failure: `chat_max`, unfenced but still corrupts `PerspectiveCamera`, `BoxGeometry`, `MeshBasicMaterial` (`BBoxGeometry`, `MMeshBasicMaterial`).


Result at 13:16 PDT:

- Live artifact:
  `build/current-dsv4-chatmax-budget-stop-rail-live-20260524-1309.json`
- Refreshed objective digest:
  `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260524-post-chatmax-budget-stop-rail.json`

Findings:

- `chatmax_512_baseline`, `chatmax_1024_budget`,
  `chatmax_1024_stop_roles`, `chatmax_1024_thinking_on`,
  `chatmax_1024_effort_low`, `responses_chatmax_1024`, and
  `completion_chatmax_512` all fail exactness.
- Every failed row produced the same 445 completion tokens and the same visible
  corruption shape:
  `PersPerspectiveCamera`, `BBoxGeometry`, `MMeshBasicMaterial`.
- Larger output budget is ruled out.
- Role stop strings are ruled out.
- Requested thinking-on and normalized low->high effort are ruled out.
- Responses and completions adapters are ruled out; this reproduces across all
  three public surfaces for the same wording.
- Effective rail remains `thinking_open`; `low` normalizes to `high` by current
  DSV4 encoder contract, which is already covered in
  `test_dsv4_reasoning_effort_preserves_requested_rails`.

Conclusion:

- This is not max-output pressure, stop handling, route adapter behavior, cache
  overlap, requested thinking toggle, or simple rep-pen/default forcing.
- It remains prompt/instruction-conditioned DSV4 identifier corruption under the
  current runtime/model stack.
- Do not "fix" by rewriting user prompts or substituting identifiers. The
  remaining release blocker is real:
  `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`.
