## [2026-05-27 20:03 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Local-only EPIPE and non-MiMo matrix follow-up.

Eric asked why `unexpected error write EPIPE` was still recurring. Current
root-cause work found two concrete local issues:

- old real-UI proof runs had left proof-owned Electron dev children under
  temp `vmlx-real-ui-userdata-*` dirs; current process table is now clean for
  vMLX proof Electron/node/server children;
- gateway passthrough still used `proxyRes.pipe(clientRes)` on non-Ollama
  OpenAI/Responses/cache/audio/MCP routes, which bypassed the guarded
  `writeResponse()` path when a client disconnected mid-stream.

Changed:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts`: direct passthrough now forwards
  `proxyRes.on("data")` through `writeResponse(clientRes, chunk)` and ends via
  `endResponse(clientRes)`, so EPIPE/ECONNRESET/stream-destroyed client
  disconnects follow the same guard as Ollama streaming.
- `panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs`: proof cleanup now walks the
  child process tree with `ps -axo pid=,ppid=` and sends SIGTERM/SIGKILL to
  descendants plus the process group, reducing orphaned Electron/dev-server
  proof children after failures or timeouts.
- `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts` and
  `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py` have red/green regressions for
  the guarded proxy path and descendant cleanup.

MiniMax Small local-only Responses/tools/cache-controls proof is now wired:

`docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-minimax-m27-small-responses-tools-cachecontrols-localonly-20260527-proof.json`

Regenerated manifest:

`build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260527-localonly-nonmimo-minimax-ling-nemotron-epipe.json`

Findings/fixes:

- ZAYA-VL point/box tokens are visual-grounding control spans, not chat text.
  `vmlx_engine/server.py` now strips `<|point_start|>`, `<|point_end|>`,
  `<|box_start|>`, and `<|box_end|>` spans from visible streaming and final
  text. The release gate still rejects raw point/box leakage.
- `vmlx_engine/tool_parsers/zaya_tool_parser.py` now handles malformed live
  ZAYA output where a new `<parameter=...>` starts before the previous
  `</parameter>`.
- Server tool filtering now schema-gates ZAYA-style `create_file` to vMLX
  `write_file` only when `write_file` is in the request tools and path/content
  args can be recovered.

## [2026-05-27 20:03 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Local-only API gateway EPIPE hardening; do not overclaim release clearance.

Eric reported continued `unexpected error write EPIPE`. I did not use max2 and
did not work MiMo. Local log search did not find a current vMLX EPIPE stack;
the visible `unexpected error` hits under `~/Library/Logs` were Warp DNS/auth
noise. Still, the gateway had a real remaining disconnect risk: the prior
guarding covered Ollama streaming conversion, but not every response boundary.

Patched `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts`:

- attach a response error guard for every gateway request
- classify `EPIPE`, `ECONNRESET`, `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`, and matching
  disconnect messages as client disconnects
- route CORS/root/HEAD/liveness JSON/NDJSON writes through guarded
  `writeHeadResponse`, `writeResponse`, `endResponse`, and `writeJsonLine`
- replace generic `proxyRes.pipe(clientRes)` with explicit guarded byte
  forwarding so passthrough SSE/JSON responses cannot write after client close

Durable gate:

- `tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py` now runs
  `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts` and exposes
  `panel_gateway_streaming_disconnect_epipe_guard`
- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py` requires that named API
  surface check

- `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260527-issues-175-178-bundled-sync.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `panel_gateway_streaming_disconnect_epipe_guard=true`
  - `missing_panel_markers=[]`
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260527-release-blocker-ledger.json`
  - top-level `status=pass`
  - `current_proof_sweep=fail`
  - API EPIPE check is passing, but release remains blocked by the open proof
    sweep items outside this EPIPE slice.

## [2026-05-28 00:12 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Remaining installed Ollama EPIPE path fixed and installed.

Boundary:

- local-only in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`
- no Swift
- no `/Users/eric/vmlx`
- no max2 testing

Root cause:

- The prior EPIPE fix covered generic gateway passthrough plus most Ollama
  streaming writes.
- A remaining `/api/chat` Ollama final tool/finish JSON write still ignored
  the guarded `writeJsonLine()` return. On client disconnect that could leave
  the backend stream alive after a failed client write.
- Installed app initially proved stale/incomplete: `app.asar` had 3 Ollama
  streaming JSON write sites but only 2 guarded.

Fix:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts` now checks that final Ollama chat streaming
  `writeJsonLine()` result and destroys `proxyRes` on disconnect.
- `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts` requires every same-line Ollama
  streaming `this.writeJsonLine(res, ...)` call to be guarded.
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_installed_app_runtime_parity_audit.py` adds
  `installed_panel_ollama_streaming_json_writes_guarded`, with installed
  write-count diagnostics.
- Release proof validation requires the new installed check.

- Ran `panel/scripts/build-and-install.sh`.
- It rebuilt bundled Python, verified bundled `vmlx_engine`/`jang_tools`
  parity, built Electron, copied to `/Applications/vMLX.app`, and verified the
  local app signature.
- Re-ran installed parity after install:
  `build/current-installed-app-runtime-parity-audit-20260527.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `missing_or_stale=[]`, Ollama write counts `3/3`.

Fresh release-board artifacts:

- `build/current-regression-suite-20260527-local-only-mimo-boundary.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed_steps=[]`.
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-installed-ollama-epipe-parity.json`

## [2026-05-28 01:35 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Installed EPIPE multiline guard is now proven in the local Python app.

Boundary:

- local-only in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`
- no Swift
- no `/Users/eric/vmlx`
- no max2 testing
- no MiMo generation/root-cause work in this lane

Root cause for the remaining `write EPIPE` surface:

- Prior gateway hardening covered raw proxy/response forwarding and some
  Ollama streaming writes.
- The durable test only counted same-line `this.writeJsonLine(res, ...)`
  calls, so it missed multiline writes.
- Actual source had 6 Ollama streaming JSON writes and only 3 were guarded.

Fix:

- `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts` now counts multiline raw/guarded
  `writeJsonLine(res, ...)` calls.
- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts` now guards all 6 Ollama streaming JSON writes
  in final/end paths.
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_installed_app_runtime_parity_audit.py` uses the same
  multiline count so installed app parity cannot pass with half the writes
  unguarded.
- Rebuilt and installed `/Applications/vMLX.app`.

## [2026-05-28 01:58 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

#179 reporter parity collector is now available.

Boundary:

- local-only in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`
- no Swift
- no `/Users/eric/vmlx`
- no max2 testing
- no MiMo generation/root-cause work in this lane

Change:

- Added `tests/cross_matrix/run_issue179_reporter_parity_metadata.py`.
- Added `tests/test_issue179_reporter_parity_metadata.py`.
- Wired the collector into current-suite source hashing, focused pytest, and
  the release manifest row for the real UI/model issue boundary.

Purpose:

The #179 audit now requires reporter-side parity metadata, and this collector
is the no-heavy way to produce that package on the reporter/session machine. It
does not load the model. It records:

- installed `server.py` sha256;
- Responses cancel route marker;
- engine abort-call marker;
- model manifest sha256;
- model file hashes from the MiniMax-K manifest;
- chat id;
- session settings JSON;
- response id;
- whether that response id was active at cancel time;
- raw SSE/cancel lifecycle JSON.

Local template run:

```sh
.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_issue179_reporter_parity_metadata.py \
  --out build/current-issue179-reporter-parity-metadata-template-20260528.json
# status=open
# missing_fields=[
#   "chat_id",
#   "session_settings",
#   "response_id",
#   "response_active_at_cancel",
#   "raw_sse_cancel_lifecycle"
# ]
```

Reporter-side intended shape:

```sh
.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_issue179_reporter_parity_metadata.py \
  --model-manifest build/current-issue179-minimax-k-local-model-manifest-20260527.json \
  --chat-id 33a744d8 \
  --session-settings-json /path/to/reporter-session-settings.json \
  --response-id resp_66d7e36b833e \
  --response-active-at-cancel false \
  --raw-sse-cancel-lifecycle-json /path/to/reporter-sse-cancel-lifecycle.json \
  --out build/issue-179/reporter-parity-metadata-20260527.json
```

Fresh artifacts:

```sh

  --skip-release-gate \
  --out build/current-regression-suite-20260528-reporter-parity-collector.json

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \

  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-reporter-parity-collector.json
```

  - reporter cancel route and engine abort markers;

  - reporter model file hashes against local model file hashes;

  - reporter response id against the submitted log;
  - response active-state is recorded;
  - raw SSE/cancel lifecycle is present.

  artifacts missing `reporter_parity_comparison`.

Root-cause update:

- Prior EPIPE work guarded outgoing response writes, generic byte forwarding,
  and Ollama streaming JSON writes.
- Remaining gap was Ollama-specific backend response streams: `/api/chat`,
  `/api/generate`, and `/api/embeddings` did not attach
  `proxyRes.on("error")` disconnect guards.
- Added those guards in `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts`; EPIPE, ECONNRESET,
  and ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED now stay treated as client disconnects at that
  proxy-response boundary too.

- panel test marker:
  `guards every Ollama backend response stream error as a disconnect boundary`
- API surface check:
  `panel_gateway_ollama_proxy_response_error_guard=true`

Issue #179 reporter parity shape guard tightened. Keep this invariant.

Boundary:

- local-only in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`
- no Swift
- no `/Users/eric/vmlx`
- no max2 testing
- no MiMo work; user said MiMo is broken/deferred for now

What changed:

- `tests/cross_matrix/run_issue179_reporter_parity_metadata.py` no longer
  accepts arbitrary `session_settings` / `raw_sse_cancel_lifecycle` objects.
  Reporter parity now requires the exact MiniMax issue shape:
  - `wireApi=responses`
  - `detectedFamily=minimax`
  - `route=/v1/responses`
  - `stream=true`
  - `sessionHasReasoningParser=true`
  - sampling `{temperature:1.0, top_p:0.95, top_k:40, max_tokens:4096}`
  - raw lifecycle proves `ECONNRESET`, `fullContentLen=0`,
    `readerAcquired=true`, abort before visible content, and cancel 404 after
    the same response-id stream abort
  - lifecycle `response_active_at_cancel`, when present, must match the
    top-level field
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_issue179_minimax_k_root_cause_audit.py` now treats
  collector-side `missing_fields` as a hard open state even if someone
  hand-edits `status=pass`.
- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py` accepts
  `collector_missing_fields` as a valid #179 open proof state and requires the
  accompanying list.

## [2026-05-28 03:40 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

EPIPE/socket-destroy guard is now installed in `/Applications/vMLX.app` and the
current proof sweep has been refreshed.

Boundary:

- local-only in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`
- no Swift
- no `/Users/eric/vmlx`
- no max2 testing
- no MiMo work; Eric explicitly said MiMo is broken/deferred for now

Root cause for the remaining EPIPE class:

- Earlier gateway guards covered raw response writes and proxy response errors,
  but `responseWritable()` / `requestWritable()` still only checked high-level
  stream flags.
- A response/request can have `socket.destroyed === true` while the helper still
  considers it writable, so writes after client/backend disconnect can still
  produce EPIPE.

Source/installed fix:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts` now treats `writableDestroyed` and
  `socket?.destroyed` as non-writable for gateway responses and proxied backend
  requests.
- `panel/tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts` covers:
  - destroyed client response sockets;
  - destroyed backend request sockets.
- `/Applications/vMLX.app` was rebuilt/reinstalled with
  `./panel/scripts/build-and-install.sh`.
- strict codesign verify passed.
- packaged and installed `app.asar` were extracted and both contain
  `writableDestroyed` and `socket?.destroyed`.

Fresh artifacts:

```sh
build/current-api-surface-contract-20260528-epipe-socket-guard.json
# status=pass

build/current-installed-app-runtime-parity-audit-20260528-epipe-socket-guard.json
# status=pass, missing_or_stale=[]

build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260528-epipe-socket-guard-installed-sync.json
# status=pass, failed=[]

build/current-regression-suite-20260528-epipe-socket-guard.json
# status=pass, failed_steps=[]

build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-epipe-installed-sync.json
# status=pass, current_proof_sweep=pass
```

Do not claim release cleared. Current suite open rows remain:

- `Cross-family live multi-turn smoke matrix is release-cleared`
- `Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared`
- `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`

Ledger blockers still include MiMo deferred rows, #179 MiniMax-K reporter
parity, real UI MiMo missing, and real UI DSV4 memory blocked. If EPIPE still
appears in the now-installed app, capture the exact stack/log path before
changing code again; the old raw write/proxy/socket helper path is covered.

## [2026-05-28 04:33 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

#179 cancel-order causality narrowed.

Boundary: local-only in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
No Swift, no `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2 testing, no MiMo work, no model load.

The reporter lifecycle already proves the cancel 404 followed stream abort on
the same response id:

- `responses_cancel_404_after_request_error=true`

## [2026-05-28 04:58 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Gateway EPIPE request-end gap fixed and proven.

Boundary: local-only in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
No Swift, no `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2 testing, no MiMo work.

Root cause for the latest "still write EPIPE" path: the earlier response-side
EPIPE guard covered streamed response writes, but backend request finalization
still had raw `proxyReq.end()` calls and the cancel-broadcast path still had raw
`cancelReq.end()`. A socket could die after guarded `write()` but before raw
`end()`, leaving another EPIPE path.

Fix:

- added `endProxyRequest()` alongside `writeProxyBody()`;
- routed generic proxy, Ollama chat/generate/embed, and cancel-broadcast request
  finalization through it;
- added regressions for destroyed backend sockets and for absence of raw
  `proxyReq.end()` / `cancelReq.end()` calls outside the helper;
- moved API/current-suite/release-manifest proof pointers to the new artifact.

Fresh proof:

```sh
build/current-api-surface-contract-20260528-epipe-request-end-guard.json

# server_api_surface: 27 passed
# panel_api_request_builders: 123 passed

build/current-regression-suite-20260528-epipe-request-end-guard.json
# status=pass
# failed_steps=[]
# open_requirements=[
#   "Cross-family live multi-turn smoke matrix is release-cleared",
#   "Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared",
#   "DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared"
# ]

build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-epipe-request-end-guard.json
# status=pass
# current_proof_sweep=pass
```

## [2026-05-28 05:28 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

IPC EPIPE request-finalization guard is source-proven and wired into the
current proof sweep.

Boundary: local-only in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
No Swift, no `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2 testing, no MiMo work.

Root cause extension: the prior gateway fix guarded response writes and gateway
backend request finalization, but chat IPC and image IPC still had raw backend
request paths:

- `panel/src/main/ipc/chat.ts`: raw `req.end(bodyBuf)`;
- `panel/src/main/ipc/image.ts`: raw `req.write(bodyStr)` / `req.end()` in
  image generation, edit, and cancel.

Current source fix:

- `endChatBackendRequest()` checks backend request/socket writability and
  catches `EPIPE`, `ECONNRESET`, and `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`;
- image IPC now uses `writeImageServerRequestBody()`,
  `endImageServerRequest()`, and `writeAndEndImageServerRequest()`;
- image error classification maps `EPIPE` and `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED` to the
  existing connection-lost user message instead of leaking raw Node text.

Fresh proof:

```sh
cd panel && npx vitest run \
  tests/image-system.test.ts \
  tests/image-generation-state.test.ts \
  tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts \
  --reporter=verbose

cd panel && npm run typecheck
# pass

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py \
  --out build/current-api-surface-contract-20260528-ipc-epipe-request-guard.json
# status=pass
# server_api_surface: 27 passed
# panel_api_request_builders: 244 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_packaged_integrity_contract.py \
  --out build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260528-ipc-epipe-request-guard.json
# status=pass

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
  --skip-release-gate \
  --out build/current-regression-suite-20260528-ipc-epipe-request-guard.json

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
```

## [2026-05-28 06:39 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

DSV4 source preflight case guard added.

Boundary: local-only in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
No Swift, no `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2 testing, and no DSV4 launch.

Problem: the release validator required DSV4 source exactness memory preflight
to be skipped for insufficient memory, but did not require that the skip cover
the intended direct/off source cases. A too-narrow skipped artifact could have
satisfied the boundary.

Fix:

- `_validate_dsv4_open_requirement_details` now requires
  `current_source_full_output_preflight.selected_cases` to include:
  `chat_off_rep1`, `chat_off_no_punct_rep1`, `responses_off_rep1`,
  `responses_off_no_punct_rep1`, with `case_count >= 4`.
- Added regression
  `test_release_regression_manifest_rejects_narrow_dsv4_source_preflight_cases`.
- Current suite now writes embedded manifest output to
  `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-dsv4-0625-preflight-caseguard.json`.

Fresh proof:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_current_regression_suite.py::test_current_regression_suite_refreshes_release_regression_manifest \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_rejects_narrow_dsv4_source_preflight_cases \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_validates_current_proof_sweep_artifacts
# 3 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
  --skip-release-gate \
  --out build/current-regression-suite-20260528-dsv4-0625-preflight.json
# status=pass
# failed_steps=[]

build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-dsv4-0625-preflight-caseguard.json
# status=pass
# current_proof_sweep=pass
```

Direct inspection confirms the nested DSV4 preflight has `case_count=4` and
selected cases exactly:
`chat_off_rep1`, `chat_off_no_punct_rep1`, `responses_off_rep1`,
`responses_off_no_punct_rep1`.

## [2026-05-28 07:56 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Installed app now contains the expanded EPIPE fixes.

Boundary: local Python/panel worktree only. No deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`,
no Swift, no max2 testing, no MiMo investigation.

Root cause for the still-visible `write EPIPE` class was broader than the first
gateway patch:

- Ollama streaming conversion paths had guarded writes but did not abort their
  backend response stream when the client response closed.
- Chat IPC still had raw backend disconnect/error paths that could surface
  `write EPIPE`, `EPIPE`, or `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`.

Implemented source and durable coverage for:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts`: client-close abort helper wired into
  generic proxy plus Ollama chat/generate/embeddings proxy callbacks.
- `panel/src/main/ipc/chat.ts`: backend disconnect classification now covers
  `EPIPE`, `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`, literal `write EPIPE`, and stream error
  handler classification.
- Panel tests, API surface contract, current suite, packaged integrity, and
  release manifest pointers now pin the new proof.

## [2026-05-28 10:02 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

EPIPE top-level gateway handler is fixed and built into current app bundle
content; release signing is blocked.

Boundary: local Python/panel worktree only. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no model loads.

Root cause found after the prior EPIPE guard pass: response/proxy writes were
guarded, but `_tryListen()` still had a top-level
`this.handleRequest(req, res).catch(...)` that logged every thrown error as
`[gateway] Unhandled request error`. A disconnect-shaped throw reaching that
boundary could still surface as an unexpected gateway error.

Fix:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts` now routes the catch through
  `handleRequestError(err, res)`.
- Disconnect-shaped `EPIPE`, `ECONNRESET`, `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`,
  `write EPIPE`, socket hangup/reset, and destroyed-stream failures are
  suppressed at that boundary.
- Non-disconnect failures still log and return the existing 500 JSON when
  possible.

New proof artifacts:

```text
build/current-api-surface-contract-20260528-epipe-top-handler.json
  status=pass
  panel_gateway_streaming_disconnect_epipe_guard=true
  panel_gateway_ollama_proxy_response_error_guard=true
  panel_ipc_backend_request_epipe_guard=true

build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260528-epipe-top-handler.json
  status=pass

build/current-regression-suite-20260528-epipe-top-handler.json
  status=pass
  failed_steps=[]
  open_requirements=[
    Cross-family live multi-turn smoke matrix is release-cleared,
    Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared,
    DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared,
  ]

build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-epipe-top-handler.json
  current_proof_sweep=pass
```

Additional verification:

```text
panel selected API/gateway tests: 249 passed
npm run typecheck: pass
npm run build: pass
rebuilt panel/dist/main/index.mjs contains handleRequestError
npm run package: app content built, extracted app.asar contains handleRequestError
py_compile + git diff --check: clean
```

Important release blocker: `npm run package` is not shippable yet because
Developer ID signing failed on:

```text
panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app/Contents/Resources/bundled-python/python/lib/python3.12/site-packages/scipy/sparse/linalg/_eigen/arpack/_arpacklib.cpython-312-darwin.so
codesign: errSecInternalComponent
```

Direct repro on the same file returns `errSecInternalComponent`.
`security show-keychain-info` for `vmlx-build`, `build`, and `login` keychains
returns `User interaction is not allowed`. The app bundle content has the EPIPE
fix, but notarized release is blocked on noninteractive keychain/signing access.

Do not call release cleared. Current release blockers remain the known live
matrix rows plus DSV4 exactness; signing is an additional packaging blocker for
actually shipping this rebuilt app.

- Developer ID identities are visible (`Developer ID Application: ShieldStack
  LLC (55KGF2S5AY)` appears 9 times in the current identity scan).

  fails with `errSecInternalComponent`.

## [2026-05-28 11:39 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Ollama embeddings gateway timeout/EPIPE lifecycle gap is fixed and promoted.

Boundary: local Python/panel worktree only. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no model loads, no MiMo heavy testing.

Root cause:

- Prior EPIPE work guarded streaming writes and generic/Ollama proxy response
  disconnects.
- `/api/embeddings` still differed from chat/generate: it proxied to
  `/v1/embeddings` without `effectiveGatewayProxyTimeoutMs(...)` and without a
  `proxyReq.on("timeout")` destroy/504 path.
- That left embeddings with a longer-lived backend request lifecycle than the
  guarded routes.

Change:

- Added panel contract:
  `applies gateway timeout handling to Ollama embeddings proxy requests`.
- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts` now sets embeddings proxy timeout with
  `this.effectiveGatewayProxyTimeoutMs(session, parsed)`.
- Embeddings now destroys the backend request on timeout and returns guarded
  504 only if the client response is still writable.
- Promoted the marker into `tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py`
  and advanced the current suite/manifest pointers.

Fresh artifacts:

```text

  status=pass
  missing_panel_markers=[]
  panel_api_request_builders: 252 passed

build/current-regression-suite-20260528-ollama-embedding-timeout.json
  status=pass
  failed_steps=[]
  open_requirements=[
    Cross-family live multi-turn smoke matrix is release-cleared,
    Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared,
    DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared,
  ]

build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-current-cohesive-audit.json
  status=pass
  current_proof_sweep=pass
  release_clearance.release_ready=false
```

```text
npm test -- --run \

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py \

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
```

regressions and "not skipping issues." The existing cache architecture matrix
proved cache archetypes, but it did not publish named-family rows for several
real release families. That let a future generic-family fallback hide behind a
passing cache archetype artifact.

  - points current suite proof at:

  still treated legacy paths as missing current evidence:

  - `build/dsv4-chat-prompt-ablation-20260520101331/result.json`

  instead of the current DSV4 exactness boundary.

  under `legacy_clearance_artifacts`.
- They no longer appear under `missing_clearance_artifacts`.
- Legacy DSV4 clearance artifacts do not count as current release evidence, so
  the row remains open unless current exactness artifacts pass.
- `tests/test_objective_proof_digest.py` now has a regression test for this.
- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py` now points at the fresh
  suite artifact:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260528-dsv4-legacy-clearance-cleanup.json`.

## [2026-05-28 13:38 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

I fixed a real release-pipeline logic bug in the prior DMG gate.

Boundary: Python worktree only
`/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no MiMo runtime load.

Problem found:

- Strict `--require-release-ready` before DMG build is circular.
- `packaged_app_developer_id_signing_blocked` can only be cleared after a
  signed package/DMG exists, so pre-DMG build must not require that blocker to
  already be cleared.

Fix:

- Added `--require-prepackage-ready` to
  `tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py`.
- It requires proof sweep pass and no open objective rows.
- It allows only packaging/signing blockers such as
  `packaged_app_developer_id_signing_blocked`.
- It still rejects model/proof blockers before any public DMG build.
- Final `--require-release-ready` remains strict and still blocks until signing
  and all model/proof blockers are cleared.
- `panel/scripts/build-release-dmgs.sh` and `npm run dist` now use the
  prepackage gate. `npm run release:ready` remains the final strict gate.

Fresh verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_current_regression_suite.py::test_current_regression_suite_refreshes_release_regression_manifest \
  tests/test_current_regression_suite.py::test_current_regression_suite_refreshes_current_packaged_integrity_artifact \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_tracks_current_post_budget_edge_proof_sweep \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_current_sweep_uses_latest_live_smoke_artifacts \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_runner_prepackage_allows_only_packaging_blockers \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_runner_prepackage_rejects_model_blockers \
  tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestStartupCompatibilityGuards::test_release_build_declares_sequoia_and_tahoe_dmg_flavors
# 7 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
  --skip-release-gate \
  --out build/current-regression-suite-20260528-prepackage-gate.json
# status=pass failed_steps=[]

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --require-current-proof-sweep \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-prepackage-gate.json
# current_proof_sweep=pass prepackage_ready=false release_ready=false

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --require-prepackage-ready \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-prepackage-gate-check.json
# rc=1 current_proof_sweep=pass prepackage_ready=false release_ready=false

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --require-release-ready \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-prepackage-gate-strict.json
# rc=1 current_proof_sweep=pass prepackage_ready=false release_ready=false

npm run release:prepackage
# rc=1 current_proof_sweep=pass prepackage_ready=false release_ready=false

VMLINUX_RELEASE_OUTPUT_DIR=/tmp/vmlx-release-gate-bypass-proof \
VMLX_PREPACKAGE_READY_MANIFEST_OUT=$PWD/build/current-release-regression-manifest-pre-dmg-release-build-proof.json \
panel/scripts/build-release-dmgs.sh sequoia

```

## [2026-05-28 14:00 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

EPIPE follow-up: source had one more real wrapper gap.

Boundary: local Python/panel worktree only. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no MiMo runtime load.

Finding:

- The previous gateway guard handled direct `EPIPE`, `ECONNRESET`,
  `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`, `ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END`, matching messages, and
  nested `cause`.
- It did not inspect `AggregateError.errors`.
- Node can surface socket failures as an AggregateError whose top-level message
  is not the raw socket message while a nested error is `write EPIPE`.
- That can still reach the global unexpected-error dialog.

Fix:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts`: `isClientDisconnectError()` now recursively
  checks nested `errors[]`.
- `panel/src/main/index.ts`: `isExpectedClientDisconnectError()` now does the
  same before showing the crash dialog.
- `panel/tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts`: covers
  `AggregateError([EPIPE])`.
- `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts`: source guard asserts the global
  nested-errors path.

Verification:

```sh
npm test -- --run \
  tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts \
  tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts
# 44 passed

npm run typecheck
# pass

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py \
  --out build/current-api-surface-contract-20260528-epipe-aggregate-guard.json
# status=pass failed=[]

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_current_regression_suite.py::test_current_regression_suite_refreshes_release_regression_manifest \
  tests/test_current_regression_suite.py::test_current_regression_suite_refreshes_current_packaged_integrity_artifact \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_current_sweep_uses_latest_live_smoke_artifacts \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_tracks_current_post_budget_edge_proof_sweep
# 4 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
  --skip-release-gate \
  --out build/current-regression-suite-20260528-epipe-aggregate-guard.json
# status=pass failed_steps=[]

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --require-current-proof-sweep \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-epipe-aggregate-guard.json
# current_proof_sweep=pass prepackage_ready=false release_ready=false
```

Prepackage and strict gates still correctly return `rc=1` because release
blockers remain.

Important installed-app boundary:

`/Applications/vMLX.app/Contents/Resources/app.asar` is stale for this fix:
it contains older `write EPIPE` handling but not `nestedErrors` or
`ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END`. If Eric is running the installed app, he can still
see the old dialog until a rebuilt/synced app is installed. I did not overwrite
`/Applications/vMLX.app` with an unsigned partial build.

Release is not cleared.
---

## [2026-05-28 18:46 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

DSV4 exactness memory blocker now carries concrete no-launch evidence.

Boundary: Python/panel proof worktree only:

  recorded `reason=insufficient_free_memory` and telemetry, but the artifact and
  release blocker did not expose `available_gb`, `memory_gap_gb`,
  `did_not_launch`, or `launch_decision` directly.
- That made the DSV4 exactness blocker too vague for RAM-safe release triage.

Fix after red tests:

- `run_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py` now writes top-level
  `available_gb`, `memory_gap_gb`, `did_not_launch=true`, and
  `launch_decision=do_not_launch` when the min-free preflight blocks launch.
- `summarize_objective_proof.py` carries those fields into
  `current_source_full_output_preflight`, computing a gap for older artifacts.
- `release_regression_manifest.py` attaches those details to
  `dsv4_long_output_code_exactness_open`.

  `details.did_not_launch=true`,

## [2026-05-28 18:59 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Current suite DSV4 exactness preflight is now explicitly no-launch.

Boundary: Python/panel proof worktree only:
`/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no DSV4 model launch.

Finding:

- The DSV4 exactness runner had a memory floor, but if memory exceeded that
  floor it would continue into the live DSV4 launch.
- That is unsafe for the no-heavy current suite: the suite should refresh the
  memory/case inventory artifact, not opportunistically load DSV4.

Fix after red tests:

- Added `--memory-preflight-only` to
  `tests/cross_matrix/run_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py`.
- In this mode the runner records either:
  - `status=skipped`, `launch_decision=do_not_launch` when below floor; or
  - `status=ready_to_launch`, `launch_decision=launch_allowed` when above floor;
  but it always sets `did_not_launch=true` and never spawns the DSV4 server.
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py` now has a
  `dsv4_source_exactness_memory_preflight` step using this mode and the current
  post-install-sync artifact path.

Fresh evidence:

```text

returncode=0
command=.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py --memory-preflight-only --out build/current-dsv4-route-mode-code-exactness-source-memory-preflight-20260528-post-install-sync.json

artifact=build/current-dsv4-route-mode-code-exactness-source-memory-preflight-20260528-post-install-sync.json
status=skipped
reason=insufficient_free_memory
case_count=14
did_not_launch=true
launch_decision=do_not_launch
memory_gap_gb=14.16
```

Verification:

```sh

  tests/test_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py::test_dsv4_code_exactness_probe_preflight_only_never_spawns_when_ready \

  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_validates_current_proof_sweep_artifacts
# 5 passed
```

## [2026-05-28 23:11 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

EPIPE/client-disconnect guard tightened for Node `closed` stream state.

Boundary: Python/panel proof worktree only:
`/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no DSV4 launch, no MiMo live generation, no
keychain mutation, no notarization attempt.

Root-cause slice:

- Existing guards covered `destroyed`, `writableEnded`, `writableDestroyed`,
  and `socket.destroyed`.
- Node can mark `ServerResponse` / `ClientRequest` as `closed` before those
  other fields catch up. Late `write()` / `end()` calls in that state can still
  produce noisy `write EPIPE`.

Patch:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts`
  - `responseWritable()` now rejects `closed: true`.
  - `requestWritable()` now rejects `closed: true`.
- `panel/src/main/ipc/chat.ts`
  - `chatBackendRequestWritable()` now rejects `closed: true`.
- `panel/src/main/ipc/image.ts`
  - `imageServerRequestWritable()` now rejects `closed: true`.

New/updated coverage:

- `panel/tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts`
  - gateway response write skips Node-closed responses;
  - proxy body write skips Node-closed backend requests;
  - proxy end skips Node-closed backend requests.
- `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts`
  - static coverage pins `closed` guards in gateway and chat IPC.
- `panel/tests/image-system.test.ts`
  - static coverage pins `closed` guard in image IPC.
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py` and
  `tests/test_api_surface_contract.py`
  - API surface markers and thresholds now include the closed-stream cases.

Fresh artifacts:

```text

failed=[]
missing_panel_markers=[]
panel_api_request_builders: rc=0 passed=255

  "Cross-family live multi-turn smoke matrix is release-cleared",
  "Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared",

]
```

## [2026-05-28 23:24 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Packaged EPIPE proof is now sealed in the app bundle artifact.

Boundary: Python/panel proof worktree only:
`/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no DSV4 launch, no MiMo live generation, no
keychain mutation, and no notarization attempt.

Important correction to the 23:11 EPIPE source fix: source/panel tests alone
were not enough. The packaged `app.asar` was stale before rebuild and lacked
the closed-stream guard markers. I added a packaged integrity gate so this
cannot silently regress:

```text
packaged_epipe_closed_stream_guards=true
```

New/updated proof wiring:

- `tests/cross_matrix/run_packaged_integrity_contract.py`
  checks the packaged `app.asar` for:
  `responseWritable(res)`, `!anyRes.closed`, `requestWritable(req)`,
  `!anyReq.closed`, `function chatBackendRequestWritable(req)`,
  `function imageServerRequestWritable(req)`, and `!req.closed`.
- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
  requires `packaged_epipe_closed_stream_guards` in the current packaged
  integrity matrix.
- `tests/test_packaged_integrity_contract.py`
  has reject/accept coverage for stale vs current asar content.

The app directory was rebuilt with:

```sh
cd panel
npx electron-vite build && npx electron-builder --dir
```

`electron-vite build` completed and `electron-builder --dir` updated
`panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app/Contents/Resources/app.asar`, then failed at
the known signing/keychain blocker:

```text
errSecInternalComponent
```

Do not treat that as a new EPIPE failure. It is the same Developer ID private
key access blocker already surfaced in packaged integrity.

## [2026-05-28 23:31 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Staged app runtime parity restored after the app.asar rebuild.

Boundary: Python/panel proof worktree only:
`/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no DSV4 launch, no MiMo live generation, no
keychain mutation, and no notarization attempt.

Root cause:

- The app directory rebuild updated `app.asar`, but `electron-builder --dir`
  failed at the known signing/keychain blocker.
- That left staged bundled Python native libraries unsigned.
- Direct staged bundled Python failed with dyld:
  `Trying to load an unsigned library` for `libpython3.12.dylib`.
- Therefore `serve_help_runs`, `responses_cancel_route`, LoRA markers,
  `xml_function_tool_parser_cli`, and native-cache status checks all appeared
  stale because Python never started.

Local staged proof repair only:

```sh
# signed 499 bundled Python Mach-O files, then re-sealed the staged app
codesign --force --sign - <each bundled-python Mach-O>
codesign --force --deep --sign - panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app
codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=2 panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app
# valid on disk
```

This is ad-hoc signing only. It does not clear Developer ID signing or
notarization.

Fresh proof:

```text
direct staged serve --help: rc=0
help contains --lora-paths, --lora-scales, and xml_function

missing_or_stale=[]

build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-local-mimo-boundary.json
status=fail
current_proof_sweep=fail
component_ok.staged_app_runtime_parity_audit=true
```

## [2026-05-28 23:39 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Release DMG script now guards against the half-signed bundled-Python failure.

Boundary: Python/panel proof worktree only:
`/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no DSV4 launch, no MiMo live generation, no
keychain mutation, and no notarization attempt.

Before touching release packaging I audited the current proof board:

- live smoke `non_mimo_status=pass`;
- live tool smoke `non_mimo_status=pass`;
- real Electron UI unblocked non-MiMo status is `pass` for Gemma4, Hy3,
  Ling/Bailing, MiniMax, Nemotron Omni, Qwen3.6, Zaya text, and Zaya VL;
- the remaining real UI missing families are `mimo_v2` and `dsv4`;
- DSV4 has an explicit insufficient-memory preflight blocker on this laptop.

Root-cause slice:

- Rebuilding the staged app updated `app.asar`, then signing failed at the
  known keychain blocker.
- That can leave bundled Python native libs unsigned; direct bundled Python
  then aborts with dyld before `serve --help` or parity checks can run.
- The release DMG script previously called `electron-builder --mac dmg`
  directly and had no explicit bundled-Python native signing/final app seal
  step of its own.

Patch:

- `panel/scripts/build-release-dmgs.sh`
  - builds each flavor as a staged app directory first;
  - signs every bundled Python Mach-O (`*.dylib`, `*.so`, executables) with
    the release identity;
  - re-seals and strict-verifies the staged app;
  - builds the public DMG from `--prepackaged "$app_path"`.
- Added static regression coverage in `tests/test_engine_audit.py`.

This does not bypass Developer ID signing. If the Developer ID private key is
locked/unusable, release packaging still fails. The point is to avoid a
misleading half-signed app/DMG path and make native-library signing an explicit
release-build invariant.

Verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestStartupCompatibilityGuards::test_release_build_signs_bundled_python_before_dmg_signing \
  tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestStartupCompatibilityGuards::test_release_build_declares_sequoia_and_tahoe_dmg_flavors
# red before patch, then pass

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestStartupCompatibilityGuards::test_release_build_signs_bundled_python_before_dmg_signing \
  tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestStartupCompatibilityGuards::test_release_build_declares_sequoia_and_tahoe_dmg_flavors \
  tests/test_packaged_integrity_contract.py \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py
# 198 passed

bash -n panel/scripts/build-release-dmgs.sh
# pass

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
  --skip-release-gate \
  --out build/current-regression-suite-20260528-local-mimo-boundary.json
# status=open, same three open requirements

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --require-current-proof-sweep \
  --require-release-ready \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260528-local-mimo-boundary.json
# status=fail, same real blockers

git diff --check
# clean
```

Release remains not cleared. Remaining open release blockers are packaged
Developer ID signing/keychain access, DSV4 exactness/memory/real-UI, MiMo
live/tool/sink/root-cause/real-UI, and the full real UI/live model matrix until
those blocked rows are actually proven.

Release-surface finding:

  release mostly match local `1.5.49`, but
  `public_github_source_release_tag_matches_source_head=false`.
- So this worktree has post-public-release changes while still labeled
  `1.5.49`. That must not be treated as releaseable under the same version.

  check fails.

## [2026-05-29 00:31 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Max Output vs Max Context generation-defaults proof tightened.

Boundary: Python/panel proof worktree only:
`/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`. No Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, no model launch, no DSV4 launch, no MiMo work, no
keychain mutation, and no notarization attempt.

Gap found:

- `panel/tests/settings-flow.test.ts` already had
  `surfaces Max Output Tokens separately from Max Context Tokens`.
- The generation-defaults contract did not require that test to actually run.
  Verbose Vitest output can include skipped test names, so marker presence
  alone was too weak for this UI/defaults invariant.

Patch:

- Added required marker:
  `surfaces Max Output Tokens separately from Max Context Tokens`.

## [2026-05-29 19:26 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Step-3.7 local MacBook registry/cache proof tightened. Boundary: stayed in
`/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`; no max2, no Swift, no
deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no model launch, no DSV4 launch.

Gap found:

- Local Step-3.7 lives at
  `/Users/eric/.mlxstudio/models/JANGQ-AI/Step-3.7-Flash-JANG_2L`.
- Its `config.json` has top-level `model_type=step3p7`, inner
  `text_config.model_type=step3p5`, VL metadata, `sliding_window=512`, and
  full/sliding KV layer types.
- Python was only getting a minimal `step3p7` from `jang_config.json`; the
  canonical registry did not pin the architecture metadata.
- Panel detection needed the top-level `step3p7` mapping so the UI would not
  fall back to unknown/no parser/no VLM for real-user dev builds.

Patch:

- Python registry now has explicit `step3p7`:
  `cache_type=kv`, `cache_subtype=step3p7_full_sliding_kv`,
  `tool_parser=step3p5`, `reasoning_parser=qwen3`,
  `think_in_template=True`, `supports_thinking=True`, `is_mllm=True`,
  architecture hints `text_model_type=step3p5`,
  `attention_arch=full_and_sliding_kv`, `sliding_window=512`.
- Panel registry now maps `model_type=step3p7` to `step-3.7-flash` with
  Step3p5 tools, qwen3 reasoning, KV cache, and VLM enabled.
- No-heavy model-family and cache-architecture gates now include the Step-3.7
  full/sliding KV row.

Nemotron Omni hybrid metadata proof. Boundary: local MacBook only; no max2, no
Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

What I found:

- Local Nemotron Omni configs under `/Users/eric/models/dealign.ai` expose
  `model_type=nemotron_h`, `architectures=["NemotronHForCausalLM"]`, and
  `hybrid_override_pattern=MEMEM*EMEMEM*EMEMEM*EMEMEM*EMEMEM*EMEMEMEM*EMEMEMEME`.
- Existing lookup already got the broad family right:
  `family=nemotron_h`, `cache_type=hybrid`, `tool_parser=nemotron`,
  `reasoning_parser=deepseek_r1`, `is_mllm=False`.
- Missing piece was typed cache/architecture metadata:
  `cache_subtype=None` and empty `architecture_hints`, so release matrices
  could not distinguish Nemotron-H SSM+attention from generic hybrid.

Patch:

- `vmlx_engine/model_configs.py`: Nemotron-H now declares
  `cache_subtype=nemotron_h_ssm_attention` and
  `architecture_hints={"attention_arch": "hybrid_ssm_attention"}`.
- `vmlx_engine/model_config_registry.py`: config/stamped lookup preserves
  `hybrid_override_pattern` into `architecture_hints`.
- Added/updated tests so stale Omni stamps without real media metadata remain
  text-loader/hybrid, but still preserve the Nemotron hybrid pattern.
- Wired the new Nemotron test into model-family/cache-architecture matrices and
  updated local high-risk decode rows to expect the typed cache subtype.

Nemotron panel cache-subtype parity is wired and verified. Boundary: local

Why:

  `attention_arch=hybrid_ssm_attention`.

  not distinguish Nemotron-H SSM+attention from generic hybrid.

Patch:

- `panel/src/main/model-config-registry.ts`
  - added `cacheSubtype?: string` to `ModelConfig` / `DetectedConfig`;
  - added `architectureHints?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>`;
  - `nemotron-h` registers `cacheSubtype=nemotron_h_ssm_attention` and
    `architectureHints.attentionArch=hybrid_ssm_attention`;
  - config metadata override preserves `hybrid_override_pattern` as
    `architectureHints.hybridOverridePattern`;
  - JANG capability `cache_subtype` is preserved generically.
- `panel/tests/model-config-registry.test.ts`
  - red test proved Nemotron-H panel detection was missing `cacheSubtype`;
  - stale Omni sidecars still remain `isMultimodal=false`.

```text
## [2026-05-31 14:46 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

LFM2 base model_type now routes to typed hybrid SSM cache.

Boundary:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No Swift.
- No deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Finding:

- `model_configs.py`/the registry cover both `lfm2` and `lfm2_moe`.
- `vmlx_engine.utils.ssm_companion_cache.is_hybrid_ssm_config()` only pinned
  `lfm2_moe`.
- Base LFM2 configs can use `model_type=lfm2`; those could miss the SSM
  companion cache path unless layer metadata happened to include recognized
  markers. That is the wrong cache architecture: LFM2 should stay typed
  hybrid SSM, with SSM companion state native and only attention KV storage
  quantized where proven.

Fix:

- Added `lfm2` to `_HYBRID_MODEL_TYPES`.
- Added tests:
  - `tests/test_model_config_registry.py::TestModelConfigs::test_lfm2_base_config`
  - `tests/test_engine_audit.py::TestHybridSSMCompanionCacheGating::test_lfm2_base_model_type_uses_hybrid_ssm_companion_cache`
- Added cache architecture contract markers so future sweeps require both base
  LFM2 and LFM2-MoE coverage.
- Synced `vmlx_engine/utils/ssm_companion_cache.py` into:
  - `panel/bundled-python/.../vmlx_engine/utils/ssm_companion_cache.py`
  - `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app/.../vmlx_engine/utils/ssm_companion_cache.py`
  - `/Applications/vMLX.app/.../vmlx_engine/utils/ssm_companion_cache.py`

Proof:

- Red focused tests failed before the implementation patch.
- Focused tests after patch: 3 passed.
- Cache architecture contract:
  `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260530-lfm2-tool-parser-local.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `missing_markers=[]`.
- Refreshed stale no-heavy artifacts; objective digest:
  `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260531-lfm2-base-ssm-cache.json`
  -> only two open rows remain:
  - `Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared`
  - `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`
- `cd panel && npm run verify-bundled`: pass.
- Packaged integrity:
  `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260531-lfm2-base-ssm-cache.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`.

Release remains blocked; this is an engine/cache architecture fix, not release
clearance.
```

slice is fixed.

  nested `step3p5`, vision config, `jang_config` has vision), but

  `runtime_blockers.step37.reason=missing_mlx_vlm_step3p7_vlm_runtime` and
  `unblocked_non_mimo_status=pass`.

Packaging issue found and fixed:

- Rebuilding `panel/bundled-python` exposed a real bundler regression:
  dependency install can leave `python/bin/python3`/`python3.12` and
  `lib/libpython3.12.dylib` missing before local vMLX/JANG install.
- Patched `panel/scripts/bundle-python.sh` to save the standalone Python
  archive outside the mutable bundle tree and restore `python3.12`,
  `python3`, and `libpython3.12.dylib` immediately after dependency install.
- Rebuilt the bundle from scratch after the patch. `npm run verify-bundled`
  now passes with local `vmlx==1.5.50`, local `jang==2.5.30`, critical
  vMLX/JANG hash parity, MLX/VLM/JANG imports, and relocatable shebangs.

## [2026-05-29 20:33 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Step 3.7 local routing boundary is now fixed and proof-backed.

Scope:

- Stayed local on `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
- No Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2 testing, no MiMo runtime lane.

Root cause:

- `/Users/eric/.mlxstudio/models/JANGQ-AI/Step-3.7-Flash-JANG_2L`
  advertises VLM metadata (`step3p7`, `has_vision=true`), but its
  `step3p7_mlx.py` bridge currently exposes Step3p5 text runtime only and
  drops vision/projector tensors.
- The prior Python API/registry and panel detection treated
  `architecture.has_vision=true` as unconditional MLLM routing, which sends
  the bundle into missing `mlx_vlm.models.step3p7`.

Fix:

- Python API detection, Python model registry, and panel registry now detect
  `model_type=step3p7`, `model_file=step3p7_mlx.py`,
  `text_config.model_type=step3p5` as `text_bridge_no_vlm`.
- Text/chat/tool/reasoning/cache metadata remains Step3.7:
  `tool_parser=step3p5`, `reasoning_parser=qwen3`,
  `cache_subtype=step3p7_full_sliding_kv`.
- `is_mllm/isMultimodal` is false for this bridge. Do not re-force
  `--is-mllm` for Step3.7 JANG_2L until a real Step3p7 VLM runtime exists.
- VLM/image proof remains open and explicit:
  `build/current-step37-vlm-runtime-audit-20260529-local.json`
  now reports `status=open`,
  `root_cause=missing_mlx_vlm_step3p7_vlm_runtime`,
  `local_bridge_kind=text_only`.

Verification/artifacts:

- Red/green Step tests:
  - Python registry/API focused tests: 3 passed after failing red.
  - Panel Step3.7 focused test: passed after failing red.
- Wider checks:
  - `tests/test_model_config_registry.py tests/test_api_utils.py`: 194 passed.
  - `panel/tests/model-config-registry.test.ts`: 63 passed.
  - `panel npm run typecheck`: passed.
  - release/objective tests: 265 passed.
  - `git diff --check`: clean.
- Refreshed no-heavy contract artifacts, all pass:
  - `build/current-model-family-detection-contract-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`
  - `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`

  - `build/current-native-mtp-contract-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`
  - `build/current-vl-media-cache-contract-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`

  `cd panel && npm run verify-bundled` passed.
- Packaged integrity refreshed:
  `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`

## [2026-05-29 21:02 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Local-only EPIPE gap fixed in installed app; release still blocked.

Scope:

- Stayed in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
- No max2. No Swift. No deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No heavy model launch.

Root cause found for the remaining "write EPIPE" class:

- Gateway/global stream disconnect guards already handled direct `EPIPE` and
  aggregate disconnects.
- `panel/src/main/ipc/chat.ts` and `panel/src/main/ipc/image.ts` only checked
  the direct error and `cause`; they did not inspect nested
  `AggregateError.errors[]`.
- Node can surface write failures through that nested aggregate shape, so chat
  IPC/image IPC could still miss a backend disconnect even though the gateway
  guard existed.

Change:

- Added recursive nested aggregate disconnect checks to chat IPC and image IPC.
- Added regression coverage in:
  - `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts`
  - `panel/tests/image-system.test.ts`
- Extended installed-app parity proof to require:
  - `installed_panel_chat_ipc_epipe_aggregate_guard`
  - `installed_panel_image_ipc_epipe_aggregate_guard`
- Extended release-manifest current sweep to reject an installed app missing
  either IPC aggregate guard.
- Rebuilt and installed local `/Applications/vMLX.app`; app is ad-hoc signed
  and `codesign --verify` passes, but Developer ID signing remains blocked.

Fresh artifacts:

- Red preinstall proof:
  `build/current-installed-app-runtime-parity-audit-20260529-ipc-epipe-aggregate-preinstall.json`
  -> `status=open`, missing chat/image IPC aggregate guards.
- Installed proof:
  `build/current-installed-app-runtime-parity-audit-20260529-ipc-epipe-aggregate-installed.json`
  -> `status=pass`, chat/image/gateway EPIPE aggregate guards true, user-data
  and DiagnosticReports disconnect scans clean.
- API surface proof:
  `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260529-ipc-epipe-aggregate.json`
  -> `status=pass`, panel gateway streaming/backend-request EPIPE guards true.
- Packaged integrity:
  `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`, `false_checks=[]`.

Current proof board:

- `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`
  now has open rows exactly:
  - `Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared`
  - `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`
- `Cross-family live multi-turn smoke matrix is release-cleared` is `pass`
  with MiMo V2 explicitly deferred from that objective row.
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`
  remains `status=open` only because `release_regression_manifest` correctly
  fails release readiness while blockers remain.
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`
  remains `status=fail`, `release_ready=false`, `prepackage_ready=false`.

Remaining release blockers include Developer ID signing, DSV4 exact-code
memory-gated proof, MiMo V2 broken quality/root-cause/local proof, MiMo real UI,
DSV4 real UI memory, and Step3.7 missing real VLM runtime.

## [2026-05-29 21:22 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Gateway single-model auto-switch race fixed and proof promoted.

Scope:

- Stayed in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
- No max2, no Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No heavy model load.

Root cause:

- The gateway had per-session JIT dedupe but no cross-model transition
  serialization. In single-model mode, two concurrent requests for different
  stopped local targets could both complete the unload phase and then start or
  wake different targets at the same time.

Change:

- `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts` now serializes the single-model
  unload/start/wake transition and re-reads the target session after the wait
  before proxying.
- OpenAI, Responses, Ollama chat/generate/embeddings, capabilities, cache, and
  MCP routes still preserve payloads/streaming; they now go through the same
  prepared-session path.
- Added panel regression:
  `serializes concurrent single-model switches before starting a second target`.
- Updated API surface/current proof pointers to:
  `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260529-single-model-transition-lock.json`.

Verification:

```sh
npm test -- --run tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts \
  -t "serializes concurrent single-model switches"
# red before fix: target-b started while target-a was still starting
# green after fix

npm test -- --run \
  tests/request-builder.test.ts \
  tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts \
  tests/api-gateway-ollama-behavior.test.ts \
  tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts \
  tests/chat-override-policy.test.ts \
  tests/image-generation-state.test.ts \
  tests/image-system.test.ts
# 258 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py
# build/current-api-surface-contract-20260529-single-model-transition-lock.json
# status=pass; server_api_surface 31 passed; panel_api_request_builders 258 passed

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_api_surface_contract.py \
```

## [2026-05-29 22:19 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Child-process stdio EPIPE guard added and promoted into API surface proof.

Scope:

- Stayed in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
- Used this MacBook only. No max2.
- Did not touch Swift or deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx` implementation paths.
- No model/server launch.
- Step reminder from Eric: Step JANGTQ is not made yet. Treat Step3.7 as only
  the existing Step JANG_2L text bridge plus missing real VLM runtime; do not
  claim or test Step JANGTQ/2K as available.

Finding:

- Existing EPIPE contracts covered gateway/chat/image request writes and the
  installed aggregate audit passed, but app-managed child process stdout/stderr
  streams were not pinned in the source/API-surface contract.

Change:

- Added expected disconnect guards for child stdout/stderr stream `error`
  events in:
  - `panel/src/main/process-manager.ts`
  - `panel/src/main/engine-manager.ts`
  - `panel/src/main/ipc/developer.ts`
- Expected disconnects are ignored:
  `EPIPE`, `ECONNRESET`, `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`,
  `ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END`, `write EPIPE`, `broken pipe`, destroyed stream,
  and write-after-end messages.
- Unexpected stdio stream errors are reported into the relevant log/output
  path instead of bubbling as an app-level unexpected error.
- Added source regression:
  `guards child process stdio stream EPIPE across app-managed process lanes`.
- Promoted the proof to the API surface artifact as
  `panel_child_process_stdio_epipe_guard`.

Fresh artifact:

`build/current-api-surface-contract-20260529-ipc-epipe-aggregate.json`

```text
missing_nested_markers=[]
```

## [2026-05-29 22:38 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

DSV4 route-mode exactness preflight now uses the same strict local RAM gate as
the real-UI DSV4 preflight.

Scope:

- Stayed in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
- Used this MacBook only. No max2.
- Did not touch Swift or deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx` implementation paths.
- Did not launch DSV4 or any model server.

Finding:

- The real-UI DSV4 preflight gates on `vm_stat`
  free+speculative+purgeable memory.
- The DSV4 route-mode exactness preflight was using `psutil.available`.
- On this MacBook right now, those diverge enough to matter:
  `psutil.available_gb=108.77`, but stricter
  `vm_stat_free_plus_speculative_purgeable=92.54`.
- The exactness probe still stayed below the 120 GB floor, but it was reporting
  the less conservative metric. That is unsafe for release gating because
  inactive cache can make DSV4 look closer to launch-ready than it is.

Change:

- `tests/cross_matrix/run_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py` now records both:
  - `available_gb` from `psutil`
  - `preflight_available_gb` from `vm_stat` free+speculative+purgeable
- Launch/skipping now uses `preflight_memory_source=vm_stat_free_plus_speculative_purgeable`
  when `vm_stat` is available.
- Added a regression proving `psutil.available=130 GB` does not allow launch
  when `vm_stat` free+speculative+purgeable is below the DSV4 floor.

Fresh DSV4 no-launch artifacts:

```text
build/current-real-ui-dsv4-memory-preflight-20260530-local-refresh.json
status=skipped_insufficient_memory
free_plus_speculative_purgeable_gb=92.54
memory_gap_gb=27.46
active_heavy_process_count=0
launch_decision=do_not_launch

build/current-dsv4-route-mode-code-exactness-source-memory-preflight-20260530-local-refresh.json
status=skipped
reason=insufficient_vm_stat_memory
available_gb=108.77
preflight_available_gb=92.54
preflight_memory_source=vm_stat_free_plus_speculative_purgeable
memory_gap_gb=27.46
launch_decision=do_not_launch
```

Refreshed top-level artifacts:

- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json`

Current suite truth:

```text
failed_steps=["release_regression_manifest"]
open_requirements=[
  "Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared",
  "DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared"
]
```

Verification:

```sh
# 22 passed; red test failed first before the vm_stat gate patch
```

## [2026-05-29 22:46 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Expanded the `unexpected error write EPIPE` guard to the remaining app-managed
child-process stdio lanes.

Scope:

- Stayed in `/Users/eric/mlx/vllm-mlx-finite-launch-guard`.
- Used this MacBook only. No max2.
- Did not touch Swift or deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx` implementation paths.
- Did not launch a model/server.

Finding:

- Existing source already guarded gateway/chat/image request writes and
  process-manager, engine-manager, and developer IPC child stdout/stderr.
- `panel/src/main/ipc/models.ts` download workers and
  `panel/src/main/tools/executor.ts` built-in tool subprocesses still had raw
  stdout/stderr listeners with no expected EPIPE/ECONNRESET/write-after-end
  stream-error guard.
- That is a real remaining source path for noisy `write EPIPE` / destroyed
  stream errors during cancellation, renderer disconnect, or subprocess
  teardown. This is source-level hardening; installed app users will still see
  stale behavior until the panel/app is rebuilt and packaged from this tree.

Change:

- Added `isExpectedChildProcessStreamDisconnectError` and
  `attachChildProcessStreamErrorGuard` to:
  - `panel/src/main/ipc/models.ts`
  - `panel/src/main/tools/executor.ts`
- Expanded `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts` so the child-process stdio
  EPIPE contract covers process manager, engine manager, developer IPC, model
  downloads, and tool executor.
- Added `panel/src/main/ipc/models.ts` and
  `panel/src/main/tools/executor.ts` to `SOURCE_HASH_FILES` in
  `tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py`.

Artifacts:

```text

panel_child_process_stdio_epipe_guard=true
all_required_panel_api_markers_present=true
server_api_surface=31 passed
panel_api_request_builders=259 passed

build/current-regression-suite-20260529-step37-text-bridge.json
status=open
failed_steps=["release_regression_manifest"]
open_requirements=[
  "Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared",
  "DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared"
]
```

Verification:

```sh
cd panel && npx vitest run tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts --reporter=verbose
# 30 passed; red test failed first before adding models/tool-executor guards

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_api_surface_contract.py
# 5 passed; red source-hash test failed first before adding new files

cd panel && npm run typecheck
# pass

.venv/bin/python -m py_compile \
  tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py \
  tests/test_api_surface_contract.py
```

## [2026-05-29 23:39 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Local-only LFM2.5 JANG_2L tool-loop fix/proof. No max2, no Swift, no MiMo.

Root cause was real, not sampler noise:

1. Streaming Chat/Responses had a stale tokenizer-vocab fallback that could
   flip known `lfm2` `think_in_template=False` into `True` just because the
   tokenizer has thinking tokens.
2. Even with the false seed fixed, LFM2.5 generated an explicit `<think>`
   block on tool turns under `enable_thinking=false` and never reached the
   native tool marker before the cap. The prompt needs a closed empty thought
   sentinel before the LFM native tool rail.

Source changes:

- `vmlx_engine/server.py`
  - added `_apply_tokenizer_thinking_vocab_fallback`;
  - Chat + Responses streaming now only use tokenizer `has_thinking` fallback
    for unknown configs.
- `vmlx_engine/utils/chat_template_kwargs.py`
  - LFM2/LFM2-MoE gets `<think>\n</think>\n\n` even when tools are present;
  - non-LFM tool turns keep the old no-sentinel behavior.
- `vmlx_engine/engine/simple.py`, `vmlx_engine/engine/batched.py`
  - always call the sentinel helper for thinking-off prompts and let the helper
    decide per family/tool state.
- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
  - LFM2.5 row now uses the local JANG_2L sentinel Chat + Responses proofs.

Live evidence:

- Before sentinel direct local `/v1/chat/completions` stream:
  512 completion tokens of suppressed reasoning, no tool call.
- After sentinel direct local `/v1/chat/completions` stream:
  structured `run_command` tool call after 26 completion tokens.
- Passing Electron-dev Chat proof:
  `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-lfm25-moe-a1b-jang2l-lfmsentinel-20260530-proof.json`
  covers `long_tool_loop`, cache telemetry, native cache, parser/language leak
  checks, server cache controls, settings persistence.
- Responses proof:
  `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-lfm25-moe-a1b-jang2l-lfmsentinel-responses-20260530-proof.json`
  covers `responses_api`, but its generated shell args are still poor. Do not
  treat that single artifact as long-tool-loop proof; the family passes by
  union with the Chat proof.
- Current manifest:
  `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260530-lfm-sentinel-local-v2.json`
  still fails release overall, but direct current proof sweep has LFM2.5 pass
  and unblocked non-MiMo real UI status pass.

Remaining release blockers are not LFM/MiMo:

- Developer ID signing/keychain;
- DSV4 memory/exactness;
- Step3.7 real VLM runtime;
- stale packaged/current-suite artifacts still pointing at older proof rows.

Verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_reasoning_modes.py::test_known_lfm2_config_disables_tokenizer_vocab_thinking_seed \
  tests/test_reasoning_modes.py::test_unknown_config_can_fall_back_to_tokenizer_vocab_thinking_seed \
  tests/test_thinking_template_render.py::test_lfm2_thinking_off_sentinel_applies_even_with_tools \
  tests/test_thinking_template_render.py::test_thinking_off_sentinel_does_not_touch_other_families_or_tools \
  tests/test_vl_video_regression.py::TestReasoningParserWiring::test_thinking_off_does_not_synthesize_think_tags \
  tests/test_tool_format.py::TestFallbackToolPromptFormat::test_lfm2_parser_id_forces_python_call_list_tool_example \
  tests/test_tool_parsers.py::TestLfm2ToolParser \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_real_ui_matrix_rejects_lfm2_raw_tool_markers
# 10 passed

.venv/bin/python -m py_compile \
  vmlx_engine/server.py \
  vmlx_engine/utils/chat_template_kwargs.py \
  vmlx_engine/engine/simple.py \
  vmlx_engine/engine/batched.py \
  tests/test_reasoning_modes.py \

  tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py
```

## [2026-05-30 00:24 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Follow-up: LFM2.5 JANG_2L is now locally strict-pass in the real UI matrix.

Still local MacBook only. No max2, no MiMo, no Swift, no `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Additional root cause fixed:

- LLM scheduler hybrid SSM had no block-boundary feedback loop. When paged KV
  hit at e.g. 448 tokens but no SSM companion existed, it full-prefilled and
  queued only the full prompt key, so the next same block boundary missed
  again. `_finalize_hybrid_paged_cache_on_worker()` now queues an SSM rederive
  for the exact cached-token boundary before full-prefill fallback.

Fresh LFM proof:

`docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-lfm25-moe-a1b-jang2l-stricttools-responses-filesemantic-20260530-proof.json`

It now proves:

- both tool sentinel files:
  - `real_ui_tool_probe_1.txt` -> `REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_ONE`
  - `real_ui_tool_probe_2.txt` -> `REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_TWO`
- final hybrid cache reuse:
  `last_cache_execution.cache_detail=paged+ssm`, `reconstruction_ok=true`
- log chain:
  block-boundary rederive queued/stored, then
  `hybrid paged HIT -- 320 tokens (KV + 18 SSM layers)`.

Manifest refresh:

`build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260530-lfm-ssm-filesemantic-fixed.json`

`current_proof_sweep=fail`, as expected. Real UI matrix is still open only on
`dsv4` and `step37`; LFM, MiniMax, Ling/Bailing, Hy3, Nemotron, Qwen3.6, ZAYA
text, and ZAYA-VL are pass in this artifact.

## [2026-05-30 01:25 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Local-only Step3.7 source-runtime progress, still not release clearance. No
max2, no Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no MiMo work, and no heavy
DSV4 launch.

I found and fixed a real MLX-port bug in the partial Step3.7 VLM path:

- `_process_image_features()` was reshaping visual tokens into Torch-style NCHW
  before calling `nn.Conv2d`.
- MLX `nn.Conv2d` expects NHWC.
- `VisionModel` now owns the two Step3.7 downsamplers from the HF reference:
  `hidden_size -> hidden_size * 2 -> hidden_size * 4`, both
  `kernel_size=3`, `stride=2`, `padding=1`.
- `_process_image_features()` now reshapes `[batch, tokens, channels]` into
  `[batch, height, width, channels]`, runs both MLX downsamplers, flattens back
  to token features, and then applies `vit_large_projector`.

Fresh Step3.7 audit:

`build/current-step37-vlm-runtime-audit-20260530-source-surface.json`

- `status=open`
- `root_cause=missing_mlx_vlm_step3p7_vlm_runtime`
- `release_clearance=blocked_until_real_step3p7_vlm_runtime`
- `source_owned_runtime_progress.vision_downsamplers_available=true`

- `build/current-regression-suite-20260530-bundled-sync-step37-projector-rerun.json`
  -> `status=open`, `failed_steps=[]`.
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260530-bundled-sync-step37-projector.json`
  -> `status=fail`, `release_ready=false`, `prepackage_ready=false`,
  `current_proof_sweep.component_ok.regression_suite=true`.

- `packaged_app_developer_id_signing_blocked`
- `dsv4_long_output_code_exactness_open`

## [2026-05-30 01:32 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Local-only Step3.7 source-runtime progress, still not release clearance. No
max2, no Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no MiMo work, and no heavy
DSV4 launch.

Next Step3.7 VLM source slice is in:

- `Step3VisionProcessor` emits NCHW pixel tensors.
- MLX `nn.Conv2d` requires NHWC.
- `VisionModel` now has a patch-embedding conv matching the Step3.7 reference:
  `num_channels -> hidden_size`, `kernel_size=patch_size`,
  `stride=patch_size`, `bias=False`.
- `VisionModel.patch_embed()` accepts NCHW or NHWC pixels, converts to NHWC
  for MLX, flattens patch embeddings to `[batch, tokens, hidden]`, applies
  class/absolute position embeddings where configured, and returns the patch
  grid.
- Current source also exposes `sample_abs_posemb()` for non-base patch grids.

  patch sizes.

  before any release claim, run/refresh a systematic local proof sweep for
  multi-turn tool calls, reasoning parser stripping, parser autodetect, prefix
  cache reuse, L2 disk cache reuse, streaming content/tool deltas, cancellation,
  disconnect/EPIPE behavior, and no language drift.
- Gateway/single-model mode: keep the auto-switch toggle in scope. Prove that
  OpenAI-compatible model-id calls unload the previous model, load the requested
  model, survive sleep/wake/JIT timer conditions, and stream deltas without
  writes after disconnect.
- Bundled/installed app: source pass is not enough. For release evidence, run
  bundled/installed checks from isolation (`/tmp`, `PYTHONPATH=`,
  `PYTHONNOUSERSITE=1`) so imports cannot fall back to source.

packaged integrity, installed/bundled proof, and release manifest all agree with
no unexpected blockers. If a row remains open, say it remains open.

```text
engine_is_mllm=false
registry_is_mllm=true
family=step3p7
route=/v1/responses
```

So the Step3.7 blocker has moved from "registry/no-heavy route missing" to a
real app/server runtime mismatch: the launcher/serve/load path still produced a
text-only engine even though registry metadata says Step3.7 is MLLM and the
serve command included `--is-mllm`.

Next target for the remaining agent:

- trace `--is-mllm` from `panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs` through
  `vmlx_engine.cli serve` into the actual engine class/load path;
- find why Step3.7 reaches `/v1/responses` with `engine_is_mllm=false`;

  deeper in model execution with:

```text
[matmul] Last dimension of first input with shape (1,1024,4096)
must match second to last dimension of second input with shape (1024,288)
```

- Root cause found: Step3.7 uses `text_config.moe_num_experts=288`, while the
  generic JANG gate-dequant guard only checked `n_routed_experts`. That left a
  packed gate weight shaped `(288,1024)` in the MLLM/VLM route.
- Source patch in `vmlx_engine/utils/jang_loader.py` adds `_moe_expert_count`
  and uses it in both text and VLM JANG gate-dequant paths. It checks
  `n_routed_experts`, `num_experts`, `num_local_experts`, and
  `moe_num_experts` on top-level config and nested `text_config`.
- Regression added:
  `tests/test_jang_loader.py::TestJangDetection::test_step37_moe_num_experts_triggers_gate_dequant`.

## [2026-05-30 20:54 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Step3.7 VLM norm corruption is fixed in this worktree; text/tools/cache now
have a passing real Electron dev-app proof. Image/VL remains open.

Scope guard:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Root cause found:

- The coherent text loader and corrupt source-owned VLM loader had matching
  quantized weights/scales/biases.
- The VLM loader missed the Step3.7 zero-centered RMSNorm `+1` offset on
  dense-only language norm shards.
- That made direct VLM-language generation collapse into punctuation/newlines
  even though direct text generation was coherent.

Code changes:

- `vmlx_engine/utils/jang_loader.py`
  - added Step3.7 text-runtime model-type normalization,
    raw MoE/share-expert remap, and dense-only zero-centered norm helper;
  - wired those around text-loader sanitize before gate-sidecar handling.
- `vmlx_engine/models/step3p7_mlx_vlm.py`
  - `Model.sanitize()` now applies the Step3.7 norm offset for dense-only
    language shards and avoids double-add when raw MoE keys let upstream
    Step3p5 sanitize handle the offset.
- Tests added in `tests/test_jang_loader.py` and
  `tests/test_step37_mlx_vlm_runtime.py`.
