## [2026-05-30 21:28 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Step3.7 VL image path no longer drops pixels, but color semantics still block.

Scope guard:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Root cause for the earlier "cannot see attached image" result:

- `load_jang_vlm_model()` returned a plain `TokenizerBackend` for Step3.7 VLM.
- That tokenizer accepted the processor call but ignored `images=`, producing
  no `pixel_values`.
- The engine's `num_images_processed` counter was therefore too weak as proof;
  it counted media sources, not successful pixel tensor production.

Code changes in this slice:

- `vmlx_engine/utils/jang_loader.py`
  - Added Step3.7-specific VLM processor construction using source
    `Step3VLProcessor`.
  - Preserves detokenizer/stopping criteria without falling back to a tokenizer
    processor.
- `vmlx_engine/models/step3p7_mlx_vlm.py`
  - Processor accepts file-path images.
  - Processor converts tokenizer `input_ids` / `attention_mask` lists to MLX
    arrays for `return_tensors="mlx"`.
  - Model supports mlx-vlm's
    `get_input_embeddings(input_ids, pixel_values, mask=...)` contract.
  - Vision `conv1`, `vit_downsampler1`, and `vit_downsampler2` weights sanitize
    from HF OIHW to MLX OHWI layout.
  - Language wrapper returns a logits object for direct `mlx_vlm.generate`;
    `Model.__call__` still unwraps to raw logits for existing app/internal
    callers.
- `tests/test_step37_mlx_vlm_runtime.py`
  - Added red/green coverage for each boundary above.

Fresh direct artifacts:

- Processor proof:
  `build/step37-current-probe-20260531/direct-vlm-processor-class-after-step37-processor-tensor-fix.json`
  - source `Step3VLProcessor`
  - `pixel_values_shape=[1,3,728,728]`
  - `expanded_image_token_count=169`
  - `image_token_id=128001`
- Direct generation proof:
  `build/step37-current-probe-20260531/direct-vlm-red-image-semantic-after-step37-output-contract-fix.json`
  - `ok=true`, direct generation no longer drops/crashes
  - semantic answer is still wrong: red image described as black/white
- RGB A/B:
  `build/step37-current-probe-20260531/direct-vlm-rgb-color-ab-thinkingoff-after-output-contract-fix.json`
  - red -> `blue`
  - green -> `blue`
  - blue -> `gray`
- Embedding A/B:
  `build/step37-current-probe-20260531/direct-vlm-rgb-embedding-distances-after-output-contract-fix.json`
  - RGB embeddings differ, so this is not total image drop.

## [2026-05-31 00:05 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Step3.7 VL projector/root runtime fixed; direct RGB image proof now passes.

Scope:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Findings/fixes:

- `load_jang_vlm_model()` could still hit
  `No module named 'mlx_vlm.models.step3p7'` outside the MLLM wrapper because
  source runtime registration only happened in `vmlx_engine.models.mllm`.
  `vmlx_engine/utils/jang_loader.py` now registers local mlx-vlm runtimes before
  `get_model_and_args(config=...)`.
- Step3.7 image semantics were broken below vision feature extraction:
  `model.vit_large_projector.{weight,bias}` were sanitized to
  `vit_large_projector.{weight,bias}`, but the source module parameter path is
  `vit_large_projector.proj.{weight,bias}`. The projector stayed default/random.
  `vmlx_engine/models/step3p7_mlx_vlm.py` now maps projector checkpoint keys to
  the actual `proj` submodule.

Evidence:

- `build/step37-current-probe-20260531/actual-loaded-vision-patchembed-parity-red-20260531.json`
  confirms early vision parity against local torch reference.
- `build/step37-current-probe-20260531/full-downsample-projector-split-parity-after-projector-key-fix-20260531.json`
  confirms projected token parity after the key fix:
  `loaded_projector_weight.mean_abs_raw_delta=0`,
  `projected_features.mean_abs=0.00295`, `p99_abs=0.01944`.
- `build/step37-current-probe-20260531/direct-vlm-rgb-color-ab-after-projector-key-fix-20260531.json`
  now passes direct real-bundle generation:
  red -> `red`, green -> `green`, blue -> `blue`, with 169 image tokens each.

## [2026-05-30 21:55 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Step3.7 local Electron media E2E proof now passes after the app-placeholder
fix.

Scope:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Additional finding after the direct projector fix:

- The real UI/scheduler sends images as separate media payloads. The Step3.7
  processor only handled prompts that already contained `<im_patch>`, so the
  dev-build media proof reached the server but failed inside
  `Step3VLProcessor.replace_placeholder()` with:
  `ValueError: The number of placeholders does not match the number of replacements`.
- Added a focused regression for app-style image payloads with no explicit image
  placeholder and patched `Step3VLProcessor.__call__()` to prepend the required
  image placeholder only when images/replacements exist and the prompt contains
  none.

Fresh proof:

- `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-step37-jang2l-media-only-after-placeholder-projector-fix-20260531-proof.json`

  `vl_image`, `responses_api`, `current_electron_dev_build`,
  `settings_persistence`, `real_loaded_model`, `cache_endpoint_stats`,
  `native_cache_status`, `cache_hit_telemetry`, `language_leak_check`,
  `parser_leak_check`.
- Media result: persisted image attachment, `imageSemanticVerified=true`,
  `imageVerified=true`, assistant answered `Red`.
- Error/leak checks: `sendErrors=[]`, `rawParserLeak=false`,
  `reasoningRawParserLeak=false`.
- Cache result: `cache_hit_tokens=20`,
  `cache_hit_tokens_by_detail={"paged+mixed_swa":20}`,
  reconstruction/dequantization OK. Media cache store was skipped intentionally
  on the image request because media embeddings are path-dependent.

## [2026-05-30 22:03 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Step3.7 combined tools+media+cache real UI proof is now green after a concrete
app tool/media policy fix.

Scope:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Failure reproduced first:

- Artifact:
  `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-step37-jang2l-responses-tools-image-cachecontrols-after-placeholder-projector-fix-20260531-proof.json`.
- The first two built-in `run_command` turns worked and wrote:
  - `real_ui_tool_probe_1.txt=REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_ONE`
  - `real_ui_tool_probe_2.txt=REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_TWO`
- The image turn still failed because direct composer media and filesystem
  media-reader tools were conflated. With built-ins enabled, Step called
  `read_image`, then `list_directory`, then `read_image` on the text files,
  and answered that no image file was present. The server did receive the image
  payload, but the app's advertised tools steered the model away from native VL.

Fix:

- `panel/src/main/ipc/chat.ts` now treats direct composer media attachments as
  native multimodal input for that request:
  - `read_image` and `read_video` are removed from the advertised built-in tools
    only for direct attachment requests.
  - other built-ins such as `run_command` remain available.
  - the built-in tool system instructions tell the model to inspect attached
    media directly unless the user explicitly gives a local filesystem path.
- Added focused regression in `panel/tests/tool-media-followup.test.ts`.

Passing proof:

- Artifact:
  `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-step37-jang2l-responses-tools-image-cachecontrols-after-direct-media-tool-filter-20260531-proof.json`.
- Proven surfaces:
  `long_tool_loop`, `vl_image`, `responses_api`, `server_cache_controls`,
  `current_electron_dev_build`, `settings_persistence`, `real_loaded_model`,
  `cache_endpoint_stats`, `native_cache_status`, `cache_hit_telemetry`,
  `language_leak_check`, `parser_leak_check`, `chat_completions`.
- App result:
  - tool files contain the expected sentinel text.
  - image turn answered: `The dominant color of the attached image is red.`
  - `sendErrors=[]`
  - `rawParserLeak=false`
  - `reasoningRawParserLeak=false`
- Cache/runtime result:
  - `cache_hit_tokens=7179`
  - `cache_hit_tokens_by_detail={"paged+mixed_swa":7179}`
  - native cache reports mixed-SWA KV with full-attention KV,
    sliding-window KV, rotating-window metadata, prefix, paged, and block disk
    L2 enabled.
  - last cache execution reconstructed and dequantized successfully.

## [2026-05-31 00:54 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

LFM25 Responses exact tool-loop path is now fixed and live-proven locally.

Scope constraints honored:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

What failed after the earlier guardrail:

- The first new live rerun hit a real engine bug:
  `BatchedEngine.chat() got multiple values for keyword argument 'request_id'`.
- After that was fixed, the exact-finalizer fired on the second user turn
  because old tool output existed in history. That made the model's new tool
  call visible instead of letting the normal stream parser execute it.
- After narrowing the trigger, both tools executed, but the fast path leaked
  suppressed reasoning text before `</think>` while the normal non-stream
  Responses endpoint returned only the post-think visible answer.

Fixes:

- `_await_chat_with_disconnect_abort()` now normalizes duplicate `request_id`
  in `chat_kwargs`.
- Responses streaming exact-finalizer only triggers when a tool result appears
  after the latest user message.
- The exact-finalizer uses the same visible post-`</think>` boundary for
  thinking-disabled output as the existing non-stream Responses contract.
- Panel built-in tool prompt injection is suppressed for `reply exactly:`
  prompts so generic "explain after tools" guidance does not override strict
  user wording.

## [2026-05-31 09:42 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Real-UI CDP EPIPE proof-harness gap fixed; release still blocked.

Scope constraints honored:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Finding:

- App/gateway EPIPE guards are already present, and a local log scan did not
  find a fresh raw vMLX EPIPE stack.
- One unguarded path remained in the live UI proof harness:
  `CdpSocket.send()` wrote directly to the Electron DevTools socket. If the
  app/DevTools target closed mid-proof, that could surface raw `write EPIPE`
  instead of a structured proof failure.

Change:

- `panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs` now:
  - classifies CDP socket disconnects (`EPIPE`, `ECONNRESET`,
    `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`, `ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END`, nested
    causes/AggregateError);
  - tracks closed CDP sockets;
  - rejects pending CDP calls through `rejectPending(...)`;
  - avoids direct raw `socket.write(...)` in `send(...)`.
- Added regression coverage:
  `test_release_regression_manifest_real_ui_live_model_cdp_write_guards_epipe`.

Verification:

- focused red/green test: pass
- `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`: `217 passed`
- `node --check panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs`: pass
- `py_compile`: pass
- `git diff --check`: clean

Refreshed artifacts:

- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260531-cdp-epipe-guard.json`
  remains `status=fail`, `prepackage_ready=false`, `release_ready=false`.
- `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260531-cdp-epipe-guard.json`
  has only two open rows:
  - `Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared`
  - `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`

  artifact as `developer_id_keychain_user_interaction_not_allowed`, but only
  checked `security show-keychain-info` tails for that phrase. A direct
  `codesign --force --sign ...` probe can report `User interaction is not

LFM/Step architecture-cache policy is now an explicit real UI release surface.
Do not turn this into release clearance; it only hardens one previously implicit
surface.

Scope constraints honored:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.
- MiMo ignored/deferred per Eric.

Changed:

- Added `architecture_cache_policy` to the real UI release matrix.
- Required it for `lfm25` and `step37`.
- LFM must prove:
  - `family=lfm2_moe`
  - `schema=hybrid_ssm_v1`
  - `cache_type=hybrid_ssm_typed`
  - components include attention KV, SSM companion state, and async rederive
  - generic TurboQuant KV disabled for `hybrid_ssm_state`
  - attention-KV storage-boundary quantization is 4-bit group 64
  - KV cache quantization is enabled at 4-bit group 64
  - SSM companion L2 disk storage is visible
- Step must prove:
  - `family=mixed_attention`
  - `schema=mixed_swa_kv_v1`
  - `cache_type=mixed_swa_kv`
  - components include full-attention KV, sliding-window KV, and rotating-window
    metadata
  - generic TurboQuant KV disabled
  - KV cache quantization is enabled at 4-bit group 64

## [2026-05-31 07:23 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Gemma4 mixed-SWA L2 endpoint stats are fixed and live-proven; release still blocked.

Scope constraints honored:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Root cause:

- Gemma4 and Step 3.7 mixed-SWA cache write-through emitted
  `rotating_kv` block records with absolute `offset` / `_idx` metadata.
- `BlockDiskStore._serialize_block()` only accepted the older five-field
  `rotating_kv` tuple, so the async L2 write failed before SQLite indexing.
- `/v1/cache/stats` was not lying: it showed zero blocks/tokens/writes because
  the block files were never indexed.

Changed:

- `vmlx_engine/block_disk_store.py` now serializes/deserializes
  `("rotating_kv", keys, values, max_size, keep, offset, idx)`.
- `tests/test_block_disk_bfloat16.py` has regression coverage proving
  rotating-KV numpy write-through reaches endpoint-visible stats.
- `panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs` now passes the remote session id
  into the L2 wait helper explicitly.
- Gemma4 cache-controls row now points at the fresh L2 proof.

Fresh live proof:

- `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-gemma4-cachecontrols-l2storage-20260531-proof.json`
- real Electron dev UI, real Python server, local Gemma4 bundle
- `provenSurfaces` includes `l2_disk_storage`
- `/v1/cache/stats`: `blocks_on_disk=2`, `total_tokens_on_disk=65`,
  `disk_writes=2`
- no CJK/Korean leak; native cache is `mixed_swa_kv_v1`; server cache controls
  verified.

Refreshed manifest:

- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260531-gemma4-l2-rotating-kv-fix.json`
- Gemma4 is no longer in `real_ui_unblocked_non_mimo_partial`.
- Remaining blockers:
  - `packaged_app_developer_id_signing_blocked`
  - `dsv4_long_output_code_exactness_open`
  - `real_ui_dsv4_memory_blocked`
  - `real_ui_unblocked_non_mimo_partial` for Step 3.7 only

Step 3.7 note:

- Step is also mixed-SWA and should benefit from this engine fix.
- I did not rerun Step locally because the bundle is 82 GB and prior Step UI
  proof peaked around 84-88 GB active while current strict
  free+speculative+purgeable headroom is about 57 GB.
- Do not mark Step cleared until the real UI proof is rerun memory-safely and
  records `l2_disk_storage`.

Bundled/app sync:

- Synced the fixed `vmlx_engine/block_disk_store.py` into:
  - `panel/bundled-python`
  - `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app`
  - `/Applications/vMLX.app`
- All three hash-match source at
  `059016ee3aa1edbdc829d8b59367243118b83b23e79e21687e5ff7c8ec6c8344`.
- `npm --prefix panel run verify-bundled` passes.
- Packaged integrity refreshed:
  `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260531-gemma4-l2-rotating-kv-fix.json`
  is `status=pass`, `failed=[]`; signing remains the known Developer ID
  keychain interaction blocker.

## [2026-05-31 07:10 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Real UI matrix now requires endpoint L2 disk storage; do not release yet.

Scope constraints honored:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Finding:

- Step 3.7 live UI logs show block-disk write-through queued/wrote blocks, but
  the proof artifact's `/v1/cache/stats` snapshot reports zero
  `block_disk_cache.total_tokens_on_disk`, zero `disk_writes`, and zero
  `cache_totals.l2_tokens_on_disk`.
- That means the old matrix could count native `block_disk_l2=true` and cache
  hits without endpoint-visible L2 storage, which is too weak for release.

Changed:

- `panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs`
  - waits up to 15s for async L2 disk stats during cache-control proofs;
  - adds `l2_disk_storage` to derived proof surfaces;
  - fails cache-control proofs when native `block_disk_l2` is true but endpoint
    L2 storage telemetry is absent.
- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
  - adds required real-UI surface `l2_disk_storage`;
  - derives it from `/v1/cache/stats` fields, not server logs;
  - includes partial-family missing surfaces in the release blocker details.
- Updated current-suite and packaged-integrity expected-open lists for the new
  honest row:
  `Real Electron UI unblocked non-MiMo live model matrix is proven`.

Fresh results:

- Focused tests: 10 passed.
- Packaged integrity:
  `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260531-live-signing-refresh.json`
  is `status=pass`, `failed=[]`.

  `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260531-live-epipe-signing-dsv4-refresh.json`
  is `status=fail`, `release_ready=false`, `prepackage_ready=false`.
- Current suite:

  is `status=open`, `failed_steps=[]`.

## [2026-05-31 05:05 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

ZAYA/ZAYA-VL native tool scaffold fixed; release still blocked.

Scope constraints honored:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Changed:

- ZAYA fallback now scopes tool examples to the latest user turn, so stale
  prior tool requests do not contaminate later media/image turns.
- ZAYA fallback preserves native `<tools>` and `<IMPORTANT>` scaffolds when the
  tokenizer template already provides them.
- Scaffoldless ZAYA-VL processor templates now receive a full native Zyphra
  tool scaffold plus concrete examples.
- `BatchedEngine._apply_chat_template()` now applies the processor fallback for
  MLLM tool-enabled prompts without dropping image/media prompt handling.
- Release manifest request-contract failures now include row ids, e.g.
  `request_contract_missing:gemma4`, instead of repeating an anonymous
  `request_contract_missing` string.

Fresh proof boundary:

- ZAYA text Responses/tools/cache-controls proof is green:
  `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-zaya-text-responses-stricttools-cachecontrols-20260530-proof.json`
  with exact probe files
  `real_ui_tool_probe_1.txt=REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_ONE` and
  `real_ui_tool_probe_2.txt=REAL_UI_LIVE_TOOL_TWO`.
- ZAYA-VL image-only current-source proof is green:
  `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-zaya-vl-image-current-source-20260531-proof.json`.
- ZAYA-VL combined tool/cache-controls proof now proves native tool loop/files,
  but its long-history image turn did not pass `vl_image`; keep the separate
  image proof as media evidence until a combined tool+image proof is green.

```sh
  tests/test_tool_format.py::TestFallbackToolPromptFormat::test_zaya_fallback_skips_concrete_examples_when_request_names_no_tool
```

## [2026-05-31 04:20 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

ZAYA fallback file-tool argument binding is fixed, but live native tool
emission remains a blocker.

Scope constraints honored:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Finding:

- The ZAYA XML fallback prompt path was teaching file tools with placeholder
  path `.`. That came from treating `read_file`/`write_file` as directory
  tools in the example-value path.
- Prior diagnostic evidence included `write_file failed: cannot write to .`.

Changed:

- `vmlx_engine/api/tool_calling.py` now binds requested `path`/`content` values
  for file tools from the user request before falling back.
- Only actual directory-listing tools get the placeholder `.` example.
- Added focused regression coverage in `tests/test_tool_format.py`.

Fresh live boundary:

- Reran ZAYA text Responses file-tool proof with explicit `write_file`,
  `read_file`, `write_file` prompts and max tokens 512.
- Thinking off still records no real tool calls/files; the model fabricates
  that it used the tools.
- Thinking on diagnostic goes reasoning-only with no visible assistant message
  and no tool calls.
- Therefore the prompt binding bug is fixed, but ZAYA text/VL native tool
  emission is still not release-cleared.

- focused ZAYA fallback tests: 6 passed.

- objective digest now passes the API/cache row again.

## [2026-05-31 03:59 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

ZAYA-VL media+tools prompt path fixed; release still blocked.

Scope constraints honored:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- No Swift.

Findings:

- The real UI proof script's default PNG fixture had an invalid IDAT chunk CRC.
  That made ZAYA-VL image proof fail in image decode before testing model
  behavior.
- `BatchedEngine._apply_chat_template()` skipped the VLM processor template
  whenever tools were present. For ZAYA1-VL media+tools requests this dropped
  the `<image>` placeholder while still sending image bytes, causing
  `ZAYA1-VL image count does not match <image> tokens in prompt`.

Changed:

- Replaced the proof script default PNG data URL with a valid PNG.
- Changed the BatchedEngine MLLM branch so ZAYA1-VL still uses the processor
  template when tools are enabled, and passes `tools` through to the processor
  template kwargs.
- Added focused regression tests for the PNG CRC and ZAYA1-VL + tools
  processor-template path.
- Added `vmlx_engine/engine/batched.py` to current suite/release manifest source
  hashes.
- Refreshed bundled Python from this checkout after the engine source change.

- `npm run verify-bundled`: pass.
- `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260531-childstream-epipe-refresh.json`:
  `status=pass`, `failed=[]`.
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260531-childstream-epipe-guard.json`:

  - `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`
- Focused verification: 8 pytest targets passed, `node --check`, `py_compile`,
  and `git diff --check` passed.

Current live model boundary:

- ZAYA-VL image-only current Electron UI proof now passes with `requestContract`

Integrated LFM/Step proof now requires same-artifact Responses delta streaming.

Boundary held:

I found one more proof-board gap in the LFM/Step integrated tool+L2 claim.
`tool_l2_cache_integrated` required Responses cache-detail usage in the same
artifact, but did not require Responses delta streaming in that same artifact.

streaming from another run.

for `model_file=step3p7_mlx.py`: it forced `forceTextOnly` and made
`resolveJangMultimodal()` return false. That could make the UI launch Step3.7
without `--is-mllm` even though the Python runtime path exists.

Change:

- `panel/src/main/model-config-registry.ts`
  - Step3.7 built-in family now carries
    `cacheSubtype=step3p7_full_sliding_kv`, full/sliding KV architecture hints,
    `usePagedCache=true`, and multimodal defaults.
  - `isStep37TextBridge()` no longer demotes JANG bridge bundles to text-only.
  - Bridge bundles now keep `isMultimodal=true`, delete stale
    `forceTextOnly`, and expose source-VLM hints:
    `runtimeScope=source_vlm_needs_live_proof`,
    `vlRuntimeAvailable=true`,
    `textBridgeRuntimeScope=text_bridge_ignored_for_source_vlm`,
    `slidingWindow=512`.
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_vl_media_cache_contract.py`
  - Panel family-detection command now includes the Step3.7 route test.
  - Required panel markers now include:
    `routes Step3.7 JANG bridge through the source VLM runtime when available`.

## [2026-05-31 12:59 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Step3.7 native cache status now follows the registry subtype; do not allow
`step3p7_full_sliding_kv` to fall back to `plain_kv_v1`.

Boundary held:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No Swift.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Finding:

`_native_cache_status()` recognized generic mixed-SWA models via the internal
`_mixed_attention_cache_model` flag or `cache_subtype=mixed_swa_kv`, but it did
not recognize the new Step3.7 subtype name `step3p7_full_sliding_kv`. A
Step3.7 scheduler without that internal flag could report plain KV in health
status even though the registry correctly says full+sliding KV.

Change:

- Added `_current_model_config()` in `vmlx_engine/server.py`.
- `/health` and scheduler stats now pass the loaded registry config into
  `_native_cache_status()`.
- `_native_cache_status()` treats `step3p7_full_sliding_kv` as
  `mixed_swa_kv_v1`.
- The returned native cache status includes `cache_subtype` and reports
  storage-boundary quantization over `full_and_sliding_attention_kv` with
  rotating-window metadata preservation.
- Added the red/green test marker to the default cache-architecture contract.

Fresh proof:

- Red/green:
  `test_native_cache_status_reports_step37_full_sliding_kv_from_registry_subtype`
  failed before the fix with `plain_kv_v1`, then passed with `mixed_swa_kv_v1`.

  - `failed=[]`
  - `missing_markers=[]`

  - `missing_api_command_markers=[]`
  - `missing_panel_markers=[]`

Step37 JANG runtime is now covered by bundled hash/import release gates.

Boundary:

- Local MacBook only.

Finding:

- Local JANG source contains the Step37 source VLM runtime:
  - `jang_tools/step37/__init__.py`
  - `jang_tools/step37/nvfp4_codec.py`
  - `jang_tools/step37/step3p7_mlx.py`
- These files were not in the bundled JANG tools hash parity list, and
  `verify-bundled-python.sh` did not import `jang_tools.step37.step3p7_mlx`.
  That meant a packaged app could drift from local Step37 runtime source without
  the release gate catching it.

Fix:

- Added the Step37 runtime files to:
  - `panel/scripts/verify-bundled-python.sh`
  - `panel/scripts/release-gate-python-app.py`
- Added red/green tests in:
  - `tests/test_engine_audit.py`
  - `tests/test_release_gate_python_app.py`
- Added bundled import check for `jang_tools.step37.step3p7_mlx`.

Proof:

- Red tests failed before the script changes.
- Focused tests after patch: 5 passed.
- `cd panel && npm run verify-bundled`: pass, including:
  `ok   jang_tools.step37.step3p7_mlx`.
- Refreshed:
  - `build/current-regression-suite-20260531-packaged-pointer-bundle-refresh.json`
    -> `status=open`, `failed_steps=[]`, open rows only:
    - `Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared`
    - `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`
  - `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260531-nemotron-exact-finalizer-ledger.json`
    -> only those same two open rows.
  - `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260531-after-adhoc-reseal.json`
    -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`.

Release remains blocked only by the already-known blockers:

- `packaged_app_developer_id_signing_blocked`

## [2026-05-31 14:53 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

DSV4 exactness memory preflight now enforces the same model-size margin as the
real-UI gate.

Boundary:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No Swift.
- No deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Finding:

- `run_real_ui_dsv4_memory_preflight.py` already records model size and rejects
  floors below model-size plus a 40 GB safety margin.
- `run_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py` only checked the raw floor, so an
  artificially low `--min-free-gb` could be treated as ready-to-launch even if
  the requested floor was below the actual model footprint plus headroom.

Fix:

- Added `model_size_gb`, `required_model_margin_gb`, `safety_margin_gb`, and
  `floor_valid` to the exactness memory preflight artifact.
- Under-margin floors now produce `status=invalid_preflight_floor`,
  `launch_decision=do_not_launch`, and an `invalid_preflight_floor` launch
  blocker before any server spawn.
- Added red/green test coverage for that case in
  `tests/test_dsv4_route_mode_code_exactness.py`.

Fresh artifacts:

- `build/current-dsv4-route-mode-code-exactness-memory-preflight-20260531-release-decision-refresh.json`
  - `status=skipped`
  - `floor_valid=true`
  - `model_size_gb=79.98`
  - `required_available_gb=120.0`
  - `safety_margin_gb=40.02`
  - `preflight_available_gb=72.44`
  - `memory_gap_gb=47.56`
  - `launch_decision=do_not_launch`
- `build/current-real-ui-dsv4-memory-preflight-20260531-release-decision-refresh.json`
  - `status=skipped_insufficient_memory`
  - `free_plus_speculative_purgeable_gb=72.45`
  - `memory_gap_gb=47.55`
  - `launch_decision=do_not_launch`
- `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260531-dsv4-exactness-preflight-margin.json`
  still has exactly these open rows:
  - `Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared`
  - `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260531-dsv4-exactness-preflight-margin.json`
  remains `status=fail`, `current_proof_sweep=fail`,
  `release_ready=false`, with open blockers:
  - `packaged_app_developer_id_signing_blocked`
  - `dsv4_long_output_code_exactness_open`
  - `real_ui_dsv4_memory_blocked`

## [2026-05-31 15:44 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Step3.7 full/sliding KV cache subtype is now wired through the real runtime and
panel cache policy.

Boundary:

- Local MacBook only. I did not use max2.
- No Swift and no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- This is not release clearance. The suite still has the same two real open
  rows: real Electron UI cross-family matrix and DSV4 exact-code quality.

Root cause fixed:

- Registry/status could report Step3.7 as
  `cache_subtype=step3p7_full_sliding_kv`, but the LLM/MLLM schedulers only
  recognized mixed attention from explicit `layer_types`. The fallback now also
  covers `model_type=step3p7` + `text_config.model_type=step3p5` +
  top-level-or-text `sliding_window`.
- Panel session/settings code treated Step3.7 as generic `cacheType=kv`, so a
  stale saved `usePagedCache=false` could survive even though the subtype needs
  paged cache for its full/sliding KV architecture.

Implementation:

- `vmlx_engine/scheduler.py`
- `vmlx_engine/mllm_scheduler.py`
- `panel/src/main/sessions.ts`
- `panel/src/renderer/src/components/sessions/SessionSettings.tsx`
- `panel/src/renderer/src/components/sessions/SessionConfigForm.tsx`
- Tests/manifest wiring in:
  - `tests/test_engine_audit.py`
  - `tests/cross_matrix/run_cache_architecture_contract.py`
  - `tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py`
  - `tests/cross_matrix/run_packaged_integrity_contract.py`
  - `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
  - `tests/test_cache_architecture_contract.py`
  - `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`
  - `panel/tests/settings-flow.test.ts`

Proof artifacts:

- `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260531-step37-mixed-swa-runtime.json`
  - `status=pass`
- `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260531-step37-mixed-swa-runtime.json`

## [2026-05-31 16:30 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Step3.7 UI paged-cache lock gap fixed and locally proven.

Boundary:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No Swift.
- No deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

What failed first:

- Fresh live dev-app proof with
  `VMLINUX_REAL_UI_EXPECT_PAGED_CACHE_LOCKED=1` failed only the UI cache
  controls surface. `Use Paged KV Cache` was checked but not disabled in the
  Create Session form for Step3.7.
- The same failed artifact still proved the engine/runtime path: Responses,
  tool loop, L2 writes, mixed-SWA native cache, storage quantization,
  generation defaults, live speed floor, and no parser/language leak.

Root cause:

- `SessionConfigForm` supported `detectedCacheSubtype`, but `CreateSession` and
  `ServerSettingsDrawer` never stored/passed the detected subtype. The renderer
  IPC type also omitted `cacheSubtype`.

Fix:

- Plumbed `cacheSubtype` into `CreateSession` and `ServerSettingsDrawer`.
- Added `cacheSubtype?: string` to `panel/src/env.d.ts`.
- Extended `panel/tests/settings-flow.test.ts` so this cannot regress.
- Updated the release manifest Step3.7 tool+L2 proof pointer to the new
  paged-lock artifact and refreshed the current suite pointer.

Passing live proof:

`docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-step37-jang2l-responses-tools-l2storage-pagedlocked-after-subtype-ui-fix-20260531-proof.json`

Key proof facts:

- `serverCacheControls.verified=true`
- `Use Paged KV Cache`: checked and disabled
- `native_cache.family=step3p7`
- `cache_subtype=step3p7_full_sliding_kv`
- `cache_type=mixed_swa_kv`
- storage-boundary q4 applies to full+sliding attention KV
- `cache_detail=paged+mixed_swa`
- reconstructed/dequantized true
- L2 block tokens on disk: 3179
- Responses delta streaming + built-in two-turn tool loop
- no parser/language leaks, no send errors
- live speed samples: 50.2 and 50.9 tok/s

Refreshed:

- `build/current-regression-suite-20260531-step37-ui-pagedlock.json`
  - `status=open`
  - `failed_steps=[]`

- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260531-step37-ui-pagedlock.json`
  - `status=fail`, `release_ready=false`
  - failed components now limited to Developer ID signing, DSV4 exact-code,
    no-open-objective requirement, and real UI DSV4
  - no stale suite source hashes

- Current regression suite refreshed with `failed_steps=[]`.

## [2026-05-31 16:45 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

LFM2.5 natural built-in-tool prompt regression fixed and proven locally.

Boundary:

- Local MacBook only. No max2 was used.
- No Swift or deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx` paths were touched.
- DSV4 was not launched; memory preflight remains the gate there.

Finding:

- Fresh real Electron dev UI LFM2.5 run with natural file-create wording failed
  before the patch: cache/runtime were healthy, but LFM emitted a JSON
  `{"content": "..."}` pseudo-tool response instead of a parsed native tool
  call.
- This was not an EPIPE/server crash, cache miss, parser leak, or sampler
  forcing issue. It was an LFM2 fallback prompt-contract gap for a real UI
  built-in-tool request.

Patch:

- `vmlx_engine/api/tool_calling.py`
  - LFM2 fallback now explicitly forbids content-only JSON pseudo-tool calls:
    `{"content": "..."}` is assistant text, not a tool call.
- `tests/test_tool_prompt_fallback.py`
  - Added regression coverage that the LFM2 fallback derives the exact shell
    command for the natural file-create request and bans the bad JSON form.
- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
  - `lfm25_moe_a1b_responses_delta` now points to the new stronger proof.
- `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`
  - Updated fixture/pointer expectations.

Fresh proof:

- `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-lfm25-moe-a1b-jang2l-responses-tools-l2storage-pagedlocked-after-content-json-ban-20260531-proof.json`
- Companion screenshot:
  `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-lfm25-moe-a1b-jang2l-responses-tools-l2storage-pagedlocked-after-content-json-ban-20260531-chat.png`

What the proof shows:

- Real Electron dev UI, Responses API, delta streaming.
- Two real built-in `run_command` executions and both probe files written.
- Server cache controls verified in UI; paged cache checked and locked for
  hybrid/Mamba policy.
- LFM native cache: `family=lfm2`, `schema=hybrid_ssm_v1`,
  `cache_type=hybrid_ssm_typed`.
- Generic TurboQuant KV remains disabled for hybrid SSM state; attention-KV
  storage quantization is enabled at q4/group64.
- Paged prefix cache, block L2, and SSM companion L2 all record tokens on disk.
- No parser tag leak, no CJK/Korean leak, no send errors.
- Live speed samples: 228.3 and 226.8 tok/s.

Verification so far:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_tool_prompt_fallback.py::test_lfm2_fallback_for_file_request_forbids_content_only_pseudo_call \
  tests/test_tool_parsers.py::TestLfm2ToolParser \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_real_ui_live_model_rows_include_ling_bailing_slice \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_real_ui_requires_step37_and_lfm25
# 6 passed

.venv/bin/python -m py_compile \
  vmlx_engine/api/tool_calling.py \
  tests/test_tool_prompt_fallback.py \
  tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py
git diff --check
# clean

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260531-lfm2-content-json-ban.json
# writes artifact; exits red because release gate remains blocked
```

## [2026-05-31 18:25 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Live real-UI ZAYA cache proof found and fixed a disk-only terminal-state bug.

Boundaries:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No Swift or deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.
- MiMo ignored per Eric; DSV4 not launched because local memory preflight still
  says `do_not_launch`.

What happened:

- I ran a fresh real Electron-dev UI proof on `ZAYA1-8B-MXFP4` using a real
  vmlx server, Responses API, built-in tools, server cache controls, paged
  prefix cache, and block-disk L2.
- The first proof passed functionally but exposed a cache hygiene issue:
  `current-real-ui-live-model-zaya-text-responses-tools-cachecontrols-local-refresh-20260601-proof.json`
  had cache hit tokens while the server log contained
  `worker-side paged cache reconstruction failed, treating as cache miss`.
- Root cause: the DSV4/ZAYA unsafe-terminal-chain checks only inspected
  in-RAM `block.cache_data`. If the block was disk-only/frugal, fetch could
  accept a prefix whose L2 record had only DSV4 pending markers or ZAYA CCA KV
  pages without terminal composite/CCA state; worker reconstruction then
  rehydrated the L2 data and failed later.

Fix:

- `vmlx_engine/prefix_cache.py` terminal-state checks now inspect L2 records for
  blocks with missing in-RAM `cache_data`.
- Added focused tests for:
  - ZAYA disk-only non-terminal CCA prefix -> miss
  - DSV4 disk-only pending chain without terminal composite state -> miss

Fresh live proof after fix:

- `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-zaya-text-responses-tools-cachecontrols-disk-terminal-fix-20260601-proof.json`
- `cache_hit_telemetry` present
- `tool_l2_cache_integrated` present
- Responses API and delta streaming present
- built-in run/read/write tool loop present
- block-disk L2 telemetry present
- no worker-side reconstruction-failed line; unsafe short ZAYA CCA hit is now
  rejected early as a clean miss before terminal-state hits proceed.

Packaging sync:

- Rebuilt `panel/bundled-python`.
- Synced refreshed bundled Python into
  `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app/Contents/Resources/bundled-python`.
- Removed pycache and ad-hoc re-sealed `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app`.
- `npm run verify-bundled`: pass.
- packaged integrity:
  `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260601-dsv4-preflight-refresh.json`
  now `status=pass`, `failed=[]`.

Current artifacts:

- cache architecture:
  `build/current-cache-architecture-contract-20260601-zaya-dsv4-terminal-disk-guard.json`
  `status=pass`
- release manifest:
  `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260601-dsv4-local-preflight-refresh.json`
  still `release_ready=false`, `prepackage_ready=false`
- current suite:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260531-live-epipe-signing-dsv4-refresh.json`
  `status=open`, `failed_steps=[]`

Current truth: unblocked non-MiMo real-UI matrix remains pass with the fresh
ZAYA proof, but the overall release is still not ready. Remaining release
blockers are DSV4 local memory/exact-code proof and Developer ID keychain
interaction before notarization.

  `EPIPE` / `write EPIPE` hits.

  EPIPE/AggregateError/socket disconnect guards:
  `/tmp/current-installed-app-epipe-audit-after-renderer-fix.json`.
- Remaining user-visible hole was renderer IPC error display: `ChatInterface`
  used `error?.message` directly in the send-message failure toast, so an
  Electron IPC rejection containing `write EPIPE` could still be shown to the
  user even when main-process pipes were guarded.

Change:

- Added `isExpectedChatDisconnectError()` and
  `formatChatSendErrorMessage()` in
  `panel/src/renderer/src/components/chat/ChatInterface.tsx`.
- Expected disconnects now render as:
  `Server connection lost. The model server may have crashed or stopped. Try restarting the session.`
- Added red/green UI regression in `panel/tests/chat-ui.test.ts`:
  `chat send failures format expected EPIPE disconnects before showing toast`.

## [2026-05-31 20:49 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Final public DMG verification is now a packaged-integrity contract.

Why:

- Eric reiterated that live in-app proof is mandatory and release cannot rely
  on partial/static clearance.
- The current gates already block ad-hoc/unsigned packaged apps, but old release
  notes show a separate failure mode: the app inside a DMG was signed/notarized
  while the final DMG container itself was unsigned, not stapled, or rejected by
  Gatekeeper-open assessment.
- App signing/notarization is therefore not sufficient release evidence.

Action:

- Added `panel/scripts/verify-release-dmgs.sh`.
- The script is non-mutating: it does not build, sign, notarize, upload, or
  update feeds.
- It verifies both `sequoia` and `tahoe` final DMGs with:
  - `hdiutil verify`
  - `codesign --verify`
  - `codesign -dv`
  - `xcrun stapler validate`
  - `spctl --assess --type open --context context:primary-signature`
  - `shasum -a 256`
- Added `panel/scripts/verify-release-dmgs.sh` to packaged-integrity source
  hashes.
- Added `release_dmg_notarization_verifier_contract` to
  `tests/cross_matrix/run_packaged_integrity_contract.py`.

Fresh artifacts:

- `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260601-dsv4-preflight-refresh.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `release_dmg_notarization_verifier_contract=true`, verifier
  script source hash recorded.
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260601-epipe-renderer-installed-refresh.json`
  -> `status=fail`, `prepackage_ready=false`, `release_ready=false`.
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260601-epipe-renderer-installed-refresh.json`
  -> `status=open`, `failed_steps=[]`, open only:
  - `Real Electron UI cross-family live model matrix is release-cleared`
  - `DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation quality is release-cleared`

Verification:

- Red tests for missing DMG verifier contract failed before implementation and
  passed after.
- `tests/test_packaged_integrity_contract.py`,
  `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`,
  `tests/test_current_regression_suite.py`: 337 passed.
- `py_compile`, `bash -n`, and packaged integrity refresh passed.

No max2 was used. No Swift was touched. This does not clear release; it prevents
the next release lane from treating a signed app as equivalent to signed,
stapled, Gatekeeper-accepted public DMGs.

## [2026-05-31 21:02 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Live real-UI proof runner now fails missing visible assistant turns
unconditionally.

Why:

- Eric reiterated that live in-app multi-turn responses are crucial.
- The release manifest already rejected stale artifacts where a second user turn
  had no visible assistant answer, but the live runner itself only failed that
  condition when thinking was explicitly requested.
- That created a false-clearance gap for non-thinking two-turn proofs: the
  artifact validator would catch it later, but the live run could report success
  too early.

Action:

- Tightened `panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs`:
  `assertResult()` now fails any proof where `visibleAssistantAfterEachUser()`
  is false, independent of thinking mode.
- Updated the release-manifest source contract test to reject the old
  `requestedEnableThinking === true && !visibleAssistantTurnsComplete` gate.

## [2026-06-01 02:11 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Issue #182 Qwen VL patch-embed layout fixed in Python source and synced app
copies. No `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no Swift, no max2.

Root cause:

- Upstream `mlx_vlm.models.qwen3_5{,_moe}.Model.sanitize()` renamed
  `model.visual.*` to `vision_tower.*`, but did not transpose HF-native 5-D
  patch-embed weights.
- Reporter error shape matches this exactly:
  received `(1152, 3, 2, 16, 16)` while MLX Conv3D expects
  `(1152, 2, 16, 16, 3)` for
  `vision_tower.patch_embed.proj.weight`.
- JANG conversion already had the transform; the live mlx-vlm load path did
  not.

Action:

- Added the layout transform to `vmlx_engine/utils/mlx_vlm_compat.py` for
  normal Qwen3.5/Qwen3.6 VL loads.
- Added the same transform to
  `vmlx_engine/patches/mlx_vlm_mtp/qwen35_vl.py` so native-MTP Qwen VL sanitize
  cannot regress.
- Added both files to bundled/staged hash gates:
  `utils/mlx_vlm_compat.py` and `patches/mlx_vlm_mtp/qwen35_vl.py`.
- Synced both runtime files into:
  - `panel/bundled-python/python/lib/python3.12/site-packages`;
  - `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app`;
  - `/Applications/vMLX.app`.

```sh
  tests/test_release_gate_python_app.py::test_packaged_bundled_hash_gate_covers_runtime_files_changed_for_release \
```

## [2026-05-31 21:54 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Live real-UI proof runner now fails missing clean cache-hit telemetry surface.

Why:

- Eric called out prefix/cache reuse, L2, TurboQuant encode/decode, and cache
  endpoint behavior as release-critical.
- The runner already required positive cached-token telemetry, but the stricter
  `cache_hit_telemetry` surface also requires `cacheReconstructionClean()` so
  worker-side paged-cache reconstruction failures cannot count as clean cache
  reuse.
- Before this change, a future live run could pass the runner with cached tokens
  even if logs showed reconstruction fallback/miss, and only fail later in the
  manifest.

Action:

- Tightened `panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs` so `assertResult()`
  fails unless `cache_hit_telemetry` is in `provenSurfaces`.
- Added a release-manifest source-contract test requiring the runner-side
  clean-cache-hit failure.

Fresh verification:

- Red test failed before the runner change and passed after.
- `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py` +
  `tests/test_current_regression_suite.py`: 307 passed.
- `node --check panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs` and
  `git diff --check`: clean.
- Refreshed current manifest/suite:
  - manifest remains `status=fail`, `release_ready=false`;
  - suite remains `status=open`, `failed_steps=[]`;
  - open requirements remain only real Electron UI matrix and DSV4
    long-output/code quality.

No max2 was used. No Swift was touched.

Live real-UI proof runner now fails missing Responses delta/cache-detail proof.

- Eric specifically called out real in-app Responses streaming, cache reuse
  responses endpoint behavior, and delta content as release-critical.

  relevant rows, `responses_cache_detail_usage`; however the live runner itself
  only required the broad `responses_api` surface.

  only when the manifest re-read the artifact.

  - requested Responses + server cache controls now fails unless
    `responses_cache_detail_usage` is proven from nested cache detail plus
    positive cached-token metrics.
- Added `responsesCacheDetailUsageSeen(result)` to mirror the manifest-side
  recursive cache-detail check.
- Updated `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py` source-contract tests so
  this cannot regress silently.

Release DMG notarization submit/staple path is now under packaged-integrity
contract.

Boundary:

  signed DMG notarization/stapling procedure.
- Current release blockers remain real UI DSV4 matrix memory/exactness and
  Developer ID signing/keychain access for public signing.

Action:

- Added `panel/scripts/notarize-release-dmgs.sh`.
- Script operates on final signed public DMG containers:
  `release/vMLX-${VERSION}-{sequoia,tahoe}-arm64.dmg`.
- For each flavor it runs:
  - `hdiutil verify`
  - `codesign --verify`
  - `codesign -dv`
  - `xcrun notarytool submit --keychain-profile ... --wait --output-format json`
  - `xcrun stapler staple`
  - `xcrun stapler validate`
  - `spctl --assess --type open --context context:primary-signature`
  - `shasum -a 256`
- Added the script to packaged-integrity source hashes.
- Added `release_dmg_notarization_submit_contract` beside the existing verifier
  contract.

## [2026-06-01 03:41 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Wrapped EPIPE disconnects are now guarded across the app/API/chat surfaces.

Boundary:

- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`, Swift, or max2 used.
- This is not a release clearance. It fixes one local app/API disconnect path
  and refreshes the relevant proof artifacts. The remaining open rows are still
  DSV4 real UI memory and DSV4 long-output/code exactness.

Root-cause boundary:

- Prior guards caught direct `code`/`message`, `cause`, and
  `AggregateError.errors`.
- They did not catch wrapped disconnects where Electron/Promise layers carry
  the real `write EPIPE` under `reason`, `error`, or `detail`.
- That explains why a broken-pipe could still reach an unexpected-error path
  despite earlier EPIPE guards.

Action:

- Added red source-contract expectations first; they failed before patch.
- Patched wrapped-disconnect recursion in:
  - `panel/src/main/index.ts`
  - `panel/src/main/api-gateway.ts`
  - `panel/src/main/ipc/chat.ts`
  - `panel/src/renderer/src/components/chat/ChatInterface.tsx`
- Refreshed API surface current artifact and moved pointers:
  `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260601-wrapped-epipe-refresh.json`.
- Refreshed source-hash-dependent artifacts after editing app/API sources:
  - `build/current-max-output-context-contract-20260531-post-step-lfm-refresh.json`
  - `build/current-generation-defaults-contract-20260531-post-step-lfm-refresh.json`
  - `build/current-native-mtp-contract-20260531-post-step-lfm-refresh.json`
  - `build/current-vl-media-cache-contract-20260601-qwen3vl-frame-list-fallback.json`

## [2026-06-01 05:10 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Cache IPC EPIPE/reset handling is now covered by the API-surface gate.

Boundary:

- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`, Swift, max2, DSV4 launch, keychain mutation, or
  notarization attempt.
- This is app/API hardening for the cache endpoint lane. It does not clear DSV4
  live UI/exactness or Developer ID signing.

Finding:

- `panel/src/main/ipc/cache.ts` still used raw `fetch()` for
  `/v1/cache/stats`, `/v1/cache/entries`, `/v1/cache/warm`, and
  `/v1/cache?type=...`. During server/session stop or restart, wrapped
  `write EPIPE`/reset errors from this lane could still surface as generic
  cache errors even after the gateway/chat/image/child-process EPIPE fixes.

Patch:

- Added `isExpectedCacheEndpointDisconnectError()` and `fetchCacheJson()` in
  cache IPC.
- The classifier covers direct and wrapped `EPIPE`, `ECONNRESET`,
  `ERR_STREAM_DESTROYED`, `ERR_STREAM_WRITE_AFTER_END`, broken pipe, socket
  hangup, premature close, `cause`/`reason`/`error`/`detail`, and aggregate
  `errors`.
- Cache endpoint disconnects now report a stable cache connection-lost message;
  HTTP status failures and unexpected errors are still surfaced.
- Added the marker and `panel/src/main/ipc/cache.ts` hash to
  `tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py`.
- Current API-surface artifact pointer is now
  `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260601-cache-ipc-epipe-refresh.json`.

Verification:

```sh
cd panel && npx vitest run tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts --reporter=dot
# 31 passed

cd panel && npm run typecheck
# pass

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_api_surface_contract.py \
  tests/test_current_regression_suite.py -k "api_surface_contract or source_hash" \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py -k "api_surface_contract or source_hash or latest_api_surface or current_api_surface"
# 22 passed, 293 deselected

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py
# build/current-api-surface-contract-20260601-cache-ipc-epipe-refresh.json
# status=pass
# server_api_surface: 32 passed / 435 deselected
# panel_api_request_builders: 262 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py
# build/current-regression-suite-20260601-after-adhoc-reseal.json
# status=open, failed_steps=[]

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
```

## [2026-06-01 10:33 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Qwen35 installed-app #115 speed gate is now passing after a real MLLM decode
mRoPE fix. Still no release clearance.

Scope:

- Local MacBook only.
- No max2.
- No Swift.
- No `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Root cause:

- Qwen3.5/3.6 text-only MLLM prefill/cache-tail now uses explicit absolute
  `position_ids`.
- That path can leave mlx-vlm `_rope_deltas` unset.
- Decode `_step()` then called `language_model(input_tokens, cache=cache)`
  without `position_ids`, so `mlx_vlm.models.qwen3_5.language` hit
  `cache_offset + self._rope_deltas` with `_rope_deltas=None`.

Actions:

- Patched `vmlx_engine/mllm_batch_generator.py` so `_step()` passes explicit
  absolute text decode `position_ids` when the language model supports them.
- Synced that file into:
  - `panel/bundled-python/.../vmlx_engine/mllm_batch_generator.py`
  - `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app/.../vmlx_engine/mllm_batch_generator.py`
  - `/Applications/vMLX.app/.../vmlx_engine/mllm_batch_generator.py`
- Patched `tests/cross_matrix/run_decode_speed_gate.py` so `"Generation
  failed:"` text with zero usage is a hard failure.
- Updated public issue #115 audit to record Qwen35 installed-app speed gate as
  passing while keeping #115 open for Gemma installed-app speed-floor evidence.

Fresh proof:

- Red/green focused tests for decode `position_ids` and generation-failed gate.
- Focused touched tests: 8 passed.
- `py_compile` and `git diff --check`: clean.
- `npm --prefix panel run verify-bundled`: passed.
- Installed app runtime parity:
  `build/current-installed-app-runtime-parity-audit-20260601-qwen-decode-position-installed.json`
  -> `status=pass`.
- Live Qwen installed-app speed:
  `build/current-decode-speed-live-qwen35-4bit-issue115-installed-app-after-decode-position-20260601.json`
  -> `qwen35_4bit status=pass`, bundle decode about 123 tok/s, coherency clean,
  PP rows about 1.5k-1.65k prompt tok/s, no fake generation-failed content.
- Current suite:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260601-qwen35-decode-position-speed-gate.json`
  -> `failed_steps=[]`; expected open requirements remain real Electron UI
  matrix and DSV4 long-output/code exactness.

Remaining release blockers:

- DSV4 long-output/code exactness.
- Real Electron UI DSV4 matrix memory preflight.
- mlxstudio #115 Gemma installed-app speed-floor evidence.

## [2026-06-01 11:25 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Current-suite runner hang root cause fixed.

Boundary followed: Python worktree on this MacBook only. No `/Users/eric/vmlx`,
no Swift, no max2.

Root cause:

- The suite runner used `subprocess.run(..., stdout=PIPE)`.
- If a step exits after spawning a descendant that inherits stdout, Python can
  keep waiting for EOF on the pipe even though the direct child is gone.
- This matched the prior stuck current-suite parent: no model server and no
  relevant child remained, but the parent was blocked in a read.

Change:

- Added a regression for a pipe-holding descendant.
- `_run_step()` now captures output through a temp file, starts each step in its
  own process group, waits only for the direct child with a timeout, then
  terminates the process group to clean up inherited-pipe descendants.
- Current suite pointer moved to:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`.

Fresh proof:

- Focused tests for runner/manifest pointers passed.
- `py_compile` and `git diff --check` clean.
- Current suite:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`
  -> `status=open`, `failed_steps=[]`.
  It includes final `release_regression_manifest` and `release_gate_skip_app`
  steps with `timed_out=false`.

Still not release-ready:

- Real Electron UI DSV4 matrix remains open.
- DSV4 long-output/code exactness remains open.

---

## [2026-06-01 12:22 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Renderer chat EPIPE raw-error noise fixed and added to current-suite API
surface coverage.

Boundary followed: Python/panel worktree on this MacBook only. No
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no Swift, no max2.

Root cause:

- The main/backend paths had EPIPE/ECONNRESET guards, and chat send failures
  were already formatted to the normalized "Server connection lost" toast.
- The renderer still unconditionally did
  `console.error('Failed to send message:', error)` in `ChatInterface` before
  formatting the error. That kept raw `write EPIPE` visible in app/dev logs even
  for expected disconnects.

Change:

- `ChatInterface` now suppresses that raw failed-message console error only for
  classified expected disconnects.
- Non-EPIPE chat send failures still log normally.
- Added a focused `chat-ui.test.ts` regression.
- Added `tests/chat-ui.test.ts` to `run_api_surface_contract.py` and required
  marker:
  `does not log expected chat EPIPE disconnects as raw failed-message console errors`.

Fresh proof:

## [2026-06-01 14:47 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Chat stream error chunk EPIPE guard is fixed, rebuilt, and installed.

Boundary followed: local Python/panel worktree only. No `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no
Swift, no max2.

Root cause for this slice:

- Chat backend request finalization, renderer toast normalization, and outer
  `[CHAT] Error caught` logging were already guarded.
- One narrower stream path could still log raw disconnect-shaped backend error
  chunks first:
  - Responses API SSE `error` / `response.error` / `response.failed`
  - Chat Completions streamed `parsed.error`
- If the server sent an error event with `errDetail` containing `write EPIPE`,
  the app logged `[CHAT] Responses API error event: ...` or
  `[CHAT] Chat completions error chunk: ...` before the outer catch normalized
  it.

Change:

- Added `expectedChatBackendDisconnectError()` in `panel/src/main/ipc/chat.ts`.
- Both stream error branches now check
  `isExpectedChatBackendDisconnectError(errDetail)` before raw logging and
  throw the normalized backend-disconnect error into the existing outer catch.
- Non-disconnect server error chunks still log and throw as server errors.
- Added source contract coverage and installed/staged app parity coverage:
  `installed_panel_chat_stream_error_disconnect_guard`.

## [2026-06-01 15:11 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Backend stderr `write EPIPE` user-visible leak is fixed and pushed.

Boundary followed: local MacBook only, Python/panel worktree only. No
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no Swift, no max2, and no model server launched by this
slice.

Finding:

- After the prior IPC/gateway stream disconnect guards, `panel/src/main/sessions.ts`
  still relayed backend stderr chunks directly into session logs and console
  diagnostics.
- That meant expected backend disconnect lines such as `write EPIPE`, broken
  pipe, reset/destroyed stream, or write-after-end could still show as
  user-visible scary errors.

Fix:

- Added `isExpectedBackendStderrDisconnectLine(...)`.
- The stderr handler now normalizes expected disconnect stderr to:
  `[SERVER] Client disconnected during stream; backend pipe closed cleanly.`
  and returns before raw session-log/error emission.
- Added source contract coverage in `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts`.
- Added installed/staged runtime parity detection:
  `installed_panel_backend_stderr_line_disconnect_guard`.

Pushed:

- `8987137c fix: normalize backend stderr disconnect logs`
- pushed to `origin/codex/pr-intake-manifest` and `origin/main`.

Package state:

- Rebuilt and installed the app.
- Prebuild installed/staged parity artifacts were correctly stale for this new
  guard.
- Postbuild installed and staged parity artifacts are both `status=pass` with
  `installed_panel_backend_stderr_line_disconnect_guard=true`.
- Because the rebuild reset app signing, I resealed both
  `panel/release/mac-arm64/vMLX.app` and `/Applications/vMLX.app` with
  `Developer ID Application: ShieldStack LLC (55KGF2S5AY)` plus hardened
  runtime. Both strict deep codesign verifications pass.
- Packaged integrity is back to `status=pass` and
  `package_signing_preflight.status=pass`.

Fresh proof:

```sh
npm exec vitest run tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts tests/chat-ui.test.ts --reporter=verbose
# 174 passed

npm run typecheck
# pass

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_installed_app_runtime_parity_audit.py \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_current_proof_sweep_accepts_staged_app_runtime_parity_pass \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_current_proof_sweep_rejects_open_installed_app_runtime_parity
# 18 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py \
  --out build/current-api-surface-contract-20260601-backend-stderr-epipe-guard.json
# status=pass

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_current_regression_suite.py \
  --out build/current-regression-suite-20260601-backend-stderr-epipe-guard.json
# status=open, failed_steps=[]

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_packaged_integrity_contract.py \
  --out build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260601-developer-id-staged-app.json
# status=pass

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_regression_manifest.py \
  --out build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json
# status=fail, release_ready=false, prepackage_ready=false
```

## [2026-06-01 16:15 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Live proof-script EPIPE hardening landed in source and gates.

Boundary followed: local MacBook only, Python/panel worktree only. No
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no Swift, no max2.

Finding:

- App-managed process lanes already had EPIPE/ECONNRESET stdio guards.
- The live proof scripts guarded CDP/socket writes, but their spawned server/app
  stdout/stderr pipes did not have `error` handlers.
- That means an EPIPE during proof collection could crash the proof script as a
  raw `write EPIPE` and be mistaken for an app/runtime failure.

Changes:

- `panel/scripts/live-real-ui-model-proof.mjs`
  - added `attachChildProcessStreamErrorGuard`
  - wired server stdout/stderr, installed app stdout/stderr, and dev app
    stdout/stderr.
- `panel/scripts/live-chat-tools-reasoning-proof.mjs`
  - added `attachChildProcessStreamErrorGuard`
  - wired dev app stdout/stderr.
- `panel/tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts`
  - added `guards live proof script child stdio EPIPE while collecting e2e evidence`.
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py`
  - now hashes both proof scripts.
  - `panel_child_process_stdio_epipe_guard` now requires the proof-script marker.
- `tests/test_api_surface_contract.py`
  - pins the new marker and source hashes.

Fresh proof:

- Red test failed before script changes for missing
  `attachChildProcessStreamErrorGuard`.
- Panel EPIPE suite:
  `npm run --silent test -- --run tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts tests/api-gateway-single-model.behavior.test.ts tests/chat-ui.test.ts tests/image-system.test.ts`
  passed: 4 files, 307 tests.
- `node --check` for both proof scripts passed.
- API surface artifact refreshed:
  `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260601-cache-ipc-epipe-refresh.json`
  - `status=pass`
  - `panel_child_process_stdio_epipe_guard=true`
  - `missing_panel_markers=[]`
- Current suite refreshed:
  `build/current-regression-suite-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`
  - `status=open`
  - `failed_steps=[]`
  - `completed_steps=30`
  - `current_step=null`
- Release manifests refreshed:
  `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260601-proof-script-epipe.json`
  and `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`.
  Release is still red for known live blockers only:
  - `no_release_blockers`
  - `dsv4_long_output_code_exactness`
  - `no_open_objective_requirements`
  - `real_ui_dsv4`

This does not clear release. It removes another raw EPIPE failure path from the

## [2026-06-01 16:56 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

#179 objective-detail gate hardened; do not drop the open-row detail.

Boundary followed: local MacBook only, Python/panel worktree only. No
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no Swift, no max2.

Finding:

- The current regression suite already preserved MiniMax #179 not-proven details
  in `open_requirement_details`.
- The release manifest's separate `objective_digest` validator was only keeping
  open requirement names and evidence failures, not the open-row details.
- That meant a stale objective digest could still show #179 open while hiding the
  exact reporter-parity boundary.

Changes:

- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
  - preserves objective-digest `open_requirement_details`.
  - adds a #179 stale-boundary check requiring
    `release_blocker_id=issue179_minimax_k_root_cause_audit`,
    the reporter-installed-bundle-hash `not_proven` item, and a
    `#179 remains open` release boundary.
  - makes `objective_digest` component status fail on that stale-boundary check.
- `tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py`
  - adds the red/green stale-objective-detail regression.
  - updates the current objective fixture to emit the realistic #179 open-row
    boundary.

Fresh artifacts:

- `build/current-regression-suite-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`
  - `status=open`
  - `failed_steps=[]`
  - open rows are still exactly #179, real UI matrix, and DSV4 code exactness.
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`
  - `release_ready=false`
  - `prepackage_ready=false`
  - `regression_suite=true`
  - `objective_digest=true`
  - #179 details are present both in the objective row and release blocker
    ledger.

This does not clear release. It prevents stale objective-detail artifacts from
masking that #179 is still blocked on reporter installed bundle server-hash
provenance.

## [2026-06-01 17:28 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

#175-#178 release-boundary checks hardened.

Boundary followed: local MacBook only, Python/panel worktree only. No
`/Users/eric/vmlx`, no Swift, no max2.

Finding:

- The #175-#179 release-boundary validator accepted #175-#178 if
  `focused_source_slice=pass`, but did not require the concrete proof checks in
  each issue row.
- That could let a partial/stale aggregate artifact hide missing proof for:
  memory clear runtime behavior, promoted L2 block cleanup, cache-selection
  TTFT/cold paged TurboQuant behavior, or LoRA surface wiring.
- The boundary audit and release manifest still pointed #175-#177 installed/live
  inputs at May 27 artifact names, while the current local-refresh proof is
  June 1.

Changes:

- `tests/cross_matrix/release_regression_manifest.py`
  - #175-#178 now require their per-issue check keys to be present and true.
  - #175-#177 installed/live artifact pointers now use:
    - `build/current-issue175-177-installed-runtime-audit-20260601-local-refresh.json`
    - `build/current-issue175-177-live-runtime-audit-20260601-local-refresh.json`
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_issue175_177_installed_runtime_audit.py`
  and `tests/cross_matrix/run_issue175_177_live_runtime_audit.py`
  default to the June 1 local-refresh outputs.
- `tests/cross_matrix/run_issue175_179_release_boundary_audit.py` consumes the
  same June 1 local-refresh inputs.
- Added regressions:
  - missing #177 `installed_live_ttft_and_cold_paged_tq_proven` is rejected.
  - #175-#179 boundary audit cannot route back to May 27 #175-#177 inputs.

Fresh proof:

- `build/current-issue175-177-installed-runtime-audit-20260601-local-refresh.json`
  -> `status=pass`
- `build/current-issue175-177-live-runtime-audit-20260601-local-refresh.json`
  -> `status=pass`
- `build/current-issue175-179-release-boundary-audit-20260531-post-install-sync.json`
  -> `status=open`, #175-#178 pass with required checks true, #179 open
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`
  -> `status=open`, `failed_steps=[]`
- `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260601-pipe-safe-runner.json`
  -> `release_ready=false`, `prepackage_ready=false`

Verification:

```sh
  tests/test_issue175_179_release_boundary_audit.py \
  tests/test_issue175_177_installed_runtime_audit.py \
```

Finding:

- The current public app issue audit maps #180 to
  `docs/internal/agent-notes/current-real-ui-live-model-minimax-m27-small-responses-stricttools-cachecontrols-20260530-proof.json`.
- That proof has the relevant live counters:
  - `modelName=MiniMax-M2.7-Small-JANGTQ`
  - `servedModel=MiniMax-M2.7-Small-JANGTQ`
  - `rendererWireApi=responses`

  - `requestedServerCacheControls=true`
  - `visibleAssistantTurnsComplete=true`

  - `reasoningCjkLeakCount=0`

  - `reasoningRawParserLeak=false`

  require the explicit numeric-garbage guard. A partial audit could therefore
  pass without covering the issue's actual failure mode.

## [2026-06-01 20:37 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Performance health EPIPE source guard added; built app parity still needs refresh.

Boundary followed: local MacBook/Python-panel worktree only. No Swift, no
deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`, no max2, and no live model launch.

Root cause found for another raw `write EPIPE` surface: the chat/cache/image
and gateway paths had disconnect guards, but `performance:health` still wrapped
fetch failures as `Health endpoint unreachable: ${err.message}`. If the health
poll hit EPIPE/ECONNRESET while the session/server was stopping, the renderer
could still receive raw socket text.

Change:

- Added `isExpectedPerformanceEndpointDisconnectError()` in
  `panel/src/main/ipc/performance.ts`.
- Handles direct `EPIPE`/`ECONNRESET`/destroyed-stream codes, message regexes,
  nested `cause`/`reason`/`error`/`detail`, and `AggregateError.errors`.
- Maps reset-like health-poll failures to:
  `Performance health connection lost. The model server may have stopped or restarted; retry after the session is healthy.`
- Promoted this into API-surface, installed-app parity, packaged-integrity, and
  current-suite source-hash coverage.

Fresh proof:

- `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260602-performance-health-epipe.json`
  passed.
- API surface checks include `panel_performance_health_epipe_guard=true`.
- Panel request-builder suite in that artifact: `409 passed`,
  `missing_panel_markers=[]`.

Verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q \
  tests/test_api_surface_contract.py::test_api_surface_contract_default_out_tracks_current_release_proof_artifact \
  tests/test_api_surface_contract.py::test_api_surface_contract_requires_performance_health_epipe_normalization \
  tests/test_api_surface_contract.py::test_api_surface_contract_hashes_performance_health_disconnect_guard \
  tests/test_api_surface_contract.py::test_api_surface_contract_pins_named_public_surface_edges \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_tracks_api_surface_with_runner_artifact \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_api_surface_requires_performance_health_epipe_guard \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_release_regression_manifest_rejects_incomplete_current_api_surface_matrix \
  tests/test_release_regression_manifest.py::test_current_proof_sweep_rejects_installed_app_missing_ipc_epipe_aggregate_guard \
  tests/test_current_regression_suite.py::test_current_regression_suite_refreshes_current_packaged_integrity_artifact \
  tests/test_current_regression_suite.py::test_current_regression_suite_runs_api_surface_contracts \
  tests/test_installed_app_runtime_parity_audit.py::test_installed_app_runtime_parity_records_known_stale_surface
# 11 passed

cd panel && npx vitest run tests/api-gateway-ollama.test.ts --reporter=verbose
# 35 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_api_surface_contract.py \
  --out build/current-api-surface-contract-20260602-performance-health-epipe.json

cd panel && npm run typecheck
# passed
```

## [2026-06-02 22:35 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Cache-detail telemetry regression fixed and re-proven through the no-heavy API
and app-package gates.

Boundary followed: local MacBook/Python-panel worktree only. No max2, no Swift,
and no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Bug:

- Chat/Responses usage only emitted prompt/input token details when
  `cached_tokens > 0`.
- That dropped non-empty `cache_detail` on cold or cache-bypass paths where
  `cached_tokens == 0`, hiding the actual cache architecture/path selected by
  the scheduler.

Fix:

- `vmlx_engine/server.py` now emits prompt/input token details when
  `cached_tokens > 0 or cache_detail`.
- Added focused direct, chat-stream, and Responses-stream tests for
  `cached_tokens=0` plus `cache_detail`.
- Wired those tests into
  `tests/cross_matrix/run_noheavy_api_cache_contract.py` and the API-surface
  contract so this cannot silently regress again.

Fresh artifacts:

- `build/current-api-cache-contract-proof-20260602-cache-detail-zero-cached.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `missing_markers=[]`
- `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260602-cache-detail-zero-cached.json`
  -> `status=pass`
- `build/current-packaged-integrity-contract-20260602-developer-id-staged-signing.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `failed=[]`
- `build/current-public-app-issue-audit-20260602-after-performance-health-epipe-app-sync.json`
  -> `status=open`, `focused_failures=[]`, `focused_open=['117']`
- `build/current-regression-suite-20260602-cache-detail-zero-cached.json`
  -> `status=open`, `failed_steps=[]`

Release is still not clear. The remaining open rows are still MiniMax-K
reporter/root-cause parity, DSV4 real Electron UI cross-family live matrix, and
DSV4 long-output/code/file-generation exactness.

## [2026-06-01 23:28 PDT] from: codex -> @all-agents

Session lifecycle EPIPE is normalized in source, staged app, and installed app.

Boundary followed: local MacBook/Python-panel only. No max2, no Swift, no
deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`, and no model launch.

Finding:

- Existing EPIPE fixes covered gateway streaming, chat IPC, image IPC, cache
  IPC, performance health, child stdio, backend stderr, and renderer chat
  toast normalization.
- `panel/src/main/ipc/sessions.ts` still returned and forwarded raw lifecycle
  errors from session start/stop/sleep/wake and `session:error` events.
- That path can surface user-visible `write EPIPE` as a failed start/session
  lifecycle toast even when chat/gateway streaming is already guarded.

Fix:

- Added `isExpectedSessionLifecycleDisconnectError` and
  `formatSessionLifecycleError` in `panel/src/main/ipc/sessions.ts`.
- Normalized expected disconnects to:
  `Server connection lost. The model server may have stopped or restarted. Try restarting the session.`
- Applied normalization to session create/start/stop/soft sleep/deep sleep/wake
  return errors and forwarded `session:error` payloads.

Durable gates:

- API surface now includes `panel_session_lifecycle_epipe_guard=true`.
- Installed/staged app parity now includes
  `installed_panel_session_lifecycle_epipe_guard=true`.
- Current suite source hashes now include `panel/src/main/ipc/sessions.ts`.

Packaging/app proof:

- Rebuilt Electron panel with `npx electron-vite build`.
- Packaged staged app with `npx electron-builder --dir --mac`.
- Replaced `/Applications/vMLX.app` from the staged app.
- Verified strict Developer ID + hardened runtime codesign for staged and
  installed app.
- Extracted installed `app.asar` and verified the session lifecycle normalizer
  is present.

Fresh artifacts:

- `build/current-api-surface-contract-20260602-cache-detail-zero-cached.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `panel_session_lifecycle_epipe_guard=true`
- `build/current-installed-app-runtime-parity-audit-20260602-developer-id-installed-signing.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `installed_panel_session_lifecycle_epipe_guard=true`
- `build/current-staged-app-runtime-parity-audit-20260602-developer-id-staged-signing.json`
  -> `status=pass`, `installed_panel_session_lifecycle_epipe_guard=true`

## [2026-06-02 14:34 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Post-release release-surface gate hardening. No max2, no Swift, no deprecated
`/Users/eric/vmlx`.

1.5.53 is already live, signed/notarized, and updater-visible, but the release
surface gate had one fragility from the release itself: it compared the GitHub
tag ref SHA directly to source HEAD. That only works for lightweight tags. It
would mark a valid annotated release tag red even when the tag object points to
the correct commit.

Change made:

- `tests/cross_matrix/run_release_surface_contract.py` now dereferences
  annotated `refs/tags/vX.Y.Z` tag objects through the GitHub `git/tags/<sha>`
  API and compares the resolved commit SHA to current source HEAD.
- `tests/test_release_surface_contract.py` adds a red/green test for annotated
  release tags while preserving the stale-tag failure case.

Verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_release_surface_contract.py
# 14 passed

.venv/bin/python -m py_compile \
  tests/cross_matrix/run_release_surface_contract.py \
  tests/test_release_surface_contract.py
# clean

git diff --check
# clean

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_release_surface_contract.py \
  --live-public \
  --out build/current-release-surface-contract-20260602-v153-live-public-annotated-tag-safe.json
# status=fail only because public_pypi_has_release_files=false
# GitHub release, source tag, raw updater, site updater, and cache headers pass
```

Current boundary remains unchanged:

- Public signed/notarized 1.5.53 DMGs and updater are live.
- PyPI publication remains externally blocked by trusted-publisher/API-token
  configuration.
- DSV4 exact-code/real-UI remains deferred, not cleared.

## [2026-06-02 14:45 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Decode-speed/model-family gate now fails on live weight-kernel contradictions.
No max2, no Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Eric re-raised MX matmul/TurboQuant/Hadamard/attention-architecture speed
concerns. I did not add a fake Hadamard check because no current runtime
metadata exposes that as a separately provable signal. I did add an honest
live-health gate around the metadata vMLX already reports:

- JANGTQ rows must report:
  - `quantization.codec=turboquant_codebook`
  - `weight_matmul_dispatch.primary=jang_tools_turboquant_custom_kernels`
  - `uses_mlx_quantized_matmul=false`
  - `acceleration.kernel_type` beginning with `turboquant_codebook`
- JANG/MXFP/4bit rows must report:
  - `quantization.codec=affine_quantized_matmul`
  - `weight_matmul_dispatch.primary=mlx_affine_quantized_matmul`
  - `uses_mlx_quantized_matmul=true`
  - non-TurboQuant acceleration kernel

The check is wired into `run_decode_speed_gate.py` so a live row fails if
`/health` contradicts the expected weight-kernel family. It is also required by
the no-heavy model-family contract marker list under
`decode_speed_jang_only_mx_matmul_policy`.

Verification:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_model_family_detection_contract.py
# 35 passed

.venv/bin/python tests/cross_matrix/run_model_family_detection_contract.py \
  --out build/current-model-family-detection-contract-20260602-step-jangtq-boundary.json
# status=pass; engine 56 passed, panel family 48 passed, session wiring 6 passed

.venv/bin/python -m py_compile \
  tests/cross_matrix/run_decode_speed_gate.py \
  tests/cross_matrix/run_model_family_detection_contract.py \
  tests/test_model_family_detection_contract.py
# clean

git diff --check
# clean
```

Boundary:

- This is no-heavy and live-health enforcement; it does not replace real live
  decode/UI rows.
- It makes future live decode rows fail if JANGTQ falls onto affine MLX
  matmul, or JANG/MXFP/4bit falls onto TurboQuant codebook kernels.

## [2026-06-02 15:23 PDT] from: codex -> @claude, @all-agents

Step/Flash greedy generation defaults display/apply path fixed in source.
No max2, no Swift, no deprecated `/Users/eric/vmlx`.

Root cause:

- Step-3.7-Flash-JANG_2L local metadata declares greedy chat sampling in
  `jang_config.json`:
  `temperature=0.0`, `top_p=1.0`, `top_k=0`, parser `step3p5`,
  reasoning parser `qwen3`.
- The panel stored numeric startup display fields, but had no separate marker
  that sampling defaults were declared by the model. Greedy values such as
  `temperature=0` and `top_k=0` are valid model-owned values, not "missing".
- The startup summary now keys off `defaultSamplingDefaultsDeclared === true`
  as well as nonzero values, so explicit greedy defaults show as model-owned
  instead of disappearing.

Changes:

- Added `defaultSamplingDefaultsDeclared` through `ServerConfig`,
  `SessionConfig`, create-session, session-settings refresh, and session
  adoption/startup defaults.
- `readBundleStartupDefaults()` now records whether generation_config or
  jang_config actually declared sampling keys, including JANG mode-specific
  repetition penalties.
- No `--default-*` sampling flags are emitted; engine/model metadata remains
  authoritative.
- Promoted the new canonical generation-defaults proof:
  `build/current-generation-defaults-contract-20260602-step-greedy-display.json`
- Promoted current suite/manifest artifacts:
  - `build/current-regression-suite-20260602-step-greedy-display-refresh.json`
  - `build/current-release-regression-manifest-20260602-step-greedy-display-refresh.json`
  - `build/current-objective-proof-audit-20260602-step-greedy-display-refresh.json`

## [2026-06-05] from: codex -> @all-agents

CM-001 Step3p7 advertised-VLM guard source fix completed in the active Python worktree.

Root cause: `is_mllm_model()` treated JANG `jang_config.architecture.has_vision=true` as authoritative and returned `MLLM=True` before checking whether Step3p7 VLM was production-cleared. CRACK-style Step3p7 metadata therefore routed into the unsafe MLLM path by default.

Fix: added Step3p7 advertised-media guard in `vmlx_engine/api/utils.py` before `force_mllm` and cached detection. Step3p7 bundles with `vision_config` or JANG `has_vision=true` now route text-only by default; explicit `has_vision=false` remains text-only; `force_mllm=True` does not bypass the guard accidentally.

Focused proof:

```sh
.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_step3p7_mllm_detection_guard.py
# 3 passed

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_step3p7_mllm_detection_guard.py tests/test_model_family_detection_contract.py tests/test_engine_audit.py -k 'step3p7 or gemma4_unified_text_runtime or affine_qwen_hybrid or mllm_registers_step3p7_source_runtime'
# 6 passed, 531 deselected

.venv/bin/python -m pytest -q tests/test_step37_vlm_runtime_audit.py tests/test_step3p7_mllm_detection_guard.py
# 10 passed
```

Still pending for full CM-001 closure: live Step3p7 media request should produce controlled unsupported-media behavior and a later text request should recover. No live model/server probe was run in this note.

---

CM-001 live Step3p7 proof completed and passed against the actual local artifact `/Users/eric/models/dealignai/Step-3.7-Flash-JANG_2L-CRACK` from the active Python worktree.

Classification:
- Model-side hazard: the artifact advertises Step3p7 vision (`vision_config` plus JANG `architecture.has_vision=true`) although vMLX Step3p7 VLM is not production-cleared. Model upload should either ship a vMLX text-only metadata view/override or document that vMLX must serve it text-only until VLM lands.

- Server log confirms: `tier=step3p7_advertised_vlm_text_only result=False`, `SimpleEngine loaded ... (MLLM=False)`.
